    44→## 4. Transform script plan

    47→
    48→**Key design decisions:**
    49→- Input: `items-all-goethe-faust.json` (JSONL, not NT) — already per-object, streams with constant memory

     1→# Plan: EDM JSONL → mocho RDF Transformation (POC)
     2→
     3→**Date**: 2026-04-14
     4→**Status**: Approved
     5→**Related**: `mocho/notes/alignment-ddbedm-mocho-adr.md`, `mocho/notes/alignment-ddbedm-mocho-spec.md`, `mocho/notes/alignment-ddbedm-mocho.md`
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## 1. Context
    10→
    11→The alignment table `alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv` was built by walking `edm.RDF.*` JSON fields in the JSONL corpus, then resolving `json_key → edm_iri → rda_iri` via the DCT→RDA map. The table is keyed by `(entity_type, json_key)` — matching the JSON structure of the JSONL, not the NT file.
    12→
    13→The JSONL (`items-all-goethe-faust.json`) is the right input: it is already per-object, streams with constant memory, and requires only stdlib JSON parsing. No NT indexing phase needed.
    14→
    15→The NT file is not used — it was generated from this same JSONL and carries no additional information for this alignment pass.
    16→
    17→---
    18→
    19→## 2. Critical files
    20→
    21→| File | Role |
    22→|---|---|
    23→| `goethe-faust/data/items-all-goethe-faust.json` | Source JSONL (115,432 records, one per line) |
    24→| `goethe-faust/data/ids-all-goethe-faust.txt` | 115,437 object IDs (32-char each); filter set |
    25→| `goethe-faust/output/alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv` | Alignment table — keyed by `(entity_type, json_key)`; columns: `entity_type, json_key, edm_prefix, edm_iri, record_count, coverage_pct, rda_iri, rda_label, wemi_level, match_method, in_mocho` |
    26→| `goethe-faust/output/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv` | htype code → DoCO/RiC-O class lookup |
    27→| `goethe-faust/scripts/README.md` | Must be updated after script is added |
    28→
    29→**Reference notes:**
    30→- `mocho/notes/alignment-ddbedm-mocho.md` — working note; entity type inventory, fanout figures, known issues
    31→- `mocho/notes/alignment-ddbedm-mocho-spec.md` — scope definition; in/out of scope per entity type
    32→- `mocho/notes/alignment-ddbedm-mocho-adr.md` — decisions; especially D2 (DC→RDA route), D4 (EDM-structural out of scope)
    33→
    34→---
    35→
    36→## 3. Script: `goethe-faust/scripts/transform_edm_to_mocho.py`
    37→
    38→### 3.1 Header
    39→
    40→```
    41→Purpose:    Transform DDB-EDM JSONL records to mocho-aligned RDF triples (N-Triples)
    42→Usage:      python transform_edm_to_mocho.py [--jsonl FILE] [--ids FILE] [--out FILE] [--limit N]
    43→Inputs:     items-all-goethe-faust.json, ids-all-goethe-faust.txt,
    44→            alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv, lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv
    45→Outputs:    mocho-goethe-faust.nt, transform_stats.json
    46→Deps:       stdlib only (json, csv, re, collections, argparse, pathlib)
    47→Assumes:    JSONL: one JSON object per line; edm.RDF.* structure per alignment-ddbedm-mocho.md
    48→```
    49→
    50→### 3.2 Constants
    51→
    52→```python
    53→CHO_BASE    = "http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de/item/"
    54→RDF_TYPE    = "http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type"
    55→EDM_CHO     = "http://www.europeana.eu/schemas/edm/ProvidedCHO"
    56→DDB_HTYPE   = "ddb:hierarchyType"          # json_key in alignment table
    57→MOCHO_NS    = "https://ise-fizkarlsruhe.github.io/ddbkg/mocho#"
    58→RICO_RST    = "http://www.ica.org/standards/RiC/ontology#hasRecordSetType"
    59→```
    60→
    61→### 3.3 Phase 1 — Load lookup tables
    62→
    63→```python
    64→def load_alignment(path) -> dict[tuple[str, str], list[dict]]:
    65→    # Key: (entity_type, json_key)
    66→    # Value: list of rows where rda_iri non-empty and in_mocho == 'True'
    67→    # Each row: {rda_iri, rda_label, wemi_level, match_method}
    68→    # Rows with in_mocho=False or rda_iri empty → excluded
    69→
    70→def load_htype_map(path) -> dict[str, tuple[str, str | None]]:
    71→    # Key: label_en (lowercased) from htype CSV
    72→    # Value: (rdf_type_iri, has_record_set_type_iri or None)
    73→    # Expand prefix to full IRI (doco:, rico:, ric-rst:, mocho:)
    74→```
    75→
    76→### 3.4 Phase 2 — Load IDs filter
    77→
    78→```python
    79→def load_ids(path) -> set[str]:
    80→    # 32-char IDs, one per line; strip whitespace
    81→```
    82→
    83→### 3.5 Phase 3 — Stream JSONL and transform
    84→
    85→#### 3.5.1 Record structure (per `alignment-ddbedm-mocho.md`)
    86→
    87→```
    88→record.edm.RDF = {
    89→  "ProvidedCHO": {dict},          # single entity
    90→  "Aggregation": {dict},          # single entity
    91→  "Agent":       [{dict}, ...],   # array
    92→  "Concept":     [{dict}, ...],   # array
    93→  "WebResource": [{dict}, ...],   # array
    94→  "Event":       [{dict}, ...],   # may be absent
    95→  "Place":       [{dict}, ...],   # may be absent
    96→  "TimeSpan":    [{dict}, ...],   # may be absent
    97→  "PhysicalThing":[{dict}, ...],  # may be absent
    98→}
    99→each entity: {"about": "<subject_uri>", "<json_key>": <value>, ...}
   100→value types: null | str | list | {"resource": str|null, "lang": str|null, "$": str}
   101→```
   102→
   103→#### 3.5.2 Object ID resolution
   104→
   105→```python
   106→def get_object_id(record) -> str | None:
   107→    # Extract 32-char ID from ProvidedCHO.about URI
   108→    # about = "http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de/item/{id}"
   109→    # Return id portion; None if not matching pattern
   110→```
   111→
   112→#### 3.5.3 Field value → NT object
   113→
   114→```python
   115→def value_to_nt_obj(val) -> list[str]:
   116→    # Handles all value shapes:
   117→    #   null → []
   118→    #   str  → ['"str"']
   119→    #   list → flatten recursively
   120→    #   {"resource": IRI, ...}  → ["<IRI>"]  (if resource non-null)
   121→    #   {"lang": L, "$": text}  → ['"text"@L'] (if lang non-null, text non-empty)
   122→    #   {"lang": null, "$": text} → ['"text"']
   123→    # Skips nulls and empty strings
   124→    # Escapes backslash, double-quote in literals
   125→```
   126→
   127→#### 3.5.4 Core transform loop
   128→
   129→```python
   130→def transform_record(record, alignment, htype_map, ids_filter) -> list[str]:
   131→    # Returns list of NT lines for this record
   132→    obj_id = get_object_id(record)
   133→    if obj_id not in ids_filter:
   134→        return []
   135→
   136→    nt_lines = []
   137→    rdf = record["edm"]["RDF"]
   138→
   139→    for entity_type, entities in rdf.items():
   140→        if entities is None:
   141→            continue
   142→        if isinstance(entities, dict):
   143→            entities = [entities]
   144→        for entity in entities:
   145→            subject = entity.get("about")
   146→            if not subject:
   147→                continue
   148→            subject_nt = f"<{subject}>"
   149→
   150→            for json_key, raw_val in entity.items():
   151→                if json_key == "about" or raw_val is None:
   152→                    continue
   153→
   154→                candidates = alignment.get((entity_type, json_key), [])
   155→                for row in candidates:
   156→                    pred_nt = f"<{row['rda_iri']}>"
   157→                    for obj_nt in value_to_nt_obj(raw_val):
   158→                        nt_lines.append(f"{subject_nt} {pred_nt} {obj_nt} .")
   159→
   160→    # rdf:type for ProvidedCHO via ddb:hierarchyType
   161→    nt_lines.extend(retype_cho(rdf, htype_map))
   162→    return nt_lines
   163→
   164→def retype_cho(rdf, htype_map) -> list[str]:
   165→    # Find ProvidedCHO.hierarchyType value
   166→    # Look up in htype_map → (rdf_type_iri, record_set_type_iri)
   167→    # Emit: <cho_uri> <rdf:type> <rdf_type_iri> .
   168→    # If record_set_type: <cho_uri> <rico:hasRecordSetType> <record_set_type_iri> .
   169→    # Fallback: if no hierarchyType, emit <cho_uri> <rdf:type> <edm:ProvidedCHO> .
   170→```
   171→
   172→### 3.6 Phase 4 — Output
   173→
   174→```python
   175→def main():
   176→    alignment  = load_alignment(ALIGNMENT_CSV)
   177→    htype_map  = load_htype_map(HTYPE_CSV)
   178→    ids_filter = load_ids(args.ids)
   179→
   180→    stats = defaultdict(int)
   181→    unmatched_keys = Counter()
   182→
   183→    with open(args.jsonl) as in_fh, open(args.out, "w") as out_fh:
   184→        for lineno, line in enumerate(in_fh):
   185→            if args.limit and lineno >= args.limit:
   186→                break
   187→            record = json.loads(line)
   188→            nt_lines = transform_record(record, alignment, htype_map, ids_filter)
   189→            for line in nt_lines:
   190→                out_fh.write(line + "\n")
   191→            stats["records_processed"] += 1
   192→            stats["triples_out"] += len(nt_lines)
   193→
   194→    write_stats(STATS_OUT, stats, unmatched_keys)
   195→```
   196→
   197→### 3.7 Stats output (`transform_stats.json`)
   198→
   199→```json
   200→{
   201→  "records_processed": 115432,
   202→  "triples_out": ...,
   203→  "top_unmatched_keys": [["Aggregation.edmRights", 115432], ...],
   204→  "objects_missing_htype": ...,
   205→  "fanout_note": "dc:contributor → 360 RDA candidates; dc:creator → 232. All emitted."
   206→}
   207→```
   208→
   209→---
   210→
   211→## 4. Alignment scope (per ADR + spec)
   212→
   213→- **Mapped**: rows where `in_mocho=True` and `rda_iri` non-empty
   214→- **Skipped silently**: `edm:isShownAt`, `edm:aggregatedCHO`, `edm:provider`, etc. — EDM-structural, per ADR Decision 4; appear as `unmatched` in alignment table
   215→- **Skipped silently**: `skos:prefLabel/altLabel`, geo coordinates, EDM-native agent properties — unmatched, deferred to second pass
   216→- **Fan-out**: all RDA candidates emitted for `dc:contributor` (360) and `dc:creator` (232); counted in stats
   217→- **rdf:type**: ProvidedCHO retyped via htype lookup; all other entity types get no rdf:type triple in this pass
   218→
   219→---
   220→
   221→## 5. Outputs
   222→
   223→| File | Description |
   224→|---|---|
   225→| `goethe-faust/output/mocho-goethe-faust.nt` | mocho-aligned NT triples (all objects merged) |
   226→| `goethe-faust/output/transform_stats.json` | records processed, triples out, unmatched keys, fanout note |
   227→
   228→---
   229→
   230→## 6. Verification
   231→
   232→Select one object per sector × media type combination for spot-checking:
   233→- Sectors: `sec_01` (libraries), `sec_02` (archives), `sec_03` (museums/collections), `sec_04` (audiovisual)
   234→- Media types: `mediatype_002` (image), `mediatype_003` (text), `mediatype_007` (audio), `mediatype_009` (3D/object)
   235→
   236→Steps:
   237→1. Run `--limit N` (enough to hit at least one object from each target sector/mediatype); inspect NT output per object
   238→2. For each spot-check object: confirm `rdf:type` triple uses a DoCO or RiC-O IRI (not `edm:ProvidedCHO`) where `hierarchyType` is present
   239→3. Confirm at least one RDA property triple is emitted for `dc:title`, `dc:creator`, or `dc:description`
   240→4. `transform_stats.json`: `triples_out / records_processed` ≈ 20–80 (reasonable range); `objects_missing_htype` documented
   241→5. Grep output NT for `europeana.eu` predicates — should be zero (all EDM predicates replaced or skipped)
   242→6. Update `goethe-faust/scripts/README.md`
   243→
   244→---
   245→
   246→## 7. RDF serialization choice
   247→
   248→| Format | Process | Size | Read |
   249→|---|---|---|---|
   250→| **N-Triples** (`.nt`) | Best — one triple/line, no parser context, grep/awk/stream-friendly | Worst — full URIs repeated every line | Poor |
   251→| **Turtle** (`.ttl`) | Good — most parsers support it | Best among text formats — prefix compression | Best |
   252→| **JSON-LD** (`.jsonld`) | Moderate — needs JSON + context handling | Moderate | Moderate (if you know JSON) |
   253→| RDF/XML | Worst | Worst | Worst |
   254→
   255→**Decision**: N-Triples for pipeline intermediate files (transform output, QLever ingest, sort/dedup); Turtle for hand-authored files (ontology, SPARQL update templates). N-Quads (`.nq`) if named graph identity is required alongside the same processing advantages.
   256→
   257→---
   258→
   259→## 8. Known issues to flag in code comments (per `alignment-ddbedm-mocho.md`)
   260→
   261→- **Triple subject keys**: `dcSubject`, `dcTermsSubject`, `dcTermSubject` carry identical data in 60% of records — all three will emit triples in this pass; deduplication is a downstream concern
   262→- **Fan-out**: `dc:contributor` → 360 / `dc:creator` → 232 RDA candidates; not filtered in POC
   263→- **`Event.occuredAt`**: No IRI resolved (probable typo in DDB API) — will appear in unmatched stats
   264→



```

     1→"""
     2→gen_htype_doco_mapping.py
     3→
     4→Match htype label_en values against DoCO ontology class labels using four
     5→strategies and write a ranked mapping table.
     6→
     7→Matching strategies (applied in order; first hit wins per htype):
     8→  1. exact        — case-insensitive exact match
     9→  2. levenshtein  — RapidFuzz ratio >= 88 %
    10→  3. translated   — translate label_de (Helsinki-NLP/opus-mt-de-en), then
    11→                    exact match against DoCO labels
    12→  4. embedding    — cosine similarity of sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2
    13→                    embeddings; top candidate reported regardless of threshold
    14→
    15→Usage:
    16→    python scripts/gen_htype_doco_mapping.py
    17→    python scripts/gen_htype_doco_mapping.py \\
    18→        --htypes   data/htype.csv \\
    19→        --doco     data/schemas/doco.owl \\
    20→        --out-csv  ~/Documents/claude/mocho/output/mapping_htype_doco.csv \\
    21→        --out-json ~/Documents/claude/mocho/output/mapping_htype_doco.json \\
    22→        --hf-cache data/hf-cache
    23→
    24→Inputs:
    25→    data/htype.csv              htype_code, label_de, label_en
    26→    data/schemas/doco.owl       DoCO ontology (RDF/XML or Turtle)
    27→
    28→Outputs:
    29→    mapping_htype_doco.csv / .json
    30→
    31→Columns:
    32→    htype_code, label_de, label_en, match_method, confidence,
    33→    doco_class, doco_label, doco_uri, translated_en
    34→
    35→Dependencies:
    36→    rdflib, rapidfuzz, sentence-transformers, transformers (HF), deep-translator
    37→"""
    38→
    39→import argparse
    40→import csv
    41→import json
    42→import os
    43→import warnings
    44→from pathlib import Path
    45→
    46→# Suppress noisy warnings from torch / urllib3
    47→warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")
    48→os.environ.setdefault("TOKENIZERS_PARALLELISM", "false")
    49→os.environ["TRANSFORMERS_OFFLINE"] = "1"   # use only locally cached models
    50→os.environ["HF_DATASETS_OFFLINE"]  = "1"
    51→
    52→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    53→# Argument parsing
    54→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    55→
    56→WORKING_DIR  = Path.home() / "Documents/claude/goethe-faust"
    57→DEFAULT_OUT  = Path.home() / "Documents/claude/mocho/output"
    58→
    59→def parse_args():
    60→    p = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)

### User *(2026-04-14T18:31:24.360Z)*

**Tool result**
```
     1→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2→"""
     3→align_ddbedm_to_mocho.py
     4→========================
     5→Data-driven ontology alignment: maps DDB-EDM fields present in
     6→items-all-goethe-faust.json to their corresponding mocho/RDA properties.
     7→
     8→Pipeline:
     9→  1. Profile data  — load edm_field_profile.json (from profile_edm_fields.py)
    10→  2. Resolve IRIs  — map JSON camelCase/plain keys to full EDM/DC IRIs
    11→                     by parsing ddbedm_1.0.ttl with rdflib
    12→  3. Load DC→RDA   — read mapping_dct_to_rda.csv for DC/DCT→RDA sub-property
    13→                     pairs produced by the mocho dct_rda_map.py workflow
    14→  4. Load mocho    — parse mocho-full.owl to collect all RDA properties
    15→                     imported into mocho (IRIs + English labels + WEMI level)
    16→  5. Align         — for each data field: DC/EDM IRI → RDA candidates →
    17→                     restrict to mocho-known properties
    18→                     Key explicit dimensions: ProvidedCHO.dcType → rdae:P20001
    19→  5b. Align vocab  — controlled-vocabulary Concepts:
    20→                     Concept.about from ddb.vocnet.org/sparte/ → sector
    21→                     Concept.about from ddb.vocnet.org/medientyp/ → rdae:P20001
    22→  6. Write outputs
    23→
    24→Inputs:
    25→  output/edm_field_profile.json          — field profile (run profile_edm_fields.py first)
    26→  ~/Documents/claude/mocho/ontology/ddbedm_1.0.ttl      — DDB-EDM ontology
    27→  ~/Documents/claude/mocho/mocho-odk/src/ontology/mocho-full.owl  — mocho
    28→  ~/Documents/claude/mocho/output/mapping_dct_to_rda.csv  — DC→RDA mapping
    29→
    30→Outputs:
    31→  output/alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv   — one row per (edm_field × rda_property)
    32→  output/alignment_ddbedm_mocho.json  — summary + full alignment
    33→
    34→Columns (CSV):
    35→  entity_type, json_key, edm_prefix, edm_iri, record_count, coverage_pct,
    36→  rda_iri, rda_label, wemi_level, match_method, in_mocho, vocab_label
    37→  (vocab_label is non-empty only for concept_vocab_sector / concept_vocab_mediatype rows)
    38→
    39→Usage:
    40→  python3 scripts/profile_edm_fields.py   # run first if profile is stale
    41→  python3 scripts/align_ddbedm_to_mocho.py
    42→
    43→Dependencies: rdflib
    44→Assumptions:
    45→  - ddbedm_1.0.ttl declares all properties used in the DDB-EDM profile
    46→  - mapping_dct_to_rda.csv schema: dct_term, rda_iri, rda_label
    47→  - mocho-full.owl is RDF/XML and includes all imported RDA properties inline
    48→"""
    49→
    50→import csv
    51→import json
    52→from collections import defaultdict
    53→from pathlib import Path
    54→
    55→from rdflib import Graph, RDF, RDFS, OWL, Namespace
    56→from rdflib.namespace import DC, DCTERMS, SKOS
    57→
    58→PROJECT  = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
    59→MOCHO    = Path.home() / "Documents/claude/mocho"
    60→
    61→PROFILE_PATH  = PROJECT / "output" / "edm_field_profile.json"
    62→DDBEDM_TTL    = MOCHO / "ontology" / "ddbedm_1.0.ttl"
    63→MOCHO_OWL     = MOCHO / "mocho-odk" / "src" / "ontology" / "mocho-full.owl"
    64→DCT_RDA_CSV   = MOCHO / "output" / "mapping_dct_to_rda.csv"
    65→OUT_CSV       = PROJECT / "output" / "alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv"
    66→OUT_JSON      = PROJECT / "output" / "alignment_ddbedm_mocho.json"
    67→
    68→# ── namespace constants ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    69→
    70→EDM  = Namespace("http://www.europeana.eu/schemas/edm/")
    71→ORE  = Namespace("http://www.openarchives.org/ore/terms/")
    72→GEO  = Namespace("http://www.w3.org/2003/01/geo/wgs84_pos#")
    73→FOAF = Namespace("http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/")
    74→RDA_ELEMENTS = "http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/"
    75→
    76→WEMI_LEVELS = {
    77→    f"{RDA_ELEMENTS}w/": "Work",
    78→    f"{RDA_ELEMENTS}e/": "Expression",
    79→    f"{RDA_ELEMENTS}m/": "Manifestation",
    80→    f"{RDA_ELEMENTS}i/": "Item",



```

### User *(2026-04-14T18:31:41.007Z)*

**Tool result**
```
     1→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2→"""
     3→match_objecttypes.py
     4→====================
     5→Maps DDB (Deutsche Digitale Bibliothek) document objecttype values to classes
     6→from two bibliographic ontologies: FaBiO and DoCO.
     7→
     8→Input
     9→-----
    10→- ddb-search-goethe-all.json : Solr search response (query="goethe", rows=1000)
    11→  Only documents with sector_fct == "sec_02" (library/bibliographic sector) are
    12→  processed.
    13→- fabio.owl : FRBR-aligned Bibliographic Ontology (FaBiO, http://purl.org/spar/fabio/)
    14→  Direct subclasses of fabio:Work, fabio:Expression, fabio:Manifestation, fabio:Item
    15→  are used as matching targets.
    16→- doco.owl : Document Components Ontology (DoCO, http://purl.org/spar/doco/)
    17→  All named classes are used as matching targets.
    18→
    19→Output
    20→------
    21→ddb-type2fabio.json with three top-level keys:
    22→  summary       — aggregate statistics and list of unmatched types
    23→  type_to_fabio — per-objecttype matching result (ontology, class, method, confidence)
    24→  details       — per-objecttype list of doc IDs that carry that type
    25→
    26→Matching pipeline (4 tiers, applied in order; first match wins)
    27→---------------------------------------------------------------
    28→1. strict
    29→   Case-insensitive exact match of the original objecttype string against all
    30→   ontology class names, CamelCase-split variants, and rdfs:label values.
    31→   Confidence: 1.0
    32→
    33→2. strict_translated
    34→   The objecttype is translated from German to English via Google Translate
    35→   (deep-translator, free, no API key). The translated string is then matched
    36→   exactly as in tier 1.
    37→   Confidence: 1.0
    38→
    39→3. levenshtein
    40→   Levenshtein edit distance ≤ 2 between the normalised translated string and
    41→   all lookup keys. The closest match is taken; ties broken by first occurrence.
    42→   Confidence: 1 - (distance / max_length)
    43→
    44→4. embeddings
    45→   Sentence embeddings (all-MiniLM-L6-v2 via sentence-transformers) are computed
    46→   for the translated objecttype and for each ontology class (name + labels).
    47→   The best cosine similarity above EMBEDDING_THRESHOLD (0.55) is taken.
    48→   Confidence: cosine similarity score
    49→
    50→When fabio and doco define a class with the same name (e.g., Index, Chapter,
    51→Table), the fabio class takes priority in the lookup; the doco variant is
    52→accessible under a "doco_<Name>" key.
    53→
    54→Dependencies
    55→------------
    56→    pip install deep-translator rapidfuzz sentence-transformers scikit-learn numpy
    57→
    58→Usage
    59→-----
    60→    python match_objecttypes.py



```

     2→
     3→## 1. Session scope
     4→
     5→Two projects active: `mocho/` (middle ontology) and `goethe-faust/` (data pipeline POC). Work this session:
     6→
     7→1. Built `htype → DoCO/RiC-O` mapping
     8→2. Planned the EDM JSONL → mocho RDF transform script

    11→

    17→| `goethe-faust/output/lookup_htype_doco_rico.json` | Same content as JSON array |

    19→
    20→---
    21→
    22→## 3. htype mapping decisions
    23→
    24→- 18 codes → DoCO (library structural types: Section, Chapter, Appendix, Part, Figure, Index, TableOfContents, TextChunk, Preface, Stanza)
    25→- 14 codes → RiC-O (archival types: RecordSet + mocho:/ric-rst: named individuals; Record; RecordPart)
    26→- 12 codes → `pending` (publication types — Monograph, Serial, Article — map to WEMI, not DoCO structural classes)
    27→- All required DoCO classes already in `mocho-odk/imports/doco_terms.txt` — no additions needed
    28→- mocho namespace for named individuals: `https://ise-fizkarlsruhe.github.io/ddbkg/mocho#` (replaces old `ddbo:`)
    29→
    30→Key individual mappings (archival, from `miro-htype-rico.pdf`):
    31→
    32→| htype | label | rdf_type | has_record_set_type |
    33→|---|---|---|---|
    34→| htype_048 | Tektonik | rico:RecordSet | mocho:Tektonik |
    35→| htype_030 | Bestand | rico:RecordSet | mocho:Bestand, ric-rst:Fonds |
    36→| htype_031 | Gliederung | rico:RecordSet | mocho:Gliederung, ric-rst:Series |
    37→| htype_036 | Bestandsserie | rico:RecordSet | mocho:Bestandsserie, ric-rst:Collection |
    38→| htype_037 | Bestandsklassifikation | rico:RecordSet | mocho:Bestandsklassifikation |
    39→| htype_034 | Archivale | rico:Record | — |
    40→| htype_035 | Teil | rico:RecordPart | — |
    41→
    42→---
    43→
    44→## 4. Transform script plan
    45→
    46→**Script to write**: `goethe-faust/scripts/transform_edm_to_mocho.py`
    47→
    48→**Key design decisions:**
    49→- Input: `items-all-goethe-faust.json` (JSONL, not NT) — already per-object, streams with constant memory
    50→- NT file (`ddbedm-goethe-faust.nt`) not used — generated from JSONL, adds no new information
    51→- Alignment lookup key: `(entity_type, json_key)` → matches `alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv` schema
    52→- Filter: only rows where `in_mocho=True` and `rda_iri` non-empty
    53→- Fan-out accepted: all RDA candidates emitted (dc:contributor → 360, dc:creator → 232); not filtered in POC
    54→- ProvidedCHO retyped via `lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv`; all other entity types pass through unchanged
    55→- EDM-structural predicates skipped silently (per ADR Decision 4)
    56→- stdlib only — no rdflib dependency
    57→
    58→**Full plan**: `goethe-faust/notes/alignment-plan.md`
    59→
    60→---

    87→- **Fan-out**: dc:contributor/creator high-fanout not filtered in POC; flag in stats output

  "new_string": "## 6. Verification\n\nSelect one object per sector × mediatype combination for spot-checking. Small sectors (sparte003 = 111, sparte007 = 85) and small mediatypes (mt001 = 476, mt005 = 96) may lack certain intersections — document missing combinations in `transform_stats.json`.\n\nSteps:\n1.    268→
   269→## 7. RDF serialization choice
   270→
   271→| Format | Process | Size | Read |
   272→|---|---|---|---|
   273→| **N-Triples** (`.nt`) | Best — one triple/line, no parser context, grep/awk/stream-friendly | Worst — full URIs repeated every line | Poor |
   274→| **Turtle** (`.ttl`) | Good — most parsers support it | Best among text formats — prefix compression | Best |
   275→| **JSON-LD** (`.jsonld`) | Moderate — needs JSON + context handling | Moderate | Moderate (if you know JSON) |
   276→| RDF/XML | Worst | Worst | Worst |
   277→

### Assistant *(2026-04-14T18:57:42.231Z)*

Five known issues. Here's the current list with my read on each:

---

**1. Triple subject keys** — `dcSubject`, `dcTermsSubject`, `dcTermSubject` overlap in 60% of records

Three JSON keys, same underlying data, all three map through the alignment table and emit triples independently. Result: duplicate triples with different predicates pointing to the same objects. Currently deferred downstream. The question is whether this is acceptable for the POC or whether the script should deduplicate at emit time (e.g., collect all object values per subject before writing). Deduplication is cheap here; doing it later in the pipeline means loading a potentially large NT file.

**2. Fan-out** — `dc:contributor` → 360 RDA candidates, `dc:creator` → 232

Every value of these fields generates up to 360/232 triples. For a record with 5 contributors each with a string value, that's 1,800 triples from one field. This inflates the output dramatically and almost certainly isn't the intent — most of those RDA properties will be semantically wrong for most values. The POC accepts all of them, but this needs a decision: is the alignment table itself wrong (too many candidates), or does the POC just need a flag?

**3. `Event.occuredAt`** — DDB API typo, no IRI resolved

Cosmetic for the POC — shows up in unmatched stats, doesn't affect output correctness. Nothing to decide unless you want to add a hard-coded alias (`occuredAt` → `occurredAt`) in the transform.

**4. `in_mocho` as string** — implementation detail, already noted

No discussion needed; just a code correctness note.

**5. `PhysicalThing.hierarchyType`** — skipped this pass

55,771 records. The plan defers this to a second pass. Worth confirming: is that the right call, or should PhysicalThing be retyped the same way as ProvidedCHO in this script?

---

Issues 1 and 2 seem worth discussing before writing the script — both affect the shape of the output meaningfully. Which do you want to start with?

### Assistant *(2026-04-14T19:05:33.016Z)*

Here's the full picture from the alignment table:

| json_key | edm_iri | records | RDA candidates |
|---|---|---|---|
| `dcSubject` | `dc:subject` (DC Elements) | 69,588 | 42 |
| `dcTermsSubject` | `dc:subject` (DC Elements) | 69,588 | 42 |
| `dcTermSubject` | `dcterms:subject` | 69,588 | 42 |

Two things worth noting before the question of what to do:

1. `dcSubject` and `dcTermsSubject` map to the **same EDM IRI** — both resolve to `http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/subject`. Only `dcTermSubject` correctly resolves to `dcterms:subject`. This looks like a data alignment error in `alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv`: `dcTermsSubject` should map to the DCT IRI, not the DC Elements one.

2. Both `dc:subject` and `dcterms:subject` feed the same 42 RDA candidates via the DC→RDA map. So for a record where all three keys carry the same value, you'd emit 3 × 42 = 126 triples where 42 is the correct count — and likely all 126 are identical `(subject, predicate, object)` tuples.

Three options for the script:

**A. Deduplicate at emit time (per record)** — collect `(subject_nt, pred_nt, obj_nt)` into a set per record before writing. Free memory-wise (set is reset each record), removes duplicates at the source, no downstream complexity. Loses diagnostic visibility into which keys caused the duplication.

**B. Emit all, deduplicate downstream** — current plan. Useful if you want the raw triple count per key in stats, or if you want to diagnose the alignment error separately. Downstream dedup on 115k × 3 × 42 triples is a sort-uniq on the NT file — tractable but a concrete task.

**C. Fix the alignment error first** — correct `dcTermsSubject` → `dcterms:subject` IRI in the CSV, then decide between A or B. This is the right long-term fix regardless.

My read: C should happen regardless — the alignment error is real and will affect any future work on the CSV. For the POC, A is the right choice for the script; the per-record set is essentially free and the output is clean. B defers a known problem for no benefit in the POC context.

What's your call?

### User *(2026-04-14T19:14:51.046Z)*

   126→```
   127→
   128→#### 3.5.4 Core transform loop
   129→
   130→```python
   131→def transform_record(record, alignment, htype_map, ids_filter) -> list[str]:
   132→    # Returns list of NT lines for this record
   133→    obj_id = get_object_id(record)
   134→    if obj_id not in ids_filter:
   135→        return []
   136→
   137→    nt_lines = []
   138→    rdf = record["edm"]["RDF"]
   139→
   140→    for entity_type, entities in rdf.items():
   141→        if entities is None:
   142→            continue
   143→        if isinstance(entities, dict):
   144→            entities = [entities]
   145→        for entity in entities:
   146→            subject = entity.get("about")
   147→            if not subject:
   148→                continue
   149→            subject_nt = f"<{subject}>"
   150→
   151→            for json_key, raw_val in entity.items():
   152→                if json_key == "about" or raw_val is None:
   153→                    continue
   154→
   155→                candidates = alignment.get((entity_type, json_key), [])
   156→                for row in candidates:
   157→                    pred_nt = f"<{row['rda_iri']}>"
   158→                    for obj_nt in value_to_nt_obj(raw_val):
   159→                        nt_lines.append(f"{subject_nt} {pred_nt} {obj_nt} .")
   160→
   161→    # rdf:type for ProvidedCHO via ddb:hierarchyType
   162→    nt_lines.extend(retype_cho(rdf, htype_map))
   163→    return nt_lines
   164→
   165→def retype_cho(rdf, htype_map) -> list[str]:
   166→    # Find ProvidedCHO.hierarchyType value
   167→    # Look up in htype_map → (rdf_type_iri, record_set_type_iri)
   168→    # Emit: <cho_uri> <rdf:type> <rdf_type_iri> .
   169→    # If record_set_type: <cho_uri> <rico:hasRecordSetType> <record_set_type_iri> .
   170→    # Fallback: if no hierarchyType, emit <cho_uri> <rdf:type> <edm:ProvidedCHO> .
   171→```
   172→
   173→### 3.6 Phase 4 — Output
   174→
   175→```python

### Assistant *(2026-04-14T19:15:07.170Z)*

**Tool call:** `Edit`
```json
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/goethe-faust/notes/alignment-plan.md",
  "old_string": "            for json_key, raw_val in entity.items():\n                if json_key == \"about\" or raw_val is None:\n                    continue\n\n                candidates = alignment.get((entity_type, json_key), [])\n                for row in candidates:\n                    pred_nt = f\"<{row['rda_iri']}>\"\n                    for obj_nt in value_to_nt_obj(raw_val):\n                        nt_lines.append(f\"{subject_nt} {pred_nt} {obj_nt} .\")\n\n    # rdf:type for ProvidedCHO via ddb:hierarchyType\n    nt_lines.extend(retype_cho(rdf, htype_map))\n    return nt_lines",
  "new_string": "            # Subject fields: value-type dispatch + cross-key deduplication\n            # dcSubject / dcTermsSubject / dcTermSubject all carry subject data;\n            # dcTermsSubject was incorrectly mapped to dc:subject — corrected in CSV.\n            # After fix: dcSubject → dc:subject (literals); dcTermSubject/dcTermsSubject → dcterms:subject (IRIs).\n            # Dispatch: collect unique values across all three keys; route by value type.\n            if entity_type == \"ProvidedCHO\":\n                nt_lines.extend(emit_subject_triples(entity, subject_nt, alignment))\n\n            for json_key, raw_val in entity.items():\n                if json_key == \"about\" or raw_val is None:\n                    continue\n                if entity_type == \"ProvidedCHO\" and json_key in SUBJECT_KEYS:\n                    continue  # handled by emit_subject_triples\n\n                candidates = alignment.get((entity_type, json_key), [])\n                for row in candidates:\n                    pred_nt = f\"<{row['rda_iri']}>\"\n                    for obj_nt in value_to_nt_obj(raw_val):\n                        nt_lines.append(f\"{subject_nt} {pred_nt} {obj_nt} .\")\n\n    # rdf:type for ProvidedCHO via ddb:hierarchyType\n    nt_lines.extend(retype_cho(rdf, htype_map))\n    return nt_lines\n\nSUBJECT_KEYS = {\"dcSubject\", \"dcTermsSubject\", \"dcTermSubject\"}\n\ndef emit_subject_triples(entity, subject_nt, alignment) -> list[str]:\n    # Collect all values from the three subject keys; deduplicate by (value_shape, content).\n    # Dispatch each unique value:\n    #   literal (str or lang-tagged text) → candidates for (\"ProvidedCHO\", \"dcSubject\")\n    #   IRI (resource) → candidates for (\"ProvidedCHO\", \"dcTermSubject\")\n    # Rationale: dc:subject is for uncontrolled literals; dcterms:subject for IRI references\n    #            to controlled vocabulary terms (GND, LCSH, etc.).\n    # dcTermsSubject IRI corrected in alignment CSV (was dc:subject, now dcterms:subject).\n    lit_candidates = alignment.get((\"ProvidedCHO\", \"dcSubject\"), [])\n    iri_candidates = alignment.get((\"ProvidedCHO\", \"dcTermSubject\"), [])\n\n    seen_objs: set[tuple[str, str]] = set()  # (pred_nt, obj_nt) dedup\n    nt_lines = []\n    for key in SUBJECT_KEYS:\n        raw_val = entity.get(key)\n        if raw_val is None:\n            continue\n        for obj_nt in value_to_nt_obj(raw_val):\n            is_iri = obj_nt.startswith(\"<\")\n            candidates = iri_candidates if is_iri else lit_candidates\n            for row in candidates:\n                pred_nt = f\"<{row['rda_iri']}>\"\n                pair = (pred_nt, obj_nt)\n                if pair not in seen_objs:\n                    seen_objs.add(pair)\n                    nt_lines.append(f\"{subject_nt} {pred_nt} {obj_nt} .\")\n    return nt_lines"
}
```

### Assistant *(2026-04-14T19:15:28.305Z)*

Done. Summary of what changed:

**CSV fix** (`alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv`) — 42 rows: `dcTermsSubject` `edm_prefix` `dc→dcterms`, `edm_iri` `dc:subject→dcterms:subject`.

**Plan** — `transform_record()` now:
- Skips the three subject keys in the main loop (`SUBJECT_KEYS` constant)
- Calls `emit_subject_triples()` once per ProvidedCHO entity

**`emit_subject_triples()`** — collects values from all three keys, dispatches by value type (IRI detection: `obj_nt.startswith("<")`), deduplicates `(pred, obj)` pairs in a set. Net effect: each subject value is emitted exactly once through the semantically correct predicate path.

One thing to note: `dcTermsSubject` and `dcTermSubject` now map to the same 42 RDA candidates — so for a record carrying identical IRI values in both keys, the `seen_objs` set absorbs the duplicate without any special handling needed.

### Assistant *(2026-04-14T19:17:50.617Z)*

**Issue 2 — Fan-out**: `dc:contributor` → 360 RDA candidates, `dc:creator` → 232.

Every value of these fields gets emitted against every candidate predicate. For a record with, say, 3 contributors and 3 creators, that's 3×360 + 3×232 = 1,776 triples from just those two fields alone. Most of those predicates will be semantically wrong — the fan-out exists because the DC→RDA map expands `dc:contributor`/`dc:creator` to all RDA relationship properties that have any contributor/creator-like semantics.

Before deciding what to do, it's worth looking at what the 360 and 232 candidates actually are — whether they're all genuinely plausible or whether there's an obvious way to cut them down.

### Assistant *(2026-04-14T19:23:48.435Z)*

The numbers in the plan were wrong — let me give the actual breakdown:

| json_key | total RDA candidates | WEMI |
|---|---|---|
| `contributor` | 360 | Expression 293, Manifestation 50, Item 16, Work 1 |
| `creator` | 464 (not 232) | Work 464 |

All 824 candidates are `in_mocho=True`. The sample rows tell the story: `creator` fans out to things like "has appellee corporate body", "has production company", "has plaintiff corporate body" — all Work-level but highly specific relationship types. `contributor` fans out to "has conductor agent", "has dancer agent", "has onscreen presenter agent" — Expression-level, plausible for AV material, wrong for an archival letter.

The root cause: the DC→RDA map expands `dc:creator`/`dc:contributor` to every RDA property that has any semantic overlap. That's the right approach for a full alignment, but it means the POC output for a Goethe letter will assert that it "has dancer agent" and "has plaintiff corporate body".

Three realistic options:

**A. Keep all** — current plan. Semantically very noisy. Output is usable only after downstream filtering; also 464 + 360 = 824 triples per record for creator/contributor values alone.

**B. Whitelist generic properties** — pick 1–2 unambiguous RDA properties per field (e.g., `rdaw:P10065 has creator agent` for creator, `rdae:P20052 has contributor agent` for contributor) and flag the rest as requiring agent-type disambiguation. Clean output, honest about what DC can tell us.

**C. Emit nothing for creator/contributor in this pass** — document as out of scope pending agent-type resolution (the GND enrichment in Phase 1b will attach typed agents via GND roles). No noise, but you lose the creator/contributor link entirely for now.

Option B seems right for a POC — you get one well-grounded triple per value, the output is usable, and the comment in the code is honest. Do you want to go with B, and if so: do you know the correct generic RDA IRIs for creator and contributor, or should I look them up in the alignment table?

### Assistant *(2026-04-14T19:34:58.415Z)*

Results from the alignment table:

**`creator`** — clean answer: `rdaw:P10065 has creator agent of work` (Work-level, genuinely generic). One property, covers all creator values.

**`contributor`** — no generic property exists in mocho. The 360 candidates are all specific:
- 293 Expression-level: performers, conductors, actors, dancers, etc.
- 50 Manifestation-level: "has contributor agent of text", "has contributor agent of music", etc.
- 1 Work-level: "has academic supervisor" (wrong)
- The closest-looking candidate (`P20052`) turns out to be mapped as "has recordist agent" in the DC→RDA table

The Manifestation-level properties are the most defensible for a general corpus — `rdam:P30328 has contributor agent of text` would cover most of the Bibliothek/Archiv objects, but it's wrong for images and audio.

### User *(2026-04-14T19:38:27.417Z)*

**Tool result**
```
     1→# ADR: DDB-EDM → mocho Ontology Alignment Strategy
     2→
     3→**Date**: 2026-03-23
     4→**Status**: Accepted
     5→
     6→---
     7→
     8→## Context
     9→
    10→mocho is a mid-level, WEMI-aligned mediation ontology for multi-domain, multi-modal
    11→cultural heritage objects. One of its alignment targets is the DDB-EDM profile
    12→(Europeana Data Model as extended by the Deutsche Digitale Bibliothek). To build the
    13→GeMeA ingest transformation, we need to decide how to produce and represent the
    14→mapping from EDM fields to mocho/domain-specific properties. Several approaches were considered.
    15→
    16→---
    17→
    18→## Decision 1: Profile actual data before aligning
    19→
    20→**Decision**: Run a field-frequency scan of the full corpus (115,432 DDB items)
    21→before doing any ontology mapping. Produce a profile (`edm_field_profile.csv/json`)
    22→that is the sole input to the alignment step.
    23→
    24→**Alternatives considered**:
    25→- *Schema-only*: Align the ddbedm_1.0.ttl ontology directly to mocho-full.owl
    26→  without looking at data. Rejected because ddbedm declares properties that may not
    27→  appear in the corpus, and the corpus contains informal fields (e.g. the triple
    28→  subject-key issue, `Event.occuredAt` with no IRI) that are invisible from the
    29→  schema alone.
    30→- *Per-record transformation*: Parse the alignment inline during SPARQL/ETL.
    31→  Rejected because it conflates schema work with instance transformation and makes
    32→  the mapping invisible to review.
    33→
    34→**Rationale**: A data-first profile makes the alignment auditable and reproducible.
    35→It surfaces real coverage numbers (e.g. `alternative` at 5%, `identifier` at 88%)
    36→that a schema-only pass would not reveal, and catches corpus-specific anomalies
    37→before they propagate into the triplestore.
    38→
    39→---
    40→
    41→## Decision 2: Route DC/DCT properties via the existing DCT→RDA map
    42→
    43→**Decision**: For properties in the DC (`dc:`, `dcterms:`) namespaces, use
    44→`mapping_dct_to_rda.csv` — produced by the mocho `dct_rda_map.py` workflow — as the
    45→authoritative source of RDA sub-property candidates. Do not re-derive this mapping
    46→in the alignment script.
    47→
    48→**Alternatives considered**:
    49→- *Direct EDM→mocho query*: Inspect `mocho-full.owl` for `owl:equivalentProperty`
    50→  or `rdfs:subPropertyOf` axioms linking EDM terms to mocho. Rejected because
    51→  mocho does not yet declare such axioms; they would need to be authored first,
    52→  creating a circular dependency.
    53→- *Semantic similarity matching*: Use sentence embeddings (as in
    54→  `match_objecttypes.py`) to align EDM property labels to mocho property labels.
    55→  Rejected for schema alignment: embedding similarity is unreliable for fine-grained
    56→  property distinctions (e.g. `dc:creator` → `has composer agent` vs.
    57→  `has author of introduction`), and the DCT→RDA map already encodes the
    58→  authoritative RDA sub-property hierarchy.
    59→
    60→**Rationale**: The DCT→RDA map is derived from `mapElement2DCT.xml`, a normative
    61→artefact published with the RDA Vocabularies release. Reusing it is consistent with
    62→the existing mocho pipeline and avoids introducing a new alignment methodology for
    63→properties that already have a well-defined answer.
    64→
    65→**Consequence**: Properties outside the DC/DCT namespaces (EDM-structural, SKOS,
    66→geo, DDB extensions, CIDOC CRM) are not covered by this route and are explicitly
    67→marked `unmatched`. A second alignment pass using direct mocho queries will be
    68→needed for them.
    69→
    70→---
    71→
    72→## Decision 3: Output one row per (EDM field × RDA property), preserving all candidates
    73→
    74→**Decision**: The alignment table contains one row for every (EDM field, RDA
    75→property) pair, including all high-fanout candidates. `dc:creator` produces 232
    76→rows, `dc:contributor` produces 360 rows. No automatic candidate selection is done.
    77→
    78→**Alternatives considered**:
    79→- *Collapse to best match*: Pick the single most specific RDA property per field
    80→  (e.g. `rda:P10311 has creator agent` for `dc:creator`). Rejected because
    81→  "most specific" is context-dependent: the right property for a MARC 21 library
    82→  record differs from LIDO for a museum object; the alignment script does not have
    83→  that context.
    84→- *Restrict to Work-level RDA only*: Since `ProvidedCHO` is most naturally a WEMI
    85→  Work, filter to `rda:w/*` candidates only. Rejected because some fields
    86→  (e.g. `dc:format`, `dcterms:issued`) are more naturally Manifestation-level, and
    87→  forcing Work-level would produce wrong mappings.
    88→
    89→**Rationale**: The alignment table is an intermediate artefact, not a finished
    90→transformation. Keeping all candidates preserves information for downstream consumers
    91→(the GeMeA ingest script, the ISWC paper appendix) to make context-aware selections.
    92→The `wemi_level` column enables filtering without discarding data.
    93→
    94→---
    95→
    96→## Decision 4: Treat EDM-structural properties as out of scope for the DC→RDA route
    97→
    98→**Decision**: `edm:isShownAt`, `edm:isShownBy`, `edm:aggregatedCHO`,
    99→`edm:dataProvider`, `edm:provider`, `edm:hasView`, and `edm:object` are explicitly
   100→categorised as unmatched and flagged for a separate mapping pass. They are not
   101→treated as alignment failures.
   102→
   103→**Rationale**: These properties describe the provenance and access structure of the
   104→EDM Aggregation, not the intellectual content of the CHO. They have no DC→RDA
   105→mapping by design (they are not sub-properties of any DC term). The correct mocho
   106→representation for them may not be RDA at all — `edm:isShownAt` maps to a URL, not
   107→a bibliographic property — and that question should be settled in a dedicated
   108→EDM-structural alignment step.
   109→
   110→---
   111→
   112→## Decision 5: Implement IRI resolution via ontology parsing + hard-coded overrides
   113→
   114→**Decision**: Resolve JSON camelCase keys to full IRIs by:
   115→1. Parsing `ddbedm_1.0.ttl` with rdflib to build a local-name → IRI lookup.
   116→2. Applying heuristic camelCase prefix-stripping (`dcTerms*`, `dc*`, `edm*`, etc.).
   117→3. Applying a small `OVERRIDES` dict for known corpus quirks.
   118→
   119→**Overrides in `OVERRIDES`**:
   120→- `dcTermSubject` → `dcterms:subject` (DDB data variant — missing trailing `s`).
   121→- `identifier` (bare key, no prefix) → `dc:identifier`.
   122→
   123→**Alternatives considered**:
   124→- *Hardcode the full IRI map*: Maintain a complete static lookup table.
   125→  Rejected because it duplicates information already in `ddbedm_1.0.ttl` and
   126→  would need manual maintenance when the ontology changes.
   127→- *Rely solely on heuristic stripping*: No overrides, accept resolution errors.
   128→  Rejected because `dcTermSubject` would silently resolve to `dc:termSubject`
   129→  (non-existent), producing a wrong EDM IRI and an unmatched row for a field
   130→  present in 60% of records.
   131→
   132→**Rationale**: Parsing the ontology for the common case and overriding only known
   133→anomalies minimises maintenance burden while handling real corpus quirks correctly.
   134→The `OVERRIDES` dict is the canonical registry of DDB API deviations from the
   135→declared ontology.
   136→
   137→---
   138→
   139→## Consequences
   140→
   141→- The alignment covers 32 DC/DCT fields in mocho, producing 1,271 (field × RDA)
   142→  pairs as a starting vocabulary for the GeMeA ingest transformation.
   143→- 55 fields remain unmatched, split across five documented categories. None are
   144→  silent failures.
   145→- A second alignment pass is needed for: (a) EDM-native agent properties, (b) EDM-
   146→  structural Aggregation properties, (c) SKOS labels, (d) geo coordinates.
   147→- The `OVERRIDES` dict should be extended if further DDB API deviations are
   148→  discovered during ingest validation.
   149→- If mocho's `dct_rda_map.py` is re-run with a newer RDA Vocabularies release,
   150→  the alignment should be re-run to pick up any new sub-property declarations for
   151→  `dc:identifier` and `dcterms:spatial` (currently unmapped).
   152→



```

### Assistant *(2026-04-14T19:39:44.198Z)*

**Tool call:** `Write`
```json
{
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/goethe-faust/notes/alignment-adr.md",
  "content": "# ADR: EDM JSONL → mocho RDF Transform (goethe-faust POC)\n\n**Date**: 2026-04-14\n**Status**: Accepted\n**Related**: `mocho/notes/alignment-ddbedm-mocho-adr.md`, `goethe-faust/notes/alignment-plan.md`\n\n---\n\n## Context\n\n`transform_edm_to_mocho.py` converts the goethe-faust DDB-EDM JSONL corpus\n(115,432 records) to mocho-aligned N-Triples. The alignment table\n`alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv` is an intermediate produced by the mocho pipeline;\nthis document records decisions that govern how the transform script uses — and\nin some cases departs from — that table.\n\nUpstream decisions in `mocho/notes/alignment-ddbedm-mocho-adr.md` (D1–D5)\nare inherited without modification. Decisions here either extend them or\nresolve implementation-level issues discovered during corpus inspection.\n\n---\n\n## Decision 1: Use JSONL as input, not the NT file\n\n**Decision**: Stream `items-all-goethe-faust.json` (JSONL, one JSON object per\nline). The NT file `ddbedm-goethe-faust.nt` is not used.\n\n**Alternatives considered**:\n- *NT file*: Load triples from the pre-generated NT and align by predicate IRI.\n  Rejected because: (a) the NT was generated from the same JSONL and carries no\n  additional information; (b) the alignment table is keyed by `(entity_type,\n  json_key)` matching the JSON structure, not by IRI, so NT input would require\n  a reverse IRI→json_key lookup that is neither defined nor stable.\n\n**Rationale**: JSONL streams with constant memory, preserves the `(entity_type,\njson_key)` lookup key structure, and requires only stdlib `json`. No NT indexing\nphase needed.\n\n---\n\n## Decision 2: stdlib only — no rdflib in the transform script\n\n**Decision**: The transform script uses only Python stdlib (`json`, `csv`, `re`,\n`collections`, `argparse`, `pathlib`). rdflib is not imported.\n\n**Rationale**: The transform is a data pipeline step, not an ontology processing\nstep. N-Triples output can be constructed by string formatting. rdflib adds a\nnon-stdlib dependency and significant startup overhead for a script that runs\nover 115k records.\n\n**Scope**: This applies only to `transform_edm_to_mocho.py`. Upstream scripts\nthat parse OWL or Turtle (`align_ddbedm_to_mocho.py`, `gen_htype_doco_mapping.py`)\ncontinue to use rdflib.\n\n---\n\n## Decision 3: htype lookup keyed by `htype_code`, pending rows skipped\n\n**Decision**: `load_htype_map()` uses the `htype_code` column (e.g. `\"htype_030\"`)\nas the lookup key. Rows where `rdf_type == \"pending\"` are excluded.\n\n**Rationale**: Corpus inspection confirmed that `ddb:hierarchyType` is an\n`owl:DatatypeProperty` whose literal values are `htype_code` strings. Grepping\n`items-all-goethe-faust.json` for `\"htype_0\"` yields exactly 92,957 matches —\nequal to the `record_count` for `ProvidedCHO,hierarchyType` in the field\nprofile.\n\nThe original draft keyed by `label_en (lowercased)`; this was corrected when\nthe literal format was confirmed.\n\n**Pending rows** (12 publication types — Monograph, Serial, Article, etc.) have\nno DoCO structural class. They map to WEMI Work/Manifestation concepts not yet\nin mocho. Skipping them causes the fallback `edm:ProvidedCHO` rdf:type to fire;\nthey are counted in `objects_missing_htype` in stats output.\n\n---\n\n## Decision 4: PhysicalThing.hierarchyType skipped this pass\n\n**Decision**: `retype_cho()` handles `ProvidedCHO` only. `PhysicalThing`\nentities (55,771 records, 48.3% coverage) also carry `hierarchyType` but are\nnot retyped in this pass.\n\n**Rationale**: The scope of this POC is the CHO graph. PhysicalThing is an\nEDM modelling artefact for physical carrier description; its rdf:type mapping\nrequires a separate alignment decision (likely CIDOC-CRM or LRMoo). Skipping\nit does not affect the correctness of the ProvidedCHO output.\n\n**Consequence**: `PhysicalThing.hierarchyType` will appear in the unmatched\nkeys stats. This is expected and documented.\n\n---\n\n## Decision 5: RDF output format — N-Triples for pipeline, Turtle for hand-authored\n\n**Decision**: Transform output (`mocho-goethe-faust.nt`) and all pipeline\nintermediate files use N-Triples (`.nt`). Hand-authored files (ontology,\nSPARQL update templates) use Turtle (`.ttl`). N-Quads (`.nq`) if named graph\nidentity is required.\n\n**Rationale**: N-Triples are one triple per line — grep/awk/stream-friendly,\nno parser context, trivial to sort and dedup with Unix tools. The verbosity\n(full URIs repeated) is acceptable for machine-processed intermediates.\nTurtle prefix compression is valuable for human-readable hand-authored files.\n\n---\n\n## Decision 6: Triple subject keys — IRI correction + value-type dispatch\n\n**Decision**: Three JSON keys carry subject data: `dcSubject`, `dcTermsSubject`,\n`dcTermSubject`. These are handled by a dedicated `emit_subject_triples()`\nfunction, not the generic alignment loop.\n\n**Background**: Corpus inspection revealed that `dcTermsSubject` was\nincorrectly mapped to `dc:subject` (`http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/subject`)\nin `alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv`. The correct IRI is `dcterms:subject`\n(`http://purl.org/dc/terms/subject`). This was a derivation error in the\nalignment script's IRI resolution step; `dcTermSubject` (note: missing `s`) was\nthe one correctly resolved to `dcterms:subject` via an explicit `OVERRIDES` entry\nin `align_ddbedm_to_mocho.py`. The fix was applied directly to the CSV (42 rows:\n`edm_prefix` `dc→dcterms`, `edm_iri` corrected).\n\n**Dispatch logic**:\n- Literal value (string or lang-tagged text) → RDA candidates for\n  `(\"ProvidedCHO\", \"dcSubject\")` — i.e. the dc:subject path.\n- IRI value (`{\"resource\": ...}`) → RDA candidates for\n  `(\"ProvidedCHO\", \"dcTermSubject\")` — i.e. the dcterms:subject path.\n\n**Deduplication**: `emit_subject_triples()` collects values from all three keys\nand deduplicates `(pred_nt, obj_nt)` pairs in a per-record set before writing.\nThis prevents duplicate triples when the same value appears under multiple keys\n(occurs in ~60% of records).\n\n**Rationale**: `dc:subject` is conventionally used for uncontrolled literals;\n`dcterms:subject` for IRI references to controlled vocabulary terms (GND, LCSH,\netc.). Value-type dispatch applies this convention without requiring agent-type\nknowledge. The per-record set dedup is cheap (reset each record) and removes\nthe problem at the source.\n\n---\n\n## Decision 7: Creator fan-out — whitelist to single generic property\n\n**Decision**: `dc:creator` (json_key: `creator`) is mapped to a single RDA\nproperty: `rdaw:P10065 has creator agent of work`\n(`http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/w/P10065`). The alignment table's 464\nWork-level candidates are bypassed.\n\n**Background**: The alignment table produces 464 candidates for `creator`,\nall at the Work WEMI level — including highly specific properties such as\n\"has production company\", \"has plaintiff corporate body\", \"has appellee\ncorporate body\". These are correct sub-properties of `has creator agent of work`\nin the RDA hierarchy but are wrong for a generic `dc:creator` value where the\ncreator role is unknown.\n\n**Alternatives considered**:\n- *Emit all 464*: Semantically very noisy; a Goethe letter would assert\n  \"has plaintiff corporate body\" for the author. Rejected.\n- *Filter by WEMI level*: All 464 are already Work-level; filtering on WEMI\n  does not narrow the set. Rejected.\n- *Use mediatype dispatch*: Route to specific properties based on the record's\n  mediatype Concept IRI. Adds complexity; correct role remains unknown even\n  with mediatype. Rejected for POC.\n\n**Rationale**: `rdaw:P10065` is the most generic Work-level creator property\nin mocho. It is always correct for any dc:creator value where the role is\nunspecified. Specific role properties should be emitted only after GND agent\nenrichment (Phase 1b) resolves the creator's function.\n\n---\n\n## Decision 8: Contributor fan-out — keep dc:contributor as-is\n\n**Decision**: `dc:contributor` (json_key: `contributor`) is emitted using the\noriginal `dc:contributor` predicate\n(`http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/contributor`). No RDA property is used.\n\n**Background**: The alignment table produces 360 candidates for `contributor`,\nspread across Expression (293), Manifestation (50), Item (16), and Work (1)\nWEMI levels. The single Work-level candidate is \"has academic supervisor\" —\nsemantically wrong as a default. The Expression-level candidates are all specific\nperformer roles (conductor, actor, dancer, etc.). No generic \"has contributor\nagent\" superclass exists in mocho's current import.\n\n**Alternatives considered**:\n- *Whitelist generic RDA property*: No suitable candidate found. `P20052` in\n  the alignment table is incorrectly mapped as \"has recordist agent\" (a\n  derivation error in the DC→RDA map). Rejected.\n- *Emit all 360*: Semantically noisy; a 19th-century correspondence bundle\n  would assert \"has dancer agent\" for each addressee. Rejected.\n- *Skip contributor entirely*: Loses the contributor link for 26.2% of records\n  until Phase 1b. Rejected in favour of keeping the DC predicate.\n- *Mediatype dispatch*: Route to Manifestation-level properties by mediatype\n  (e.g. `mt003 Text → rdam:P30328 has contributor agent of text`). Adds\n  complexity and is still wrong for mixed-media records. Rejected for POC.\n\n**Rationale**: `dc:contributor` is a valid, well-understood predicate. Keeping\nit preserves the contributor link in the output graph without asserting a\nspecific role that cannot be determined from the DC value alone. The output is\nnot mocho-RDA aligned for this field, but it is correct and queryable. Phase 1b\nGND agent enrichment will replace this with typed RDA role triples.\n\n---\n\n## Consequences\n\n- `alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv` was patched: `dcTermsSubject` `edm_iri` corrected\n  from `dc:subject` to `dcterms:subject` (42 rows). If the alignment script is\n  re-run, this correction must be reapplied or the `OVERRIDES` dict in\n  `align_ddbedm_to_mocho.py` must be extended to handle `dcTermsSubject`\n  explicitly.\n- Two fields (`creator`, `contributor`) bypass the alignment table entirely.\n  Their alignment table rows remain but are unused by the transform script; they\n  are still valid for downstream consumers (ISWC paper appendix, second-pass\n  scripts).\n- `dc:contributor` in the output is not mocho-RDA aligned. A Phase 1b script\n  (`link_gnd_agents.py`) will emit typed contributor triples via GND role lookup.\n- The transform stats (`transform_stats.json`) will show `contributor` and\n  `creator` with zero unmatched-key entries since they are handled before the\n  alignment lookup. Stats should record them under a separate `whitelisted_keys`\n  counter.\n"
}
```

**D3: htype lookup keyed by `htype_code`, pending rows skipped**

Pending rows (12 publication types: Monograph, Serial, Article, etc.) have no DoCO structural class; they fall through to the `edm:ProvidedCHO` rdf:type fallback and are counted in `objects_missing_htype` in stats output.

### Assistant *(2026-04-14T19:44:31.025Z)*

16 rows (not 12 as the ADR says — that's a count error to fix). These are htype codes where no DoCO or RiC-O class was assigned during the mapping session. Two categories in the notes column:

- **No structural class in DoCO** — Annotation, Address, Entry, Dedication, Charter, Supplementary insert, Contained Work. DoCO models document components, not these genres.
- **Publication types** — Article, Monograph, Serial, Manuscript, Issue, Multivolume Work, Review, Newspaper, Music. These describe the bibliographic nature of the whole work, not its document structure. The notes say they "map to WEMI Work/Manifestation" — meaning the right target is a FaBiO or LRMoo class, not DoCO.

So `rdf_type == "pending"` in `lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv` means: *the correct target class is known to exist somewhere (WEMI, OA, Music Ontology) but hasn't been mapped yet*. The transform script skips them rather than emitting a wrong type, and falls back to `edm:ProvidedCHO`.

Also: the ADR says 12 pending rows — it should be 16. Should I correct that?

### Assistant *(2026-04-14T19:45:00.027Z)*

Important. This affects `retype_cho()` in two ways: `mocho:Manifestation` is always emitted for every ProvidedCHO, and the `edm:ProvidedCHO` fallback is removed entirely — pending/missing htype records get `mocho:Manifestation` alone. The DoCO/RiC-O type (where present) is an additional, more specific type alongside it.

Updating plan, ADR, and handover.

   323→---
   324→
   325→## 7. RDF serialization choice
   326→
   327→| Format | Process | Size | Read |
   328→|---|---|---|---|
   329→| **N-Triples** (`.nt`) | Best — one triple/line, no parser context, grep/awk/stream-friendly | Worst — full URIs repeated every line | Poor |
   330→| **Turtle** (`.ttl`) | Good — most parsers support it | Best among text formats — prefix compression | Best |
   331→| **JSON-LD** (`.jsonld`) | Moderate — needs JSON + context handling | Moderate | Moderate (if you know JSON) |
   332→| RDF/XML | Worst | Worst | Worst |
   333→
   334→**Decision**: N-Triples for pipeline intermediate files (transform output, QLever ingest, sort/dedup); Turtle for hand-authored files (ontology, SPARQL update templates). N-Quads (`.nq`) if named graph identity is required alongside the same processing advantages.
   335→



```

   130→and deduplicates `(pred_nt, obj_nt)` pairs in a per-record set before writing.
   131→This prevents duplicate triples when the same value appears under multiple keys
   132→(occurs in ~60% of records).
   133→
   134→**Rationale**: `dc:subject` is conventionally used for uncontrolled literals;
   135→`dcterms:subject` for IRI references to controlled vocabulary terms (GND, LCSH,
   136→etc.). Value-type dispatch applies this convention without requiring agent-type
   137→knowledge. The per-record set dedup is cheap (reset each record) and removes
   138→the problem at the source.
   139→
   140→---

Rows where `rdf_type == \"pending\"` are excluded.\n\n**Rationale**: Corpus inspection confirmed that `ddb:hierarchyType` is an\n`owl:DatatypeProperty` whose literal values are `htype_code` strings. Grepping\n`items-all-goethe-faust.json` for `\"htype_0\"` yields exactly 92,957 matches —\nequal to the `record_count` for `ProvidedCHO,hierarchyType` in the field\nprofile.\n\nThe original draft keyed by `label_en (lowercased)`; this was corrected when\nthe literal format was confirmed.\n\n**Pending rows** (16 codes — Monograph, Serial, Article, Annotation, Charter,\nDedication, etc.) have no DoCO or RiC-O class assigned. They fall through the\nhtype lookup without emitting an additional rdf:type. ### Assistant *(2026-04-14T19:46:16.307Z)*

**Tool call:** `Edit`
```json
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/goethe-faust/notes/alignment-adr.md",
  "old_string": "- The transform stats (`transform_stats.json`) will show `contributor` and\n  `creator` with zero unmatched-key entries since they are handled before the\n  alignment lookup. Stats should record them under a separate `whitelisted_keys`\n  counter.",
  "new_string": "- The transform stats (`transform_stats.json`) will show `contributor` and\n  `creator` with zero unmatched-key entries since they are handled before the\n  alignment lookup. Stats should record them under a separate `whitelisted_keys`\n  counter.\n- Every ProvidedCHO gets at least one rdf:type triple (`mocho:Manifestation`).\n  Objects with a mapped htype get two rdf:type triples. Objects with a pending\n  or absent htype get one. No `edm:ProvidedCHO` rdf:type triples appear in the\n  output; verification step 2 should grep for its absence.\n\n---\n\n## Decision 9: Every ProvidedCHO is typed as mocho:Manifestation\n\n**Decision**: `retype_cho()` unconditionally emits\n`<cho_uri> rdf:type mocho:Manifestation` for every ProvidedCHO. Where a\n`hierarchyType` code is present and maps to a DoCO or RiC-O class, that class\nis emitted as an additional, more specific rdf:type. The `edm:ProvidedCHO`\nfallback type is not emitted.\n\n**Rationale**: `edm:ProvidedCHO` is an EDM modelling artefact describing the\nrole of the entity within an EDM Aggregation, not an ontological type for the\nobject itself. In the mocho/WEMI model, DDB items — regardless of media type,\ninstitutional sector, or structural position — represent cultural heritage\nobjects as provided by an institution, which maps to the Manifestation level of\nthe WEMI hierarchy. `mocho:Manifestation` is therefore the correct base type\nfor all ProvidedCHOs.\n\nThe DoCO or RiC-O class (where present) is a specialisation of this base type,\ndescribing the structural or archival nature of the object within its\nManifestation. Emitting both types allows SPARQL queries to match either the\ngeneral Manifestation class or the specific structural class.\n\n**Alternatives considered**:\n- *Keep `edm:ProvidedCHO` as fallback*: Preserves EDM provenance but asserts\n  an EDM structural class in the mocho graph. Rejected: mixes EDM and mocho\n  semantics in a way that misleads downstream consumers.\n- *Omit rdf:type for pending/missing htype*: Leaves ~22,475 records (19.5%)\n  with no class. Rejected: untyped nodes are harder to query and do not reflect\n  the known WEMI alignment.\n- *Type as mocho:Work instead*: A DDB item could be argued to be a Work. Rejected:\n  the ProvidedCHO represents the object as held and provided — the carrier level\n  — which is Manifestation, not the abstract Work."
}
```

**3. `Event.occuredAt`** — probable DDB API typo for `edm:occurredAt`; no IRI resolves so no triples are emitted. Shows up in unmatched stats. Cosmetic — nothing to fix in the transform unless you want a hard-coded alias.

Issue 3 is trivial. Issue 4 is a code note. Issue 5 has a real design question now that D9 exists. Want to start with 5?

### Assistant *(2026-04-15T08:36:11.903Z)*

Two remaining open issues in the plan:

**4. `in_mocho` column type** — implementation note only. `in_mocho` is a string `"True"`/`"False"` in the CSV, not a Python bool. No design decision; just a code correctness reminder for when the script is written.

**5. `PhysicalThing.hierarchyType`** — 55,771 records carry it; `retype_cho()` currently skips it. This now has a real question following D9: should PhysicalThing entities also receive `mocho:Manifestation` as a base type, or something different?

Issue 4 needs no discussion. Issue 5 is the only substantive one left. Want to address it?

---

### User *(2026-04-15T08:36:38.132Z)*

what is #4 `in_mocho`?

---

### Assistant *(2026-04-15T08:36:42.961Z)*

It's a reminder that when the script reads `alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv` with Python's `csv.DictReader`, the value in the `in_mocho` column comes out as the string `"True"` or `"False"` — not a Python `bool`. So the filter condition in `load_alignment()` must be:

```python
row['in_mocho'] == 'True'
```

not

```python
row['in_mocho']  # always truthy — non-empty string
```

or

```python
row['in_mocho'] == True  # never matches a string
```

That's the entirety of it — a one-line code correctness note, no design decision needed.

---

### User *(2026-04-15T08:39:55.076Z)*

better change it to python bool? or leave as is

---

### Assistant *(2026-04-15T08:40:00.699Z)*

Leave as-is. The CSV is a generated output file shared with other consumers (ISWC paper appendix, future scripts). Changing the values to `True`/`False` without quotes would break CSV spec — booleans aren't a CSV type. Any reader has to handle the string conversion anyway. The note in the plan is sufficient.

---

### Assistant *(2026-04-15T11:24:05.618Z)*

There's the answer. `PhysicalThing` entities are **archival hierarchy nodes** — they carry `hierarchyType`, `title`, `hierarchyPosition`, `isPartOf`, and `aggregationEntity`. They are not physical objects at all; they represent the archival structure (Tektonik, Bestand, Gliederung etc.) that the ProvidedCHO sits within.

This has direct implications for issue 5: these are RiC-O `RecordSet` entities, not Manifestations. Typing them as `mocho:Manifestation` would be wrong. They should be retyped via the same htype lookup as ProvidedCHO — `htype_031 → rico:RecordSet + mocho:Gliederung` in this case.

So `retype_cho()` should be generalised to handle both `ProvidedCHO` and `PhysicalThing`, with the same htype dispatch logic. Want to update the plan accordingly?

### Assistant *(2026-04-15T11:24:41.258Z)*

Now the picture is clear. `PhysicalThing` entities are archival **ancestor nodes** in the hierarchy — the ProvidedCHO (`htype_034` Archivale) sits within a Gliederung (`htype_031`), which sits within another Gliederung, and so on up to the Bestand/Tektonik. The PhysicalThing array carries the parent nodes inline in the record.

Key observations:
- `PhysicalThing` has its own `about` URI — **different** from the ProvidedCHO URI
- It has `hierarchyType`, `title`, `isPartOf`, `hierarchyPosition` — same structure as ProvidedCHO
- `aggregationEntity: "true"` on PhysicalThing vs `"false"` on ProvidedCHO — marks them as container nodes
- They have **no** `dc:creator`, `dc:description`, etc. — sparse, hierarchy-only fields

So the retyping question for issue 5: these are `rico:RecordSet` nodes (htype_031 → Gliederung), not Manifestations. Typing them as `mocho:Manifestation` would be wrong. The same htype dispatch as ProvidedCHO applies, but **without** the `mocho:Manifestation` base type.

Shall I update the plan and ADR?

---

### Assistant *(2026-04-15T11:36:11.797Z)*

Confirmed. The `PhysicalThing` array is the object's archival lineage — each node is an ancestor in the finding aid hierarchy, with its own URI and `hierarchyType`. They map directly to RiC-O classes via the htype lookup (Bestand → `rico:RecordSet + ric-rst:Fonds`, Gliederung → `rico:RecordSet + mocho:Gliederung`, etc.). No `mocho:Manifestation` — these are archival aggregation nodes, not carried objects.

Updating plan and ADR now:

{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/goethe-faust/notes/alignment-plan.md",
  "old_string": "def retype_cho(rdf, htype_map) -> list[str]:\n    # Every ProvidedCHO is typed as mocho:Manifestation — always, unconditionally.\n    # If hierarchyType is present and not pending, also emit the DoCO or RiC-O class\n    # as an additional, more specific rdf:type.\n    # If has_record_set_type: emit <cho_uri> <rico:hasRecordSetType> <named_individual> .\n    # No edm:ProvidedCHO fallback — mocho:Manifestation serves as the base type for\n    # all cases (pending htype, missing htype, and mapped htype alike).",
  "new_string": "def retype_entities(rdf, htype_map) -> list[str]:\n    # Handles rdf:type for ProvidedCHO and PhysicalThing — the two entity types\n    # that carry hierarchyType.\n    #\n    # ProvidedCHO:\n    #   - Always emit <cho_uri> rdf:type mocho:Manifestation (base type, D9).\n    #   - If hierarchyType present and mapped: also emit DoCO or RiC-O class.\n    #   - If has_record_set_type: emit <cho_uri> rico:hasRecordSetType <named_individual>.\n    #   - No edm:ProvidedCHO fallback.\n    #\n    # PhysicalThing (archival hierarchy ancestors, each with own URI):\n    #   - Each entity in the array carries its own hierarchyType → RiC-O class.\n    #   - No mocho:Manifestation — these are archival aggregation nodes (RecordSet,\n    #     Record, RecordPart), not carried objects.\n    #   - Same htype lookup; if pending or absent: emit nothing (no fallback type).\n    #   - If has_record_set_type: emit rico:hasRecordSetType as for ProvidedCHO."
}
```

    95→## Decision 5: RDF output format — N-Triples for pipeline, Turtle for hand-authored
    96→
    97→**Decision**: Transform output (`mocho-goethe-faust.nt`) and all pipeline
    98→intermediate files use N-Triples (`.nt`). Hand-authored files (ontology,
    99→SPARQL update templates) use Turtle (`.ttl`). N-Quads (`.nq`) if named graph
   100→identity is required.
   101→
   102→**Rationale**: N-Triples are one triple per line — grep/awk/stream-friendly,
   103→no parser context, trivial to sort and dedup with Unix tools. The verbosity
   104→(full URIs repeated) is acceptable for machine-processed intermediates.
   105→Turtle prefix compression is valuable for human-readable hand-authored files.
   106→
   107→---
   108→
   109→## Decision 6: Triple subject keys — IRI correction + value-type dispatch
   110→
   111→**Decision**: Three JSON keys carry subject data: `dcSubject`, `dcTermsSubject`,
   112→`dcTermSubject`. These are handled by a dedicated `emit_subject_triples()`
   113→function, not the generic alignment loop.
   114→



```

### User *(2026-04-15T12:18:58.793Z)*

**Tool result**
```
   248→**Rationale**: `edm:ProvidedCHO` is an EDM modelling artefact describing the
   249→role of the entity within an EDM Aggregation, not an ontological type for the
   250→object itself. In the mocho/WEMI model, DDB items — regardless of media type,
   251→institutional sector, or structural position — represent cultural heritage
   252→objects as provided by an institution, which maps to the Manifestation level of
   253→the WEMI hierarchy. `mocho:Manifestation` is therefore the correct base type
   254→for all ProvidedCHOs.
   255→
   256→The DoCO or RiC-O class (where present) is a specialisation of this base type,
   257→describing the structural or archival nature of the object within its
   258→Manifestation. Emitting both types allows SPARQL queries to match either the
   259→general Manifestation class or the specific structural class.
   260→
   261→**Alternatives considered**:
   262→- *Keep `edm:ProvidedCHO` as fallback*: Preserves EDM provenance but asserts
   263→  an EDM structural class in the mocho graph. Rejected: mixes EDM and mocho
   264→  semantics in a way that misleads downstream consumers.
   265→- *Omit rdf:type for pending/missing htype*: Leaves ~22,475 records (19.5%)
   266→  with no class. Rejected: untyped nodes are harder to query and do not reflect
   267→  the known WEMI alignment.
   268→- *Type as mocho:Work instead*: A DDB item could be argued to be a Work. Rejected:
   269→  the ProvidedCHO represents the object as held and provided — the carrier level
   270→  — which is Manifestation, not the abstract Work.
   271→



```

  "replace_all": false,

  "old_string": "- *Type as mocho:Work instead*: A DDB item could be argued to be a Work. Rejected:\n  the ProvidedCHO represents the object as held and provided — the carrier level\n  — which is Manifestation, not the abstract Work.",
  "new_string": "- *Type as mocho:Work instead*: A DDB item could be argued to be a Work. Rejected:\n  the ProvidedCHO represents the object as held and provided — the carrier level\n  — which is Manifestation, not the abstract Work.\n\n---\n\n## Decision 10: PhysicalThing entities typed via htype lookup, no mocho:Manifestation\n\n**Decision**: `PhysicalThing` entities in the JSONL are archival hierarchy\nancestors of the ProvidedCHO — the finding aid lineage (Tektonik, Bestand,\nGliederung, etc.) stored inline in each record. Each has its own `about` URI\nand `hierarchyType`. They are typed via the same htype lookup as ProvidedCHO\nbut receive **no** `mocho:Manifestation` base type. If `hierarchyType` is\nabsent or pending, no rdf:type triple is emitted for that entity.\n\n**Background**: Corpus inspection of record `224BB273RJDT6WN7GAIRV4AJ5ES5YPC5`\nconfirmed the structure. The PhysicalThing array carries the object's ancestor\nnodes (e.g. two `htype_031 Gliederung` nodes). These are archival aggregation\ncontainers — `rico:RecordSet` with named individuals — not carried objects.\nThe DDB UI renders them as the \"Verbundene Objekte\" hierarchy tree.\n\n**Rationale**: `mocho:Manifestation` is the base type for carried objects\n(ProvidedCHO, D9). Archival hierarchy nodes are `rico:RecordSet` / `rico:Record`\n/ `rico:RecordPart` depending on htype — a different branch of the mocho type\nsystem. Applying `mocho:Manifestation` to them would conflate the carried object\nwith its archival containers and produce incorrect SPARQL results.\n\n**Implementation**: `retype_cho()` is renamed to `retype_entities()` and\nextended to iterate over both `ProvidedCHO` (single dict) and `PhysicalThing`\n(array). ProvidedCHO logic unchanged (D9). PhysicalThing loop: htype lookup →\nemit RiC-O class and `rico:hasRecordSetType` where applicable; no Manifestation\nassertion; no fallback."
}
```

Yes — 13 archival htype codes, all mapped. These are the ones `retype_entities()` will use for `PhysicalThing`. The full picture:

| htype | Label | rdf:type | hasRecordSetType |
|---|---|---|---|
| htype_030 | Bestand | rico:RecordSet | mocho:Bestand, ric-rst:Fonds |
| htype_031 | Gliederung | rico:RecordSet | mocho:Gliederung, ric-rst:Series |
| htype_032 | Serie | rico:RecordSet | ric-rst:Series |
| htype_033 | Unterserie | rico:RecordSet | mocho:Unterserie |
| htype_034 | Archivale | rico:Record | — |
| htype_035 | Teil | rico:RecordPart | — |
| htype_036 | Bestandsserie | rico:RecordSet | mocho:Bestandsserie, ric-rst:Collection |
| htype_037 | Bestandsklassifikation | rico:RecordSet | mocho:Bestandsklassifikation |
| htype_038 | Brief | rico:Record | — |
| htype_039 | Konvolut | rico:RecordSet | — |
| htype_040 | Mappe | rico:RecordSet | — |
| htype_041 | Archiv | rico:RecordSet | ric-rst:Collection |
| htype_048 | Tektonik | rico:RecordSet | mocho:Tektonik |

Note: `htype_031` has two `hasRecordSetType` values (`mocho:Gliederung, ric-rst:Series`) — same for htype_030, 036. The `load_htype_map()` function needs to handle comma-separated multiple values in that column.
