### User *(2026-04-15T12:31:57.695Z)*

**Tool result**
```
     1→# Plan: EDM JSONL → mocho RDF Transformation (POC)
     2→
     3→**Date**: 2026-04-14
     4→**Status**: Approved
     5→**Related**: `goethe-faust/notes/alignment-adr.md`, `mocho/notes/alignment-ddbedm-mocho-adr.md`, `mocho/notes/alignment-ddbedm-mocho-spec.md`, `mocho/notes/alignment-ddbedm-mocho.md`
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## 1. Context
    10→
    11→The alignment table `alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv` was built by walking `edm.RDF.*` JSON fields in the JSONL corpus, then resolving `json_key → edm_iri → rda_iri` via the DCT→RDA map. The table is keyed by `(entity_type, json_key)` — matching the JSON structure of the JSONL, not the NT file.
    12→
    13→The JSONL (`items-all-goethe-faust.json`) is the right input: it is already per-object, streams with constant memory, and requires only stdlib JSON parsing. No NT indexing phase needed.
    14→
    15→The NT file is not used — it was generated from this same JSONL and carries no additional information for this alignment pass.
    16→
    17→---
    18→
    19→## 2. Critical files
    20→
    21→| File | Role |
    22→|---|---|
    23→| `goethe-faust/data/items-all-goethe-faust.json` | Source JSONL (115,432 records, one per line) |
    24→| `goethe-faust/data/ids-all-goethe-faust.txt` | 115,437 object IDs (32-char each); filter set |
    25→| `goethe-faust/output/alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv` | Alignment table — keyed by `(entity_type, json_key)`; columns: `entity_type, json_key, edm_prefix, edm_iri, record_count, coverage_pct, rda_iri, rda_label, wemi_level, match_method, in_mocho` |
    26→| `goethe-faust/output/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv` | htype code → DoCO/RiC-O class lookup |
    27→| `goethe-faust/scripts/README.md` | Must be updated after script is added |
    28→
    29→**Reference notes:**
    30→- `mocho/notes/alignment-ddbedm-mocho.md` — working note; entity type inventory, fanout figures, known issues
    31→- `mocho/notes/alignment-ddbedm-mocho-spec.md` — scope definition; in/out of scope per entity type
    32→- `mocho/notes/alignment-ddbedm-mocho-adr.md` — decisions; especially D2 (DC→RDA route), D4 (EDM-structural out of scope)
    33→
    34→---
    35→
    36→## 3. Script: `goethe-faust/scripts/transform_edm_to_mocho.py`
    37→
    38→### 3.1 Header
    39→
    40→```
    41→Purpose:    Transform DDB-EDM JSONL records to mocho-aligned RDF triples (N-Triples)
    42→Usage:      python transform_edm_to_mocho.py [--jsonl FILE] [--ids FILE] [--out FILE] [--limit N]
    43→Inputs:     items-all-goethe-faust.json, ids-all-goethe-faust.txt,
    44→            alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv, lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv
    45→Outputs:    mocho-goethe-faust.nt, transform_stats.json
    46→Deps:       stdlib only (json, csv, re, collections, argparse, pathlib)
    47→Assumes:    JSONL: one JSON object per line; edm.RDF.* structure per alignment-ddbedm-mocho.md
    48→```
    49→
    50→### 3.2 Constants
    51→
    52→```python
    53→CHO_BASE        = "http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de/item/"
    54→RDF_TYPE        = "http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type"
    55→EDM_CHO              = "http://www.europeana.eu/schemas/edm/ProvidedCHO"
    56→DDB_HTYPE            = "ddb:hierarchyType"          # json_key in alignment table
    57→MOCHO_NS             = "https://ise-fizkarlsruhe.github.io/ddbkg/mocho#"
    58→MOCHO_MANIFESTATION  = MOCHO_NS + "Manifestation"   # every ProvidedCHO is an instance of this
    59→RICO_RST        = "http://www.ica.org/standards/RiC/ontology#hasRecordSetType"
    60→
    61→# Fan-out whitelist (issue 2): bypass alignment table for creator/contributor
    62→CREATOR_IRI     = "http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/m/P30263"   # rdam:P30263 has creator agent of manifestation
    63→CONTRIBUTOR_IRI = "http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/contributor" # dc:contributor (kept as-is; no generic RDA equivalent in mocho)
    64→```
    65→
    66→### 3.3 Phase 1 — Load lookup tables
    67→
    68→```python
    69→def load_alignment(path) -> dict[tuple[str, str], list[dict]]:
    70→    # Key: (entity_type, json_key)
    71→    # Value: list of rows where rda_iri non-empty and in_mocho == 'True'
    72→    # Each row: {rda_iri, rda_label, wemi_level, match_method}
    73→    # Rows with in_mocho=False or rda_iri empty → excluded
    74→
    75→def load_htype_map(path) -> dict[str, tuple[str, str | None]]:
    76→    # Key: htype_code (e.g. "htype_030") — confirmed format in JSONL literals
    77→    # Value: (rdf_type_iri, has_record_set_type_iri or None)
    78→    # Skip rows where rdf_type == "pending"
    79→    # Expand prefix to full IRI (doco:, rico:, ric-rst:, mocho:)
    80→```
    81→
    82→### 3.4 Phase 2 — Load IDs filter
    83→
    84→```python
    85→def load_ids(path) -> set[str]:
    86→    # 32-char IDs, one per line; strip whitespace
    87→```
    88→
    89→### 3.5 Phase 3 — Stream JSONL and transform
    90→
    91→#### 3.5.1 Record structure (per `alignment-ddbedm-mocho.md`)
    92→
    93→```
    94→record.edm.RDF = {
    95→  "ProvidedCHO": {dict},          # single entity
    96→  "Aggregation": {dict},          # single entity
    97→  "Agent":       [{dict}, ...],   # array
    98→  "Concept":     [{dict}, ...],   # array
    99→  "WebResource": [{dict}, ...],   # array
   100→  "Event":       [{dict}, ...],   # may be absent
   101→  "Place":       [{dict}, ...],   # may be absent
   102→  "TimeSpan":    [{dict}, ...],   # may be absent
   103→  "PhysicalThing":[{dict}, ...],  # may be absent
   104→}
   105→each entity: {"about": "<subject_uri>", "<json_key>": <value>, ...}
   106→value types: null | str | list | {"resource": str|null, "lang": str|null, "$": str}
   107→```
   108→
   109→#### 3.5.2 Object ID resolution
   110→
   111→```python
   112→def get_object_id(record) -> str | None:
   113→    # Extract 32-char ID from ProvidedCHO.about URI
   114→    # about = "http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de/item/{id}"
   115→    # Return id portion; None if not matching pattern
   116→```
   117→
   118→#### 3.5.3 Field value → NT object
   119→
   120→```python
   121→def value_to_nt_obj(val) -> list[str]:
   122→    # Handles all value shapes:
   123→    #   null → []
   124→    #   str  → ['"str"']
   125→    #   list → flatten recursively
   126→    #   {"resource": IRI, ...}  → ["<IRI>"]  (if resource non-null)
   127→    #   {"lang": L, "$": text}  → ['"text"@L'] (if lang non-null, text non-empty)
   128→    #   {"lang": null, "$": text} → ['"text"']
   129→    # Skips nulls and empty strings
   130→    # Escapes backslash, double-quote in literals
   131→```
   132→
   133→#### 3.5.4 Core transform loop
   134→
   135→```python
   136→def transform_record(record, alignment, htype_map, ids_filter) -> list[str]:
   137→    # Returns list of NT lines for this record
   138→    obj_id = get_object_id(record)
   139→    if obj_id not in ids_filter:
   140→        return []
   141→
   142→    nt_lines = []
   143→    rdf = record["edm"]["RDF"]
   144→
   145→    for entity_type, entities in rdf.items():
   146→        if entities is None:
   147→            continue
   148→        if isinstance(entities, dict):
   149→            entities = [entities]
   150→        for entity in entities:
   151→            subject = entity.get("about")
   152→            if not subject:
   153→                continue
   154→            subject_nt = f"<{subject}>"
   155→
   156→            # Subject fields: value-type dispatch + cross-key deduplication
   157→            # dcSubject / dcTermsSubject / dcTermSubject all carry subject data;
   158→            # dcTermsSubject was incorrectly mapped to dc:subject — corrected in CSV.
   159→            # After fix: dcSubject → dc:subject (literals); dcTermSubject/dcTermsSubject → dcterms:subject (IRIs).
   160→            # Dispatch: collect unique values across all three keys; route by value type.
   161→            if entity_type == "ProvidedCHO":
   162→                nt_lines.extend(emit_subject_triples(entity, subject_nt, alignment))
   163→
   164→            for json_key, raw_val in entity.items():
   165→                if json_key == "about" or raw_val is None:
   166→                    continue
   167→                if entity_type == "ProvidedCHO" and json_key in SUBJECT_KEYS:
   168→                    continue  # handled by emit_subject_triples
   169→
   170→                # Fan-out whitelist (issue 2):
   171→                #   creator    → single RDA property rdaw:P10065 (has creator agent of work)
   172→                #   contributor → dc:contributor kept as-is; no generic RDA equivalent in mocho
   173→                if entity_type == "ProvidedCHO" and json_key in ("creator", "contributor"):
   174→                    pred_iri = CREATOR_IRI if json_key == "creator" else CONTRIBUTOR_IRI
   175→                    pred_nt  = f"<{pred_iri}>"
   176→                    for obj_nt in value_to_nt_obj(raw_val):
   177→                        nt_lines.append(f"{subject_nt} {pred_nt} {obj_nt} .")
   178→                    continue
   179→
   180→                candidates = alignment.get((entity_type, json_key), [])
   181→                for row in candidates:
   182→                    pred_nt = f"<{row['rda_iri']}>"
   183→                    for obj_nt in value_to_nt_obj(raw_val):
   184→                        nt_lines.append(f"{subject_nt} {pred_nt} {obj_nt} .")
   185→
   186→    # rdf:type for ProvidedCHO and PhysicalThing via ddb:hierarchyType
   187→    nt_lines.extend(retype_entities(rdf, htype_map))
   188→    return nt_lines
   189→
   190→SUBJECT_KEYS = {"dcSubject", "dcTermsSubject", "dcTermSubject"}
   191→
   192→def emit_subject_triples(entity, subject_nt, alignment) -> list[str]:
   193→    # Collect all values from the three subject keys; deduplicate by (value_shape, content).
   194→    # Dispatch each unique value:
   195→    #   literal (str or lang-tagged text) → candidates for ("ProvidedCHO", "dcSubject")
   196→    #   IRI (resource) → candidates for ("ProvidedCHO", "dcTermSubject")
   197→    # Rationale: dc:subject is for uncontrolled literals; dcterms:subject for IRI references
   198→    #            to controlled vocabulary terms (GND, LCSH, etc.).
   199→    # dcTermsSubject IRI corrected in alignment CSV (was dc:subject, now dcterms:subject).
   200→    lit_candidates = alignment.get(("ProvidedCHO", "dcSubject"), [])
   201→    iri_candidates = alignment.get(("ProvidedCHO", "dcTermSubject"), [])
   202→
   203→    seen_objs: set[tuple[str, str]] = set()  # (pred_nt, obj_nt) dedup
   204→    nt_lines = []
   205→    for key in SUBJECT_KEYS:
   206→        raw_val = entity.get(key)
   207→        if raw_val is None:
   208→            continue
   209→        for obj_nt in value_to_nt_obj(raw_val):
   210→            is_iri = obj_nt.startswith("<")
   211→            candidates = iri_candidates if is_iri else lit_candidates
   212→            for row in candidates:
   213→                pred_nt = f"<{row['rda_iri']}>"
   214→                pair = (pred_nt, obj_nt)
   215→                if pair not in seen_objs:
   216→                    seen_objs.add(pair)
   217→                    nt_lines.append(f"{subject_nt} {pred_nt} {obj_nt} .")
   218→    return nt_lines
   219→
   220→def retype_entities(rdf, htype_map) -> list[str]:
   221→    # Handles rdf:type for ProvidedCHO and PhysicalThing — the two entity types
   222→    # that carry hierarchyType.
   223→    #
   224→    # ProvidedCHO:
   225→    #   - Always emit <cho_uri> rdf:type mocho:Manifestation (base type, D9).
   226→    #   - If hierarchyType present and mapped: also emit DoCO or RiC-O class.
   227→    #   - If has_record_set_type: emit <cho_uri> rico:hasRecordSetType <named_individual>.
   228→    #   - No edm:ProvidedCHO fallback.
   229→    #
   230→    # PhysicalThing (archival hierarchy ancestors, each with own URI):
   231→    #   - Each entity in the array carries its own hierarchyType → RiC-O class.
   232→    #   - No mocho:Manifestation — these are archival aggregation nodes (RecordSet,
   233→    #     Record, RecordPart), not carried objects.
   234→    #   - Same htype lookup; if pending or absent: emit nothing (no fallback type).
   235→    #   - If has_record_set_type: emit rico:hasRecordSetType as for ProvidedCHO.
   236→```
   237→
   238→### 3.6 Phase 4 — Output
   239→
   240→```python
   241→def main():
   242→    alignment  = load_alignment(ALIGNMENT_CSV)
   243→    htype_map  = load_htype_map(HTYPE_CSV)
   244→    ids_filter = load_ids(args.ids)
   245→
   246→    stats = defaultdict(int)
   247→    unmatched_keys = Counter()
   248→
   249→    with open(args.jsonl) as in_fh, open(args.out, "w") as out_fh:
   250→        for lineno, line in enumerate(in_fh):
   251→            if args.limit and lineno >= args.limit:
   252→                break
   253→            record = json.loads(line)
   254→            nt_lines = transform_record(record, alignment, htype_map, ids_filter)
   255→            for line in nt_lines:
   256→                out_fh.write(line + "\n")
   257→            stats["records_processed"] += 1
   258→            stats["triples_out"] += len(nt_lines)
   259→
   260→    write_stats(STATS_OUT, stats, unmatched_keys)
   261→```
   262→
   263→### 3.7 Stats output (`transform_stats.json`)
   264→
   265→```json
   266→{
   267→  "records_processed": 115432,
   268→  "triples_out": ...,
   269→  "top_unmatched_keys": [["Aggregation.edmRights", 115432], ...],
   270→  "objects_missing_specific_type": ...,
   271→  "whitelisted_keys": {"creator": "rdam:P30263", "contributor": "dc:contributor"}
   272→}
   273→```
   274→
   275→---
   276→
   277→## 4. Alignment scope (per ADR + spec)
   278→
   279→- **Mapped**: rows where `in_mocho=True` and `rda_iri` non-empty
   280→- **Skipped silently**: `edm:isShownAt`, `edm:aggregatedCHO`, `edm:provider`, etc. — EDM-structural, per ADR Decision 4; appear as `unmatched` in alignment table
   281→- **Skipped silently**: `skos:prefLabel/altLabel`, geo coordinates, EDM-native agent properties — unmatched, deferred to second pass
   282→- **Fan-out**: `dc:creator` → `rdam:P30263` only; `dc:contributor` → `dc:contributor` directly (see ADR D7–D8)
   283→- **rdf:type**: every ProvidedCHO gets `mocho:Manifestation`; where `hierarchyType` is present and mapped, also gets the DoCO or RiC-O class as an additional type; all other entity types get no rdf:type triple in this pass
   284→
   285→---
   286→
   287→## 5. Outputs
   288→
   289→| File | Description |
   290→|---|---|
   291→| `goethe-faust/output/mocho-goethe-faust.nt` | mocho-aligned NT triples (all objects merged) |
   292→| `goethe-faust/output/transform_stats.json` | records processed, triples out, unmatched keys, fanout note |
   293→
   294→---
   295→
   296→## 6. Verification
   297→
   298→Select one object per sector × mediatype combination for spot-checking. Coverage confirmed by grep on `items-all-goethe-faust.json`.
   299→
   300→**Sectors** — IRI base `http://ddb.vocnet.org/sparte/sparte00N`:
   301→
   302→| Code | Label | Records |
   303→|---|---|---|
   304→| sparte001 | Archiv | 50,216 |
   305→| sparte002 | Bibliothek | 50,198 |
   306→| sparte003 | Denkmalfach | 111 |
   307→| sparte004 | Mediathek | 1,283 |
   308→| sparte005 | Museum | 4,288 |
   309→| sparte006 | Sonstige | 9,215 |
   310→| sparte007 | Wissenschaftliche Einrichtung | 85 |
   311→
   312→**Mediatypes** — IRI base `http://ddb.vocnet.org/medientyp/mt00N` (mt004, mt006 absent):
   313→
   314→| Code | Label | Records |
   315→|---|---|---|
   316→| mt001 | Audio | 476 |
   317→| mt002 | Photo | 20,228 |
   318→| mt003 | Text | 52,247 |
   319→| mt005 | Video | 96 |
   320→| mt007 | Not Digitized | 42,360 |
   321→
   322→Note: not all 35 sector × mediatype combinations are populated. Small sectors (sparte003 = 111, sparte007 = 85) and small mediatypes (mt001 = 476, mt005 = 96) may lack certain intersections — document missing combinations in `transform_stats.json`.
   323→
   324→Steps:
   325→1. Run `--limit N` covering the full corpus (or run full pass); inspect NT output per spot-check object
   326→2. For each sector, select one object from each mediatype present in that sector; confirm every ProvidedCHO has a `mocho:Manifestation` rdf:type triple; where `hierarchyType` is present and mapped, confirm an additional DoCO or RiC-O rdf:type triple also appears; confirm no `edm:ProvidedCHO` rdf:type triples in output
   327→3. Confirm at least one RDA property triple is emitted for `dc:title`, `dc:creator`, or `dc:description`
   328→4. `transform_stats.json`: `triples_out / records_processed` ≈ 20–80 (reasonable range); `objects_missing_htype` documented
   329→5. Grep output NT for `europeana.eu` predicates — should be zero (all EDM predicates replaced or skipped)
   330→6. Update `goethe-faust/scripts/README.md`
   331→
   332→---
   333→
   334→## 7. RDF serialization choice
   335→
   336→| Format | Process | Size | Read |
   337→|---|---|---|---|
   338→| **N-Triples** (`.nt`) | Best — one triple/line, no parser context, grep/awk/stream-friendly | Worst — full URIs repeated every line | Poor |
   339→| **Turtle** (`.ttl`) | Good — most parsers support it | Best among text formats — prefix compression | Best |
   340→| **JSON-LD** (`.jsonld`) | Moderate — needs JSON + context handling | Moderate | Moderate (if you know JSON) |
   341→| RDF/XML | Worst | Worst | Worst |
   342→
   343→**Decision**: N-Triples for pipeline intermediate files (transform output, QLever ingest, sort/dedup); Turtle for hand-authored files (ontology, SPARQL update templates). N-Quads (`.nq`) if named graph identity is required alongside the same processing advantages.
   344→
   345→---
   346→
   347→## 8. Known issues to flag in code comments (per `alignment-ddbedm-mocho.md`)
   348→
   349→- **Triple subject keys**: ~~deduplication downstream~~ — **resolved**. `dcTermsSubject` IRI corrected in CSV (`dc:subject` → `dcterms:subject`). Transform uses `emit_subject_triples()`: value-type dispatch (literal → dc:subject candidates, IRI → dcterms:subject candidates) + per-record `(pred, obj)` deduplication across all three keys.
   350→- **Fan-out**: ~~not filtered in POC~~ — **resolved**. `creator` (464 Work-level candidates) → whitelisted to `rdaw:P10065 has creator agent of work`. `contributor` (360 candidates, no generic RDA equivalent in mocho) → kept as `dc:contributor` directly. Both bypass the alignment table via early `continue` in the transform loop.
   351→- **`Event.occurredAt`**: ~~no IRI resolved~~ — **resolved**. DDB API typo (`occuredAt`, one 'r') added to `OVERRIDES` in `align_ddbedm_to_mocho.py` → resolves to `edm:occurredAt`. `edm_field_profile.json` corrected to `occurredAt`. Will be picked up on next alignment re-run.
   352→- **`in_mocho` column type**: string `"True"` / `"False"`, not a Python bool — compare as `row['in_mocho'] == 'True'`
   353→- **`PhysicalThing.hierarchyType`**: ~~skipped~~ — **resolved**. `retype_entities()` handles both `ProvidedCHO` and `PhysicalThing`. PhysicalThing entities are archival hierarchy ancestors (Bestand, Gliederung, etc.) → typed via htype lookup to RiC-O classes only; no `mocho:Manifestation` (they are aggregation nodes, not carried objects). No fallback type if htype absent or pending.
   354→



```

{
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/.claude/plans/elegant-wondering-blanket.md",
  "content": "# Implementation Plan: `transform_edm_to_mocho.py`\n\n## Context\n\nAll design decisions are finalised in `goethe-faust/notes/alignment-plan.md` (approved)\nand `goethe-faust/notes/alignment-adr.md` (D1–D10). This plan translates them into a\nconcrete implementation checklist. The script transforms 115,432 DDB-EDM JSONL records\ninto mocho-aligned N-Triples, streaming with constant memory, stdlib only.\n\n---\n\n## Critical files\n\n| Path | Role |\n|---|---|\n| `goethe-faust/scripts/transform_edm_to_mocho.py` | **New script** |\n| `goethe-faust/data/items-all-goethe-faust.json` | Source JSONL |\n| `goethe-faust/data/ids-all-goethe-faust.txt` | ID filter set |\n| `goethe-faust/output/alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv` | Alignment table |\n| `goethe-faust/output/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv` | htype → DoCO/RiC-O |\n| `goethe-faust/output/mocho-goethe-faust.nt` | **New output** |\n| `goethe-faust/output/transform_stats.json` | **New output** |\n| `goethe-faust/scripts/README.md` | Must be updated |\n\n---\n\n## Implementation\n\n### 1. Header block\n\n```\nPurpose:    Transform DDB-EDM JSONL records to mocho-aligned RDF triples (N-Triples)\nUsage:      python transform_edm_to_mocho.py [--jsonl FILE] [--ids FILE] [--out FILE]\n            [--stats FILE] [--limit N]\nInputs:     items-all-goethe-faust.json, ids-all-goethe-faust.txt,\n            alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv, lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv\nOutputs:    mocho-goethe-faust.nt, transform_stats.json\nDeps:       stdlib only (json, csv, re, collections, argparse, pathlib)\nAssumes:    JSONL one JSON object per line; edm.RDF.* structure per alignment-ddbedm-mocho.md\n```\n\n### 2. Constants\n\n```python\nCHO_BASE             = \"http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de/item/\"\nRDF_TYPE             = \"http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type\"\nMOCHO_NS             = \"https://ise-fizkarlsruhe.github.io/ddbkg/mocho#\"\nMOCHO_MANIFESTATION  = MOCHO_NS + \"Manifestation\"\nRICO_RST             = \"http://www.ica.org/standards/RiC/ontology#hasRecordSetType\"\nCREATOR_IRI          = \"http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/m/P30263\"   # has creator agent of manifestation\nCONTRIBUTOR_IRI      = \"http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/contributor\" # kept as-is; no generic RDA equiv in mocho\nSUBJECT_KEYS         = {\"dcSubject\", \"dcTermsSubject\", \"dcTermSubject\"}\n\n# Prefix expansion for load_htype_map\nPREFIXES = {\n    \"doco\":    \"http://purl.org/spar/doco/\",\n    \"rico\":    \"http://www.ica.org/standards/RiC/ontology#\",\n    \"ric-rst\": \"http://www.ica.org/standards/RiC/vocabularies/recordSetTypes#\",\n    \"mocho\":   MOCHO_NS,\n}\n```\n\n### 3. `load_alignment(path)` → `dict[tuple[str,str], list[dict]]`\n\n- Read `alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv` with `csv.DictReader`\n- Include row iff `row['in_mocho'] == 'True'` and `row['rda_iri']` non-empty\n  (string comparison — `in_mocho` is not a Python bool)\n- Key: `(row['entity_type'], row['json_key'])`\n- Value: list of `{'rda_iri': ..., 'rda_label': ..., 'wemi_level': ..., 'match_method': ...}`\n\n### 4. `load_htype_map(path)` → `dict[str, tuple[str, list[str]]]`\n\n- Read `lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv`\n- Skip rows where `rdf_type == 'pending'` or `rdf_type` empty\n- Key: `row['htype_code']` (e.g. `\"htype_030\"`)\n- Value: `(rdf_type_iri, [rst_iri, ...])` where:\n  - `rdf_type_iri` = expand prefix → full IRI using PREFIXES dict\n  - `[rst_iri, ...]` = split `row['has_record_set_type']` on `', '`, expand each\n    prefix → full IRI; empty list if column is blank\n  - **Multi-value**: `has_record_set_type` can be comma-separated\n    (e.g. `\"mocho:Bestand, ric-rst:Fonds\"`) → emit one `rico:hasRecordSetType`\n    triple per individual\n\n### 5. `load_ids(path)` → `set[str]`\n\n- One 32-char ID per line; strip whitespace\n\n### 6. `get_object_id(record)` → `str | None`\n\n- `record['edm']['RDF']['ProvidedCHO']['about']`\n- Split on `/`, return last segment (32-char ID)\n- Return `None` if path missing or pattern mismatch\n\n### 7. `value_to_nt_obj(val)` → `list[str]`\n\nHandle all value shapes, returning valid NT object strings:\n\n| Input shape | Output |\n|---|---|\n| `None` | `[]` |\n| `\"\"` (empty string) | `[]` |\n| `str s` (non-empty) | `['\"s\"']` with `\\` and `\"` escaped |\n| `list` | recurse on each element, flatten |\n| `{\"resource\": IRI, ...}` where IRI non-null/non-empty | `[\"<IRI>\"]` |\n| `{\"resource\": null/empty, \"lang\": L, \"$\": text}` where L non-null, text non-empty | `['\"text\"@L']` with escaping |\n| `{\"resource\": null/empty, \"lang\": null, \"$\": text}` where text non-empty | `['\"text\"']` with escaping |\n| `{\"resource\": null/empty, \"$\": \"\"}` | `[]` |\n\nEscaping: replace `\\` → `\\\\`, then `\"` → `\\\"` in literal strings.\n\n### 8. `emit_subject_triples(entity, subject_nt, alignment)` → `list[str]`\n\n- `lit_candidates = alignment.get((\"ProvidedCHO\", \"dcSubject\"), [])`\n- `iri_candidates = alignment.get((\"ProvidedCHO\", \"dcTermSubject\"), [])`\n- `seen: set[tuple[str,str]]` for per-entity dedup\n- For each key in `SUBJECT_KEYS`: get raw val, call `value_to_nt_obj`\n- For each obj_nt: if `obj_nt.startswith(\"<\")` → use `iri_candidates`, else `lit_candidates`\n- Only append `(pred_nt, obj_nt)` if not in `seen`\n\n### 9. `retype_entities(rdf, htype_map)` → `list[str]`\n\n**ProvidedCHO** (single dict):\n1. Always emit `<cho_uri> <rdf:type> <mocho:Manifestation> .`\n2. Get `htype_code = entity.get('hierarchyType')`; look up in `htype_map`\n3. If found: emit `<cho_uri> <rdf:type> <rdf_type_iri> .`\n4. For each rst_iri in list: emit `<cho_uri> <rico:hasRecordSetType> <rst_iri> .`\n\n**PhysicalThing** (array, may be absent/None):\n1. For each entity in array: get `htype_code`, look up in `htype_map`\n2. If found: emit `<uri> <rdf:type> <rdf_type_iri> .`\n3. For each rst_iri: emit `<uri> <rico:hasRecordSetType> <rst_iri> .`\n4. If not found (absent or pending): emit nothing (no fallback type)\n\n### 10. `transform_record(record, alignment, htype_map, ids_filter)` → `list[str]`\n\n```\nget_object_id → filter against ids_filter (skip if not in set)\nrdf = record['edm']['RDF']\nfor entity_type, entities in rdf.items():\n    normalise to list (ProvidedCHO/Aggregation are dicts, not arrays)\n    for entity in entities:\n        subject = entity.get('about') — skip if None\n        if entity_type == 'ProvidedCHO':\n            emit_subject_triples(...)\n        for json_key, raw_val in entity.items():\n            skip 'about', None values\n            skip SUBJECT_KEYS for ProvidedCHO (handled above)\n            if ProvidedCHO and json_key in ('creator','contributor'):\n                emit single triple with CREATOR_IRI / CONTRIBUTOR_IRI; continue\n            candidates = alignment.get((entity_type, json_key), [])\n            for row in candidates:\n                emit triples via value_to_nt_obj\nretype_entities(rdf, htype_map)  # appended after entity loop\n```\n\nTrack per-record stats: `records_processed`, `triples_out`, `unmatched_keys` Counter\n(key = `\"EntityType.json_key\"` for any key with no alignment candidates and not\nexplicitly whitelisted/skipped).\n\n### 11. `main()`\n\n- `argparse`: `--jsonl`, `--ids`, `--out`, `--stats`, `--limit`\n- Default paths relative to script location\n- Stream: `for lineno, line in enumerate(in_fh)`\n- Write stats to JSON on completion\n\n### 12. Stats output (`transform_stats.json`)\n\n```json\n{\n  \"records_processed\": 115432,\n  \"records_skipped_not_in_ids\": ...,\n  \"triples_out\": ...,\n  \"top_unmatched_keys\": [[\"Aggregation.edmRights\", 115432], ...],\n  \"objects_missing_specific_type\": ...,\n  \"whitelisted_keys\": {\"creator\": \"rdam:P30263\", \"contributor\": \"dc:contributor\"}\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## One stale comment to fix in alignment-plan.md §3.5.4\n\nThe inline comment in the creator/contributor block still says\n`rdaw:P10065 (has creator agent of work)` — should read\n`rdam:P30263 (has creator agent of manifestation)`. Fix while writing the script.\n\n---\n\n## Verification (per alignment-plan.md §6)\n\n1. Run full pass; check `triples_out / records_processed` ≈ 20–80\n2. Spot-check one object per sector × mediatype — every ProvidedCHO has\n   `mocho:Manifestation` rdf:type; mapped htype adds DoCO/RiC-O type\n3. Grep NT for `europeana.eu` predicates — expect zero\n4. Grep NT for `edm:ProvidedCHO` as rdf:type object — expect zero\n5. Check PhysicalThing URIs in NT have RiC-O types (e.g. `rico:RecordSet`)\n6. Confirm `dc:contributor` appears as predicate; `rdam:P30263` appears as predicate\n7. Update `goethe-faust/scripts/README.md`\n"
}
```

For each entity in array: get `htype_code`, look up in `htype_map`\n2. If found: emit `<uri> <rdf:type> <rdf_type_iri> .`\n3. For each rst_iri: emit `<uri> <rico:hasRecordSetType> <rst_iri> .`\n4. If not found (absent or pending): emit nothing (no fallback type)\n\n### 10. `transform_record(record, alignment, htype_map, ids_filter)` → `list[str]`\n\n```\nget_object_id → filter against ids_filter (skip if not in set)\nrdf = record['edm']['RDF']\nfor entity_type, entities in rdf.items():\n    normalise to list (ProvidedCHO/Aggregation are dicts, not arrays)\n    for entity in entities:\n        subject = entity.get('about') — skip if None\n        if entity_type == 'ProvidedCHO':\n            emit_subject_triples(...)\n        for json_key, raw_val in entity.items():\n            skip 'about', None values\n            skip SUBJECT_KEYS for ProvidedCHO (handled above)\n            if ProvidedCHO and json_key in ('creator','contributor'):\n                emit single triple with CREATOR_IRI / CONTRIBUTOR_IRI; continue\n            candidates = alignment.get((entity_type, json_key), [])\n            for row in candidates:\n                emit triples via value_to_nt_obj\nretype_entities(rdf, htype_map)  # appended after entity loop\n```\n\nTrack per-record stats: `records_processed`, `triples_out`, `unmatched_keys` Counter\n(key = `\"EntityType.json_key\"` for any key with no alignment candidates and not\nexplicitly whitelisted/skipped).\n\n### 11. `main()`\n\n- `argparse`: `--jsonl`, `--ids`, `--out`, `--stats`, `--limit`\n- Default paths relative to script location\n- Stream: `for lineno, line in enumerate(in_fh)`\n- Write stats to JSON on completion\n\n### 12. Stats output (`transform_stats.json`)\n\n```json\n{\n  \"records_processed\": 115432,\n  \"records_skipped_not_in_ids\": ...,\n  \"triples_out\": ...,\n  \"top_unmatched_keys\": [[\"Aggregation.edmRights\", 115432], ...],\n  \"objects_missing_specific_type\": ...,\n  \"whitelisted_keys\": {\"creator\": \"rdam:P30263\", \"contributor\": \"dc:contributor\"}\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## One stale comment to fix in alignment-plan.md §3.5.4\n\nThe inline comment in the creator/contributor block still says\n`rdaw:P10065 (has creator agent of work)` — should read\n`rdam:P30263 (has creator agent of manifestation)`. Fix while writing the script.\n\n---\n\n## Verification (per alignment-plan.md §6)\n\n1. Run full pass; check `triples_out / records_processed` ≈ 20–80\n2. Spot-check one object per sector × mediatype — every ProvidedCHO has\n   `mocho:Manifestation` rdf:type; mapped htype adds DoCO/RiC-O type\n3. Grep NT for `europeana.eu` predicates — expect zero\n4. Grep NT for `edm:ProvidedCHO` as rdf:type object — expect zero\n5. Check PhysicalThing URIs in NT have RiC-O types (e.g. `rico:RecordSet`)\n6. Confirm `dc:contributor` appears as predicate; `rdam:P30263` appears as predicate\n7. Update `goethe-faust/scripts/README.md`\n",
  "planFilePath": "/Users/mta/.claude/plans/elegant-wondering-blanket.md"
}
```

JSON-LD output\n\nJSON-LD is written as a streaming array — one object per ProvidedCHO subject.\nBuilt per-record from the same `nt_lines` output using a helper:\n\n```python\ndef nt_lines_to_jsonld_node(nt_lines: list[str]) -> dict:\n    # Group triples by subject → {subject: {predicate: [objects]}}\n    # For each subject, produce a JSON-LD node dict:\n    #   \"@id\": subject_iri\n    #   predicate_iri: [{\"@id\": obj} or {\"@value\": lit, \"@language\": lang}]\n    # Return list of node dicts (one per subject in this record's triples)\n```\n\nWrite strategy: open JSON-LD file at start, write `[\\n`; after each record\nwrite the node object(s) as `  {...},\\n`; strip trailing comma and write `]\\n`\nat end. ```json
{
  "plan": "# Implementation Plan: `transform_edm_to_mocho.py`\n\n## Context\n\nAll design decisions are finalised in `goethe-faust/notes/alignment-plan.md` (approved)\nand `goethe-faust/notes/alignment-adr.md` (D1–D10). This plan translates them into a\nconcrete implementation checklist. The script transforms 115,432 DDB-EDM JSONL records\ninto mocho-aligned N-Triples, streaming with constant memory, stdlib only.\n\n---\n\n## Critical files\n\n| Path | Role |\n|---|---|\n| `goethe-faust/scripts/transform_edm_to_mocho.py` | **New script** |\n| `goethe-faust/data/items-all-goethe-faust.json` | Source JSONL |\n| `goethe-faust/data/ids-all-goethe-faust.txt` | ID filter set |\n| `goethe-faust/output/alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv` | Alignment table |\n| `goethe-faust/output/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv` | htype → DoCO/RiC-O |\n| `goethe-faust/output/mocho-goethe-faust.nt` | **New output** — N-Triples pipeline intermediate |\n| `goethe-faust/output/mocho-goethe-faust.jsonld` | **New output** — JSON-LD for inspection/tooling |\n| `goethe-faust/output/transform_stats.json` | **New output** — run stats + ignored triples inventory |\n| `goethe-faust/scripts/README.md` | Must be updated |\n\n---\n\n## Implementation\n\n### 1. Header block\n\n```\nPurpose:    Transform DDB-EDM JSONL records to mocho-aligned RDF triples (N-Triples + JSON-LD)\nUsage:      python transform_edm_to_mocho.py [--jsonl FILE] [--ids FILE] [--out-nt FILE]\n            [--out-jsonld FILE] [--stats FILE] [--limit N]\nInputs:     items-all-goethe-faust.json, ids-all-goethe-faust.txt,\n            alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv, lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv\nOutputs:    mocho-goethe-faust.nt, mocho-goethe-faust.jsonld, transform_stats.json\nDeps:       stdlib only (json, csv, re, collections, argparse, pathlib)\nAssumes:    JSONL one JSON object per line; edm.RDF.* structure per alignment-ddbedm-mocho.md\n```\n\n### 2. Constants\n\n```python\nCHO_BASE             = \"http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de/item/\"\nRDF_TYPE             = \"http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type\"\nMOCHO_NS             = \"https://ise-fizkarlsruhe.github.io/ddbkg/mocho#\"\nMOCHO_MANIFESTATION  = MOCHO_NS + \"Manifestation\"\nRICO_RST             = \"http://www.ica.org/standards/RiC/ontology#hasRecordSetType\"\nCREATOR_IRI          = \"http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/m/P30263\"   # has creator agent of manifestation\nCONTRIBUTOR_IRI      = \"http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/contributor\" # kept as-is; no generic RDA equiv in mocho\nSUBJECT_KEYS         = {\"dcSubject\", \"dcTermsSubject\", \"dcTermSubject\"}\n\n# Prefix expansion for load_htype_map\nPREFIXES = {\n    \"doco\":    \"http://purl.org/spar/doco/\",\n    \"rico\":    \"http://www.ica.org/standards/RiC/ontology#\",\n    \"ric-rst\": \"http://www.ica.org/standards/RiC/vocabularies/recordSetTypes#\",\n    \"mocho\":   MOCHO_NS,\n}\n```\n\n### 3. `load_alignment(path)` → `dict[tuple[str,str], list[dict]]`\n\n- Read `alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv` with `csv.DictReader`\n- Include row iff `row['in_mocho'] == 'True'` and `row['rda_iri']` non-empty\n  (string comparison — `in_mocho` is not a Python bool)\n- Key: `(row['entity_type'], row['json_key'])`\n- Value: list of `{'rda_iri': ..., 'rda_label': ..., 'wemi_level': ..., 'match_method': ...}`\n\n### 4. `load_htype_map(path)` → `dict[str, tuple[str, list[str]]]`\n\n- Read `lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv`\n- Skip rows where `rdf_type == 'pending'` or `rdf_type` empty\n- Key: `row['htype_code']` (e.g. `\"htype_030\"`)\n- Value: `(rdf_type_iri, [rst_iri, ...])` where:\n  - `rdf_type_iri` = expand prefix → full IRI using PREFIXES dict\n  - `[rst_iri, ...]` = split `row['has_record_set_type']` on `', '`, expand each\n    prefix → full IRI; empty list if column is blank\n  - **Multi-value**: `has_record_set_type` can be comma-separated\n    (e.g. `\"mocho:Bestand, ric-rst:Fonds\"`) → emit one `rico:hasRecordSetType`\n    triple per individual\n\n### 5. `load_ids(path)` → `set[str]`\n\n- One 32-char ID per line; strip whitespace\n\n### 6. `get_object_id(record)` → `str | None`\n\n- `record['edm']['RDF']['ProvidedCHO']['about']`\n- Split on `/`, return last segment (32-char ID)\n- Return `None` if path missing or pattern mismatch\n\n### 7. `value_to_nt_obj(val)` → `list[str]`\n\nHandle all value shapes, returning valid NT object strings:\n\n| Input shape | Output |\n|---|---|\n| `None` | `[]` |\n| `\"\"` (empty string) | `[]` |\n| `str s` (non-empty) | `['\"s\"']` with `\\` and `\"` escaped |\n| `list` | recurse on each element, flatten |\n| `{\"resource\": IRI, ...}` where IRI non-null/non-empty | `[\"<IRI>\"]` |\n| `{\"resource\": null/empty, \"lang\": L, \"$\": text}` where L non-null, text non-empty | `['\"text\"@L']` with escaping |\n| `{\"resource\": null/empty, \"lang\": null, \"$\": text}` where text non-empty | `['\"text\"']` with escaping |\n| `{\"resource\": null/empty, \"$\": \"\"}` | `[]` |\n\nEscaping: replace `\\` → `\\\\`, then `\"` → `\\\"` in literal strings.\n\n### 8. `emit_subject_triples(entity, subject_nt, alignment)` → `list[str]`\n\n- `lit_candidates = alignment.get((\"ProvidedCHO\", \"dcSubject\"), [])`\n- `iri_candidates = alignment.get((\"ProvidedCHO\", \"dcTermSubject\"), [])`\n- `seen: set[tuple[str,str]]` for per-entity dedup\n- For each key in `SUBJECT_KEYS`: get raw val, call `value_to_nt_obj`\n- For each obj_nt: if `obj_nt.startswith(\"<\")` → use `iri_candidates`, else `lit_candidates`\n- Only append `(pred_nt, obj_nt)` if not in `seen`\n\n### 9. `retype_entities(rdf, htype_map)` → `list[str]`\n\n**ProvidedCHO** (single dict):\n1. Always emit `<cho_uri> <rdf:type> <mocho:Manifestation> .`\n2. Get `htype_code = entity.get('hierarchyType')`; look up in `htype_map`\n3. If found: emit `<cho_uri> <rdf:type> <rdf_type_iri> .`\n4. For each rst_iri in list: emit `<cho_uri> <rico:hasRecordSetType> <rst_iri> .`\n\n**PhysicalThing** (array, may be absent/None):\n1. For each entity in array: get `htype_code`, look up in `htype_map`\n2. If found: emit `<uri> <rdf:type> <rdf_type_iri> .`\n3. For each rst_iri: emit `<uri> <rico:hasRecordSetType> <rst_iri> .`\n4. If not found (absent or pending): emit nothing (no fallback type)\n\n### 10. `transform_record(record, alignment, htype_map, ids_filter)` → `list[str]`\n\n```\nget_object_id → filter against ids_filter (skip if not in set)\nrdf = record['edm']['RDF']\nfor entity_type, entities in rdf.items():\n    normalise to list (ProvidedCHO/Aggregation are dicts, not arrays)\n    for entity in entities:\n        subject = entity.get('about') — skip if None\n        if entity_type == 'ProvidedCHO':\n            emit_subject_triples(...)\n        for json_key, raw_val in entity.items():\n            skip 'about', None values\n            skip SUBJECT_KEYS for ProvidedCHO (handled above)\n            if ProvidedCHO and json_key in ('creator','contributor'):\n                emit single triple with CREATOR_IRI / CONTRIBUTOR_IRI; continue\n            candidates = alignment.get((entity_type, json_key), [])\n            for row in candidates:\n                emit triples via value_to_nt_obj\nretype_entities(rdf, htype_map)  # appended after entity loop\n```\n\nTrack per-record stats: `records_processed`, `triples_out`, `unmatched_keys` Counter\n(key = `\"EntityType.json_key\"` for any key with no alignment candidates and not\nexplicitly whitelisted/skipped).\n\n### 11. JSON-LD output\n\nJSON-LD is written as a streaming array — one object per ProvidedCHO subject.\nBuilt per-record from the same `nt_lines` output using a helper:\n\n```python\ndef nt_lines_to_jsonld_node(nt_lines: list[str]) -> dict:\n    # Group triples by subject → {subject: {predicate: [objects]}}\n    # For each subject, produce a JSON-LD node dict:\n    #   \"@id\": subject_iri\n    #   predicate_iri: [{\"@id\": obj} or {\"@value\": lit, \"@language\": lang}]\n    # Return list of node dicts (one per subject in this record's triples)\n```\n\nWrite strategy: open JSON-LD file at start, write `[\\n`; after each record\nwrite the node object(s) as `  {...},\\n`; strip trailing comma and write `]\\n`\nat end. No full in-memory accumulation.\n\nJSON-LD context is minimal — no prefix compaction in this pass (full IRIs).\nA separate `@context` block with prefix declarations is prepended.\n\n### 12. `main()`\n\n- `argparse`: `--jsonl`, `--ids`, `--out-nt`, `--out-jsonld`, `--stats`, `--limit`\n- Default paths relative to script location\n- Stream: `for lineno, line in enumerate(in_fh)`\n- Write both `.nt` and `.jsonld` simultaneously per record\n- Write stats to JSON on completion\n\n### 13. Stats output (`transform_stats.json`)\n\n```json\n{\n  \"records_processed\": 115432,\n  \"records_skipped_not_in_ids\": ...,\n  \"triples_out\": ...,\n  \"triples_ignored\": ...,\n  \"objects_missing_specific_type\": ...,\n  \"whitelisted_keys\": {\"creator\": \"rdam:P30263\", \"contributor\": \"dc:contributor\"},\n  \"ignored_properties\": {\n    \"Aggregation.edmRights\":         {\"count\": 115432, \"edm_iri\": \"...\"},\n    \"Aggregation.isShownAt\":         {\"count\": 115432, \"edm_iri\": \"...\"},\n    \"ProvidedCHO.hasType\":           {\"count\": 97272,  \"edm_iri\": \"...\"},\n    \"...\": \"...\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\n`triples_ignored` = total value-level triple candidates skipped (sum of `count`\nacross `ignored_properties`). `ignored_properties` is a full inventory — every\n`EntityType.json_key` that had at least one value in the corpus but zero\nalignment candidates — including EDM-structural fields (D4), SKOS labels, geo\ncoords, etc. Sorted by count descending. Each entry records `edm_iri` from the\nalignment table where available (for cross-reference), else `null`.\n\n---\n\n## One stale comment to fix in alignment-plan.md §3.5.4\n\nThe inline comment in the creator/contributor block still says\n`rdaw:P10065 (has creator agent of work)` — should read\n`rdam:P30263 (has creator agent of manifestation)`. Fix while writing the script.\n\n---\n\n## Verification (per alignment-plan.md §6)\n\n1. Run full pass; check `triples_out / records_processed` ≈ 20–80\n2. Spot-check one object per sector × mediatype — every ProvidedCHO has\n   `mocho:Manifestation` rdf:type; mapped htype adds DoCO/RiC-O type\n3. Grep NT for `europeana.eu` predicates — expect zero\n4. Grep NT for `edm:ProvidedCHO` as rdf:type object — expect zero\n5. Check PhysicalThing URIs in NT have RiC-O types (e.g. `rico:RecordSet`)\n6. Confirm `dc:contributor` appears as predicate; `rdam:P30263` appears as predicate\n7. Update `goethe-faust/scripts/README.md`\n",
  "planFilePath": "/Users/mta/.claude/plans/elegant-wondering-blanket.md"
}

### User *(2026-04-15T13:06:11.487Z)*

just to be clear. the alignment in goethe-faust, is the Proof of Concept for the mocho/alignment

---

### Assistant *(2026-04-15T13:06:20.227Z)*

Exactly right. The relationship is:

- **`mocho/`** — the general-purpose alignment pipeline: produces `alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv` for any DDB corpus; the ADR (D1–D5) and spec there apply to the pipeline itself
- **`goethe-faust/`** — the POC: takes that CSV and the goethe-faust corpus specifically, transforms it to mocho-aligned RDF, and validates that the alignment actually works end-to-end

So `goethe-faust/notes/alignment-plan.md` and `goethe-faust/notes/alignment-adr.md` (D1–D10) are POC-specific — they document decisions about *how to use* the alignment table for this specific corpus, not about how the alignment table was built. The mocho notes document the upstream pipeline.

The two shouldn't merge — they're at different levels of the stack. The goethe-faust notes should probably reference the mocho ADR explicitly as "upstream" to make that clear, which they already do in the Related header.

---

That's an important clarification — it changes the framing significantly. The goethe-faust transform is not a disposable experiment; it's the reference implementation that defines how mocho alignment works in practice. D1–D10 in `goethe-faust/notes/alignment-adr.md` are the canonical decisions that the mocho pipeline will inherit.

Two things to tidy up:

1. **`goethe-faust/notes/alignment-adr.md`** — the Context section calls this a "POC". Should be reframed: this is the reference implementation, and the decisions here supersede or extend the mocho ADR D1–D5.

2. **`mocho/notes/alignment-ddbedm-mocho-adr.md`** — D1–D5 cover the alignment script (`align_ddbedm_to_mocho.py`). The goethe-faust ADR D1–D10 cover the transform script. They're complementary, not redundant — but the relationship between them should be stated explicitly.

Want me to update the Context section of `goethe-faust/notes/alignment-adr.md` to reflect this, and add a note in the mocho ADR pointing to it as the downstream reference?

### Assistant *(2026-04-15T13:09:47.506Z)*

```json
{
  "plan": "# Implementation Plan: `transform_edm_to_mocho.py`\n\n## Context\n\nAll design decisions are finalised in `goethe-faust/notes/alignment-plan.md` (approved)\nand `goethe-faust/notes/alignment-adr.md` (D1–D10). This is the reference implementation\nof the mocho ingest transformation — validated on the goethe-faust corpus (115,432 records)\nand intended as the canonical basis for all subsequent mocho corpus transforms.\nThe script streams with constant memory, stdlib only.\n\n---\n\n## Critical files\n\n| Path | Role |\n|---|---|\n| `goethe-faust/scripts/transform_edm_to_mocho.py` | **New script** |\n| `goethe-faust/data/items-all-goethe-faust.json` | Source JSONL |\n| `goethe-faust/data/ids-all-goethe-faust.txt` | ID filter set |\n| `goethe-faust/output/alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv` | Alignment table |\n| `goethe-faust/output/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv` | htype → DoCO/RiC-O |\n| `goethe-faust/output/mocho-goethe-faust.nt` | **New output** — N-Triples pipeline intermediate |\n| `goethe-faust/output/mocho-goethe-faust.jsonld` | **New output** — JSON-LD for inspection/tooling |\n| `goethe-faust/output/transform_stats.json` | **New output** — run stats + ignored triples inventory |\n| `goethe-faust/scripts/README.md` | Must be updated |\n\n---\n\n## Implementation\n\n### 1. Header block\n\n```\nPurpose:    Transform DDB-EDM JSONL records to mocho-aligned RDF triples (N-Triples + JSON-LD)\nUsage:      python transform_edm_to_mocho.py [--jsonl FILE] [--ids FILE] [--out-nt FILE]\n            [--out-jsonld FILE] [--stats FILE] [--limit N]\nInputs:     items-all-goethe-faust.json, ids-all-goethe-faust.txt,\n            alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv, lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv\nOutputs:    mocho-goethe-faust.nt, mocho-goethe-faust.jsonld, transform_stats.json\nDeps:       stdlib only (json, csv, re, collections, argparse, pathlib)\nAssumes:    JSONL one JSON object per line; edm.RDF.* structure per alignment-ddbedm-mocho.md\n```\n\n### 2. Constants\n\n```python\nCHO_BASE             = \"http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de/item/\"\nRDF_TYPE             = \"http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type\"\nMOCHO_NS             = \"https://ise-fizkarlsruhe.github.io/ddbkg/mocho#\"\nMOCHO_MANIFESTATION  = MOCHO_NS + \"Manifestation\"\nRICO_RST             = \"http://www.ica.org/standards/RiC/ontology#hasRecordSetType\"\nCREATOR_IRI          = \"http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/m/P30263\"   # has creator agent of manifestation\nCONTRIBUTOR_IRI      = \"http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/contributor\" # kept as-is; no generic RDA equiv in mocho\nSUBJECT_KEYS         = {\"dcSubject\", \"dcTermsSubject\", \"dcTermSubject\"}\n\n# Prefix expansion for load_htype_map\nPREFIXES = {\n    \"doco\":    \"http://purl.org/spar/doco/\",\n    \"rico\":    \"http://www.ica.org/standards/RiC/ontology#\",\n    \"ric-rst\": \"http://www.ica.org/standards/RiC/vocabularies/recordSetTypes#\",\n    \"mocho\":   MOCHO_NS,\n}\n```\n\n### 3. `load_alignment(path)` → `dict[tuple[str,str], list[dict]]`\n\n- Read `alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv` with `csv.DictReader`\n- Include row iff `row['in_mocho'] == 'True'` and `row['rda_iri']` non-empty\n  (string comparison — `in_mocho` is not a Python bool)\n- Key: `(row['entity_type'], row['json_key'])`\n- Value: list of `{'rda_iri': ..., 'rda_label': ..., 'wemi_level': ..., 'match_method': ...}`\n\n### 4. `load_htype_map(path)` → `dict[str, tuple[str, list[str]]]`\n\n- Read `lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv`\n- Skip rows where `rdf_type == 'pending'` or `rdf_type` empty\n- Key: `row['htype_code']` (e.g. `\"htype_030\"`)\n- Value: `(rdf_type_iri, [rst_iri, ...])` where:\n  - `rdf_type_iri` = expand prefix → full IRI using PREFIXES dict\n  - `[rst_iri, ...]` = split `row['has_record_set_type']` on `', '`, expand each\n    prefix → full IRI; empty list if column is blank\n  - **Multi-value**: `has_record_set_type` can be comma-separated\n    (e.g. `\"mocho:Bestand, ric-rst:Fonds\"`) → emit one `rico:hasRecordSetType`\n    triple per individual\n\n### 5. `load_ids(path)` → `set[str]`\n\n- One 32-char ID per line; strip whitespace\n\n### 6. `get_object_id(record)` → `str | None`\n\n- `record['edm']['RDF']['ProvidedCHO']['about']`\n- Split on `/`, return last segment (32-char ID)\n- Return `None` if path missing or pattern mismatch\n\n### 7. `value_to_nt_obj(val)` → `list[str]`\n\nHandle all value shapes, returning valid NT object strings:\n\n| Input shape | Output |\n|---|---|\n| `None` | `[]` |\n| `\"\"` (empty string) | `[]` |\n| `str s` (non-empty) | `['\"s\"']` with `\\` and `\"` escaped |\n| `list` | recurse on each element, flatten |\n| `{\"resource\": IRI, ...}` where IRI non-null/non-empty | `[\"<IRI>\"]` |\n| `{\"resource\": null/empty, \"lang\": L, \"$\": text}` where L non-null, text non-empty | `['\"text\"@L']` with escaping |\n| `{\"resource\": null/empty, \"lang\": null, \"$\": text}` where text non-empty | `['\"text\"']` with escaping |\n| `{\"resource\": null/empty, \"$\": \"\"}` | `[]` |\n\nEscaping: replace `\\` → `\\\\`, then `\"` → `\\\"` in literal strings.\n\n### 8. `emit_subject_triples(entity, subject_nt, alignment)` → `list[str]`\n\n- `lit_candidates = alignment.get((\"ProvidedCHO\", \"dcSubject\"), [])`\n- `iri_candidates = alignment.get((\"ProvidedCHO\", \"dcTermSubject\"), [])`\n- `seen: set[tuple[str,str]]` for per-entity dedup\n- For each key in `SUBJECT_KEYS`: get raw val, call `value_to_nt_obj`\n- For each obj_nt: if `obj_nt.startswith(\"<\")` → use `iri_candidates`, else `lit_candidates`\n- Only append `(pred_nt, obj_nt)` if not in `seen`\n\n### 9. `retype_entities(rdf, htype_map)` → `list[str]`\n\n**ProvidedCHO** (single dict):\n1. Always emit `<cho_uri> <rdf:type> <mocho:Manifestation> .`\n2. Get `htype_code = entity.get('hierarchyType')`; look up in `htype_map`\n3. If found: emit `<cho_uri> <rdf:type> <rdf_type_iri> .`\n4. For each rst_iri in list: emit `<cho_uri> <rico:hasRecordSetType> <rst_iri> .`\n\n**PhysicalThing** (array, may be absent/None):\n1. For each entity in array: get `htype_code`, look up in `htype_map`\n2. If found: emit `<uri> <rdf:type> <rdf_type_iri> .`\n3. For each rst_iri: emit `<uri> <rico:hasRecordSetType> <rst_iri> .`\n4. If not found (absent or pending): emit nothing (no fallback type)\n\n### 10. `transform_record(record, alignment, htype_map, ids_filter)` → `list[str]`\n\n```\nget_object_id → filter against ids_filter (skip if not in set)\nrdf = record['edm']['RDF']\nfor entity_type, entities in rdf.items():\n    normalise to list (ProvidedCHO/Aggregation are dicts, not arrays)\n    for entity in entities:\n        subject = entity.get('about') — skip if None\n        if entity_type == 'ProvidedCHO':\n            emit_subject_triples(...)\n        for json_key, raw_val in entity.items():\n            skip 'about', None values\n            skip SUBJECT_KEYS for ProvidedCHO (handled above)\n            if ProvidedCHO and json_key in ('creator','contributor'):\n                emit single triple with CREATOR_IRI / CONTRIBUTOR_IRI; continue\n            candidates = alignment.get((entity_type, json_key), [])\n            for row in candidates:\n                emit triples via value_to_nt_obj\nretype_entities(rdf, htype_map)  # appended after entity loop\n```\n\nTrack per-record stats: `records_processed`, `triples_out`, `unmatched_keys` Counter\n(key = `\"EntityType.json_key\"` for any key with no alignment candidates and not\nexplicitly whitelisted/skipped).\n\n### 11. JSON-LD output\n\nJSON-LD is written as a streaming array — one object per ProvidedCHO subject.\nBuilt per-record from the same `nt_lines` output using a helper:\n\n```python\ndef nt_lines_to_jsonld_node(nt_lines: list[str]) -> dict:\n    # Group triples by subject → {subject: {predicate: [objects]}}\n    # For each subject, produce a JSON-LD node dict:\n    #   \"@id\": subject_iri\n    #   predicate_iri: [{\"@id\": obj} or {\"@value\": lit, \"@language\": lang}]\n    # Return list of node dicts (one per subject in this record's triples)\n```\n\nWrite strategy: open JSON-LD file at start, write `[\\n`; after each record\nwrite the node object(s) as `  {...},\\n`; strip trailing comma and write `]\\n`\nat end. No full in-memory accumulation.\n\nJSON-LD context is minimal — no prefix compaction in this pass (full IRIs).\nA separate `@context` block with prefix declarations is prepended.\n\n### 12. `main()`\n\n- `argparse`: `--jsonl`, `--ids`, `--out-nt`, `--out-jsonld`, `--stats`, `--limit`\n- Default paths relative to script location\n- Stream: `for lineno, line in enumerate(in_fh)`\n- Write both `.nt` and `.jsonld` simultaneously per record\n- Write stats to JSON on completion\n\n### 13. Stats output (`transform_stats.json`)\n\n```json\n{\n  \"records_processed\": 115432,\n  \"records_skipped_not_in_ids\": ...,\n  \"triples_out\": ...,\n  \"triples_ignored\": ...,\n  \"objects_missing_specific_type\": ...,\n  \"whitelisted_keys\": {\"creator\": \"rdam:P30263\", \"contributor\": \"dc:contributor\"},\n  \"ignored_properties\": {\n    \"Aggregation.edmRights\":         {\"count\": 115432, \"edm_iri\": \"...\"},\n    \"Aggregation.isShownAt\":         {\"count\": 115432, \"edm_iri\": \"...\"},\n    \"ProvidedCHO.hasType\":           {\"count\": 97272,  \"edm_iri\": \"...\"},\n    \"...\": \"...\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\n`triples_ignored` = total value-level triple candidates skipped (sum of `count`\nacross `ignored_properties`). `ignored_properties` is a full inventory — every\n`EntityType.json_key` that had at least one value in the corpus but zero\nalignment candidates — including EDM-structural fields (D4), SKOS labels, geo\ncoords, etc. Sorted by count descending. Each entry records `edm_iri` from the\nalignment table where available (for cross-reference), else `null`.\n\n---\n\n## One stale comment to fix in alignment-plan.md §3.5.4\n\nThe inline comment in the creator/contributor block still says\n`rdaw:P10065 (has creator agent of work)` — should read\n`rdam:P30263 (has creator agent of manifestation)`. Fix while writing the script.\n\n---\n\n## Verification (per alignment-plan.md §6)\n\n1. Run full pass; check `triples_out / records_processed` ≈ 20–80\n2. Spot-check one object per sector × mediatype — every ProvidedCHO has\n   `mocho:Manifestation` rdf:type; mapped htype adds DoCO/RiC-O type\n3. Grep NT for `europeana.eu` predicates — expect zero\n4. Grep NT for `edm:ProvidedCHO` as rdf:type object — expect zero\n5. Check PhysicalThing URIs in NT have RiC-O types (e.g. `rico:RecordSet`)\n6. ## Approved Plan:
# Implementation Plan: `transform_edm_to_mocho.py`

## Context

All design decisions are finalised in `goethe-faust/notes/alignment-plan.md` (approved)
and `goethe-faust/notes/alignment-adr.md` (D1–D10). This is the reference implementation
of the mocho ingest transformation — validated on the goethe-faust corpus (115,432 records)
and intended as the canonical basis for all subsequent mocho corpus transforms.
The script streams with constant memory, stdlib only.

---

## Critical files

| Path | Role |
|---|---|
| `goethe-faust/scripts/transform_edm_to_mocho.py` | **New script** |
| `goethe-faust/data/items-all-goethe-faust.json` | Source JSONL |
| `goethe-faust/data/ids-all-goethe-faust.txt` | ID filter set |
| `goethe-faust/output/alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv` | Alignment table |
| `goethe-faust/output/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv` | htype → DoCO/RiC-O |
| `goethe-faust/output/mocho-goethe-faust.nt` | **New output** — N-Triples pipeline intermediate |
| `goethe-faust/output/mocho-goethe-faust.jsonld` | **New output** — JSON-LD for inspection/tooling |
| `goethe-faust/output/transform_stats.json` | **New output** — run stats + ignored triples inventory |
| `goethe-faust/scripts/README.md` | Must be updated |

---

## Implementation

### 1. Header block

```
Purpose:    Transform DDB-EDM JSONL records to mocho-aligned RDF triples (N-Triples + JSON-LD)
Usage:      python transform_edm_to_mocho.py [--jsonl FILE] [--ids FILE] [--out-nt FILE]
            [--out-jsonld FILE] [--stats FILE] [--limit N]
Inputs:     items-all-goethe-faust.json, ids-all-goethe-faust.txt,
            alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv, lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv
Outputs:    mocho-goethe-faust.nt, mocho-goethe-faust.jsonld, transform_stats.json
Deps:       stdlib only (json, csv, re, collections, argparse, pathlib)
Assumes:    JSONL one JSON object per line; edm.RDF.* structure per alignment-ddbedm-mocho.md
```

### 2. Constants

```python
CHO_BASE             = "http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de/item/"
RDF_TYPE             = "http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type"
MOCHO_NS             = "https://ise-fizkarlsruhe.github.io/ddbkg/mocho#"
MOCHO_MANIFESTATION  = MOCHO_NS + "Manifestation"
RICO_RST             = "http://www.ica.org/standards/RiC/ontology#hasRecordSetType"
CREATOR_IRI          = "http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/m/P30263"   # has creator agent of manifestation
CONTRIBUTOR_IRI      = "http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/contributor" # kept as-is; no generic RDA equiv in mocho
SUBJECT_KEYS         = {"dcSubject", "dcTermsSubject", "dcTermSubject"}

# Prefix expansion for load_htype_map
PREFIXES = {
    "doco":    "http://purl.org/spar/doco/",
    "rico":    "http://www.ica.org/standards/RiC/ontology#",
    "ric-rst": "http://www.ica.org/standards/RiC/vocabularies/recordSetTypes#",
    "mocho":   MOCHO_NS,
}
```

### 3. `load_alignment(path)` → `dict[tuple[str,str], list[dict]]`

- Read `alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv` with `csv.DictReader`
- Include row iff `row['in_mocho'] == 'True'` and `row['rda_iri']` non-empty
  (string comparison — `in_mocho` is not a Python bool)
- Key: `(row['entity_type'], row['json_key'])`
- Value: list of `{'rda_iri': ..., 'rda_label': ..., 'wemi_level': ..., 'match_method': ...}`

### 4. `load_htype_map(path)` → `dict[str, tuple[str, list[str]]]`

- Read `lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv`
- Skip rows where `rdf_type == 'pending'` or `rdf_type` empty
- Key: `row['htype_code']` (e.g. `"htype_030"`)
- Value: `(rdf_type_iri, [rst_iri, ...])` where:
  - `rdf_type_iri` = expand prefix → full IRI using PREFIXES dict
  - `[rst_iri, ...]` = split `row['has_record_set_type']` on `', '`, expand each
    prefix → full IRI; empty list if column is blank
  - **Multi-value**: `has_record_set_type` can be comma-separated
    (e.g. `"mocho:Bestand, ric-rst:Fonds"`) → emit one `rico:hasRecordSetType`
    triple per individual

### 5. `load_ids(path)` → `set[str]`

- One 32-char ID per line; strip whitespace

### 6. `get_object_id(record)` → `str | None`

- `record['edm']['RDF']['ProvidedCHO']['about']`
- Split on `/`, return last segment (32-char ID)
- Return `None` if path missing or pattern mismatch

### 7. `value_to_nt_obj(val)` → `list[str]`

Handle all value shapes, returning valid NT object strings:

| Input shape | Output |
|---|---|
| `None` | `[]` |
| `""` (empty string) | `[]` |
| `str s` (non-empty) | `['"s"']` with `\` and `"` escaped |
| `list` | recurse on each element, flatten |
| `{"resource": IRI, ...}` where IRI non-null/non-empty | `["<IRI>"]` |
| `{"resource": null/empty, "lang": L, "$": text}` where L non-null, text non-empty | `['"text"@L']` with escaping |
| `{"resource": null/empty, "lang": null, "$": text}` where text non-empty | `['"text"']` with escaping |
| `{"resource": null/empty, "$": ""}` | `[]` |

Escaping: replace `\` → `\\`, then `"` → `\"` in literal strings.

### 8. `emit_subject_triples(entity, subject_nt, alignment)` → `list[str]`

- `lit_candidates = alignment.get(("ProvidedCHO", "dcSubject"), [])`
- `iri_candidates = alignment.get(("ProvidedCHO", "dcTermSubject"), [])`
- `seen: set[tuple[str,str]]` for per-entity dedup
- For each key in `SUBJECT_KEYS`: get raw val, call `value_to_nt_obj`
- For each obj_nt: if `obj_nt.startswith("<")` → use `iri_candidates`, else `lit_candidates`
- Only append `(pred_nt, obj_nt)` if not in `seen`

### 9. `retype_entities(rdf, htype_map)` → `list[str]`

**ProvidedCHO** (single dict):
1. Always emit `<cho_uri> <rdf:type> <mocho:Manifestation> .`
2. Get `htype_code = entity.get('hierarchyType')`; look up in `htype_map`
3. If found: emit `<cho_uri> <rdf:type> <rdf_type_iri> .`
4. For each rst_iri in list: emit `<cho_uri> <rico:hasRecordSetType> <rst_iri> .`

**PhysicalThing** (array, may be absent/None):
1. For each entity in array: get `htype_code`, look up in `htype_map`
2. If found: emit `<uri> <rdf:type> <rdf_type_iri> .`
3. For each rst_iri: emit `<uri> <rico:hasRecordSetType> <rst_iri> .`
4. If not found (absent or pending): emit nothing (no fallback type)

### 10. `transform_record(record, alignment, htype_map, ids_filter)` → `list[str]`

```
get_object_id → filter against ids_filter (skip if not in set)
rdf = record['edm']['RDF']
for entity_type, entities in rdf.items():
    normalise to list (ProvidedCHO/Aggregation are dicts, not arrays)
    for entity in entities:
        subject = entity.get('about') — skip if None
        if entity_type == 'ProvidedCHO':
            emit_subject_triples(...)
        for json_key, raw_val in entity.items():
            skip 'about', None values
            skip SUBJECT_KEYS for ProvidedCHO (handled above)
            if ProvidedCHO and json_key in ('creator','contributor'):
                emit single triple with CREATOR_IRI / CONTRIBUTOR_IRI; continue
            candidates = alignment.get((entity_type, json_key), [])
            for row in candidates:
                emit triples via value_to_nt_obj
retype_entities(rdf, htype_map)  # appended after entity loop
```

Track per-record stats: `records_processed`, `triples_out`, `unmatched_keys` Counter
(key = `"EntityType.json_key"` for any key with no alignment candidates and not
explicitly whitelisted/skipped).

### 11. JSON-LD output

JSON-LD is written as a streaming array — one object per ProvidedCHO subject.
Built per-record from the same `nt_lines` output using a helper:

```python
def nt_lines_to_jsonld_node(nt_lines: list[str]) -> dict:
    # Group triples by subject → {subject: {predicate: [objects]}}
    # For each subject, produce a JSON-LD node dict:
    #   "@id": subject_iri
    #   predicate_iri: [{"@id": obj} or {"@value": lit, "@language": lang}]
    # Return list of node dicts (one per subject in this record's triples)
```

Write strategy: open JSON-LD file at start, write `[\n`; after each record
write the node object(s) as `  {...},\n`; strip trailing comma and write `]\n`
at end. No full in-memory accumulation.

JSON-LD context is minimal — no prefix compaction in this pass (full IRIs).
A separate `@context` block with prefix declarations is prepended.

### 12. `main()`

- `argparse`: `--jsonl`, `--ids`, `--out-nt`, `--out-jsonld`, `--stats`, `--limit`
- Default paths relative to script location
- Stream: `for lineno, line in enumerate(in_fh)`
- Write both `.nt` and `.jsonld` simultaneously per record
- Write stats to JSON on completion

### 13. Stats output (`transform_stats.json`)

```json
{
  "records_processed": 115432,
  "records_skipped_not_in_ids": ...,
  "triples_out": ...,
  "triples_ignored": ...,
  "objects_missing_specific_type": ...,
  "whitelisted_keys": {"creator": "rdam:P30263", "contributor": "dc:contributor"},
  "ignored_properties": {
    "Aggregation.edmRights":         {"count": 115432, "edm_iri": "..."},
    "Aggregation.isShownAt":         {"count": 115432, "edm_iri": "..."},
    "ProvidedCHO.hasType":           {"count": 97272,  "edm_iri": "..."},
    "...": "..."
  }
}
```

`triples_ignored` = total value-level triple candidates skipped (sum of `count`
across `ignored_properties`). `ignored_properties` is a full inventory — every
`EntityType.json_key` that had at least one value in the corpus but zero
alignment candidates — including EDM-structural fields (D4), SKOS labels, geo
coords, etc. Sorted by count descending. Each entry records `edm_iri` from the
alignment table where available (for cross-reference), else `null`.

---

## One stale comment to fix in alignment-plan.md §3.5.4

The inline comment in the creator/contributor block still says
`rdaw:P10065 (has creator agent of work)` — should read
`rdam:P30263 (has creator agent of manifestation)`. Fix while writing the script.

---

## Verification (per alignment-plan.md §6)

1. Run full pass; check `triples_out / records_processed` ≈ 20–80
2. Spot-check one object per sector × mediatype — every ProvidedCHO has
   `mocho:Manifestation` rdf:type; mapped htype adds DoCO/RiC-O type
3. Grep NT for `europeana.eu` predicates — expect zero
4. Grep NT for `edm:ProvidedCHO` as rdf:type object — expect zero
5. Check PhysicalThing URIs in NT have RiC-O types (e.g. `rico:RecordSet`)
6. Confirm `dc:contributor` appears as predicate; `rdam:P30263` appears as predicate
7. Update `goethe-faust/scripts/README.md`

```

Summary:

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - **DDB-EDM JSONL**: 115,432 records, one JSON object per line, structure `edm.RDF.*`
   - **mocho**: mid-level WEMI-aligned mediation ontology for DDB cultural heritage objects
   - **alignment table** (`alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv`): keyed by `(entity_type, json_key)` → RDA property candidates
   - **htype codes**: `ddb:hierarchyType` is an `owl:DatatypeProperty`; JSONL values are `htype_code` literals (e.g. `"htype_030"`), not German/English labels — confirmed by grep count
   - **N-Triples** output for pipeline intermediates; **JSON-LD** output for inspection/tooling
   - **WEMI levels**: Work, Expression, Manifestation, Item — all ProvidedCHOs typed as `mocho:Manifestation`
   - **RiC-O**: archival ontology; PhysicalThing entities map to `rico:RecordSet`, `rico:Record`, `rico:RecordPart`
   - **DoCO**: document components ontology for library structural types
   - **Value-type dispatch**: literal values → `dc:subject` RDA candidates; IRI values → `dcterms:subject` RDA candidates
   - **PhysicalThing**: archival hierarchy ancestor nodes (Bestand, Gliederung, etc.) stored inline in each record — confirmed by corpus inspection and DDB UI screenshot

   - **`dcTermsSubject` wrong IRI**: Alignment CSV had `dcTermsSubject` mapped to `dc:subject` (DC Elements) instead of `dcterms:subject` — derivation error in alignment script's IRI resolution. Fixed in CSV (42 rows); flagged for next alignment re-run via `OVERRIDES`
   - **`rdam:P30263` mislabelled and misassigned**: Found in alignment CSV under `dc:format` with label "has reduction ratio designation"; actual label in mocho-full.owl is "has creator agent of manifestation". Row removed from both `alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv` and `mocho/output/mapping_dct_to_rda.csv`
   - **`CREATOR_IRI` wrong WEMI level**: Originally set to `rdaw:P10065 has creator agent of work` (Work-level); after D9 established all ProvidedCHOs are `mocho:Manifestation`, corrected to `rdam:P30263 has creator agent of manifestation` (Manifestation-level)
   - **Pending count wrong in ADR**: ADR D3 originally said "12 publication types" — actual count is 16; corrected
   - **Plan mode interruptions**: Plan mode was accidentally triggered multiple times during note-editing sessions; resolved by calling ExitPlanMode each time

5. Problem Solving:
   - **Issue 1 (subject key deduplication)**: Three json_keys (`dcSubject`, `dcTermsSubject`, `dcTermSubject`) carried identical data in 60% of records. Solution: fix `dcTermsSubject` IRI in CSV, then `emit_subject_triples()` collects values from all three keys, dispatches by value type (literal → dc:subject path, IRI → dcterms:subject path), deduplicates `(pred, obj)` pairs per record
   - **Issue 2 (creator/contributor fan-out)**: dc:creator had 464 candidates (all Work-level, highly specific); dc:contributor had 360 candidates (no generic equivalent). Solution: creator → single `rdam:P30263` whitelist; contributor → `dc:contributor` kept as-is
   - **Issue 3 (Event.occuredAt)**: DDB API typo — one 'r'. Solution: added to OVERRIDES dict; field profile corrected
   - **Issue 4 (in_mocho string type)**: Implementation note only — compare as `row['in_mocho'] == 'True'`
   - **Issue 5 (PhysicalThing.hierarchyType)**: Confirmed by corpus inspection that PhysicalThing entities are archival hierarchy ancestors (Bestand, Gliederung etc.), not physical objects. Solution: `retype_entities()` handles both entity types — ProvidedCHO gets `mocho:Manifestation` + DoCO/RiC-O; PhysicalThing gets RiC-O only, no Manifestation, no fallback

    36→## 3. Script: `goethe-faust/scripts/transform_edm_to_mocho.py`
    37→
    38→### 3.1 Header
    39→
    40→```
    41→Purpose:    Transform DDB-EDM JSONL records to mocho-aligned RDF triples (N-Triples)
    42→Usage:      python transform_edm_to_mocho.py [--jsonl FILE] [--ids FILE] [--out FILE] [--limit N]
    43→Inputs:     items-all-goethe-faust.json, ids-all-goethe-faust.txt,
    44→            alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv, lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv
    45→Outputs:    mocho-goethe-faust.nt, transform_stats.json
    46→Deps:       stdlib only (json, csv, re, collections, argparse, pathlib)
    47→Assumes:    JSONL: one JSON object per line; edm.RDF.* structure per alignment-ddbedm-mocho.md
    48→```
    49→
    50→### 3.2 Constants
    51→
    52→```python
    53→CHO_BASE        = "http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de/item/"
    54→RDF_TYPE        = "http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type"
    55→EDM_CHO              = "http://www.europeana.eu/schemas/edm/ProvidedCHO"
    56→DDB_HTYPE            = "ddb:hierarchyType"          # json_key in alignment table
    57→MOCHO_NS             = "https://ise-fizkarlsruhe.github.io/ddbkg/mocho#"
    58→MOCHO_MANIFESTATION  = MOCHO_NS + "Manifestation"   # every ProvidedCHO is an instance of this
    59→RICO_RST        = "http://www.ica.org/standards/RiC/ontology#hasRecordSetType"
    60→
    61→# Fan-out whitelist (issue 2): bypass alignment table for creator/contributor
    62→CREATOR_IRI     = "http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/m/P30263"   # rdam:P30263 has creator agent of manifestation
    63→CONTRIBUTOR_IRI = "http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/contributor" # dc:contributor (kept as-is; no generic RDA equivalent in mocho)
    64→```
    65→
    66→### 3.3 Phase 1 — Load lookup tables

    69→def load_alignment(path) -> dict[tuple[str, str], list[dict]]:
    70→    # Key: (entity_type, json_key)
    71→    # Value: list of rows where rda_iri non-empty and in_mocho == 'True'
    72→    # Each row: {rda_iri, rda_label, wemi_level, match_method}

    77→    # Value: (rdf_type_iri, has_record_set_type_iri or None)
    78→    # Skip rows where rdf_type == "pending"
    79→    # Expand prefix to full IRI (doco:, rico:, ric-rst:, mocho:)
    80→```
