    85→def load_ids(path) -> set[str]:
    86→    # 32-char IDs, one per line; strip whitespace
    87→```
    88→
    89→### 3.5 Phase 3 — Stream JSONL and transform
    90→
    91→#### 3.5.1 Record structure (per `alignment-ddbedm-mocho.md`)
    92→
    93→```
    94→record.edm.RDF = {
    95→  "ProvidedCHO": {dict},          # single entity
    96→  "Aggregation": {dict},          # single entity
    97→  "Agent":       [{dict}, ...],   # array
    98→  "Concept":     [{dict}, ...],   # array
    99→  "WebResource": [{dict}, ...],   # array
   100→  "Event":       [{dict}, ...],   # may be absent
   101→  "Place":       [{dict}, ...],   # may be absent
   102→  "TimeSpan":    [{dict}, ...],   # may be absent
   103→  "PhysicalThing":[{dict}, ...],  # may be absent
   104→}
   105→each entity: {"about": "<subject_uri>", "<json_key>": <value>, ...}
   106→value types: null | str | list | {"resource": str|null, "lang": str|null, "$": str}
   107→```
   108→
   109→#### 3.5.2 Object ID resolution
   110→
   111→```python
   112→def get_object_id(record) -> str | None:
   113→    # Extract 32-char ID from ProvidedCHO.about URI
   114→    # about = "http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de/item/{id}"
   115→    # Return id portion; None if not matching pattern
   116→```
   117→
   118→#### 3.5.3 Field value → NT object
   119→
   120→```python
   121→def value_to_nt_obj(val) -> list[str]:
   122→    # Handles all value shapes:
   123→    #   null → []
   124→    #   str  → ['"str"']
   125→    #   list → flatten recursively
   126→    #   {"resource": IRI, ...}  → ["<IRI>"]  (if resource non-null)
   127→    #   {"lang": L, "$": text}  → ['"text"@L'] (if lang non-null, text non-empty)
   128→    #   {"lang": null, "$": text} → ['"text"']
   129→    # Skips nulls and empty strings
   130→    # Escapes backslash, double-quote in literals
   131→```
   132→
   133→#### 3.5.4 Core transform loop
   134→
   135→```python
   136→def transform_record(record, alignment, htype_map, ids_filter) -> list[str]:
   137→    # Returns list of NT lines for this record
   138→    obj_id = get_object_id(record)
   139→    if obj_id not in ids_filter:
   140→        return []
   141→
   142→    nt_lines = []
   143→    rdf = record["edm"]["RDF"]
   144→
   145→    for entity_type, entities in rdf.items():
   146→        if entities is None:
   147→            continue
   148→        if isinstance(entities, dict):
   149→            entities = [entities]
   150→        for entity in entities:
   151→            subject = entity.get("about")
   152→            if not subject:
   153→                continue
   154→            subject_nt = f"<{subject}>"
   155→
   156→            # Subject fields: value-type dispatch + cross-key deduplication
   157→            # dcSubject / dcTermsSubject / dcTermSubject all carry subject data;
   158→            # dcTermsSubject was incorrectly mapped to dc:subject — corrected in CSV.
   159→            # After fix: dcSubject → dc:subject (literals); dcTermSubject/dcTermsSubject → dcterms:subject (IRIs).
   160→            # Dispatch: collect unique values across all three keys; route by value type.
   161→            if entity_type == "ProvidedCHO":
   162→                nt_lines.extend(emit_subject_triples(entity, subject_nt, alignment))
   163→
   164→            for json_key, raw_val in entity.items():
   165→                if json_key == "about" or raw_val is None:
   166→                    continue
   167→                if entity_type == "ProvidedCHO" and json_key in SUBJECT_KEYS:
   168→                    continue  # handled by emit_subject_triples
   169→
   170→                # Fan-out whitelist (issue 2):
   171→                #   creator    → single RDA property rdaw:P10065 (has creator agent of work)
   172→                #   contributor → dc:contributor kept as-is; no generic RDA equivalent in mocho
   173→                if entity_type == "ProvidedCHO" and json_key in ("creator", "contributor"):
   174→                    pred_iri = CREATOR_IRI if json_key == "creator" else CONTRIBUTOR_IRI
   175→                    pred_nt  = f"<{pred_iri}>"
   176→                    for obj_nt in value_to_nt_obj(raw_val):
   177→                        nt_lines.append(f"{subject_nt} {pred_nt} {obj_nt} .")
   178→                    continue
   179→
   180→                candidates = alignment.get((entity_type, json_key), [])
   181→                for row in candidates:
   182→                    pred_nt = f"<{row['rda_iri']}>"
   183→                    for obj_nt in value_to_nt_obj(raw_val):
   184→                        nt_lines.append(f"{subject_nt} {pred_nt} {obj_nt} .")
   185→
   186→    # rdf:type for ProvidedCHO and PhysicalThing via ddb:hierarchyType
   187→    nt_lines.extend(retype_entities(rdf, htype_map))
   188→    return nt_lines
   189→
   190→SUBJECT_KEYS = {"dcSubject", "dcTermsSubject", "dcTermSubject"}
   191→
   192→def emit_subject_triples(entity, subject_nt, alignment) -> list[str]:
   193→    # Collect all values from the three subject keys; deduplicate by (value_shape, content).
   194→    # Dispatch each unique value:
   195→    #   literal (str or lang-tagged text) → candidates for ("ProvidedCHO", "dcSubject")
   196→    #   IRI (resource) → candidates for ("ProvidedCHO", "dcTermSubject")
   197→    # Rationale: dc:subject is for uncontrolled literals; dcterms:subject for IRI references
   198→    #            to controlled vocabulary terms (GND, LCSH, etc.).
   199→    # dcTermsSubject IRI corrected in alignment CSV (was dc:subject, now dcterms:subject).
   200→    lit_candidates = alignment.get(("ProvidedCHO", "dcSubject"), [])
   201→    iri_candidates = alignment.get(("ProvidedCHO", "dcTermSubject"), [])
   202→
   203→    seen_objs: set[tuple[str, str]] = set()  # (pred_nt, obj_nt) dedup
   204→    nt_lines = []
   205→    for key in SUBJECT_KEYS:
   206→        raw_val = entity.get(key)
   207→        if raw_val is None:
   208→            continue
   209→        for obj_nt in value_to_nt_obj(raw_val):
   210→            is_iri = obj_nt.startswith("<")
   211→            candidates = iri_candidates if is_iri else lit_candidates
   212→            for row in candidates:
   213→                pred_nt = f"<{row['rda_iri']}>"
   214→                pair = (pred_nt, obj_nt)
   215→                if pair not in seen_objs:
   216→                    seen_objs.add(pair)
   217→                    nt_lines.append(f"{subject_nt} {pred_nt} {obj_nt} .")
   218→    return nt_lines
   219→
   220→def retype_entities(rdf, htype_map) -> list[str]:
   221→    # Handles rdf:type for ProvidedCHO and PhysicalThing — the two entity types
   222→    # that carry hierarchyType.
   223→    #
   224→    # ProvidedCHO:
   225→    #   - Always emit <cho_uri> rdf:type mocho:Manifestation (base type, D9).
   226→    #   - If hierarchyType present and mapped: also emit DoCO or RiC-O class.
   227→    #   - If has_record_set_type: emit <cho_uri> rico:hasRecordSetType <named_individual>.
   228→    #   - No edm:ProvidedCHO fallback.
   229→    #
   230→    # PhysicalThing (archival hierarchy ancestors, each with own URI):
   231→    #   - Each entity in the array carries its own hierarchyType → RiC-O class.
   232→    #   - No mocho:Manifestation — these are archival aggregation nodes (RecordSet,
   233→    #     Record, RecordPart), not carried objects.
   234→    #   - Same htype lookup; if pending or absent: emit nothing (no fallback type).
   235→    #   - If has_record_set_type: emit rico:hasRecordSetType as for ProvidedCHO.
   236→```
   237→
   238→### 3.6 Phase 4 — Output
   239→
   240→```python
   241→def main():
   242→    alignment  = load_alignment(ALIGNMENT_CSV)
   243→    htype_map  = load_htype_map(HTYPE_CSV)
   244→    ids_filter = load_ids(args.ids)
   245→
   246→    stats = defaultdict(int)
   247→    unmatched_keys = Counter()
   248→
   249→    with open(args.jsonl) as in_fh, open(args.out, "w") as out_fh:
   250→        for lineno, line in enumerate(in_fh):
   251→            if args.limit and lineno >= args.limit:
   252→                break
   253→            record = json.loads(line)
   254→            nt_lines = transform_record(record, alignment, htype_map, ids_filter)
   255→            for line in nt_lines:
   256→                out_fh.write(line + "\n")
   257→            stats["records_processed"] += 1
   258→            stats["triples_out"] += len(nt_lines)
   259→
   260→    write_stats(STATS_OUT, stats, unmatched_keys)
   261→```
   262→
   263→### 3.7 Stats output (`transform_stats.json`)
   264→
   265→```json
   266→{
   267→  "records_processed": 115432,
   268→  "records_skipped_not_in_ids": ...,
   269→  "triples_out": ...,
   270→  "triples_ignored": ...,
   271→  "objects_missing_specific_type": ...,
   272→  "whitelisted_keys": {"creator": "rdam:P30263", "contributor": "dc:contributor"},
   273→  "ignored_properties": {
   274→    "Aggregation.edmRights":  {"count": 115432, "edm_iri": "..."},
   275→    "Aggregation.isShownAt":  {"count": 115432, "edm_iri": "..."},
   276→    "...": "..."
   277→  }
   278→}
   279→```
   280→
   281→`triples_ignored` = total values skipped across all unmatched keys.
   282→`ignored_properties` = full inventory of every `EntityType.json_key` with values
   283→but no alignment candidates, sorted by count descending, with `edm_iri` for
   284→cross-reference.
   285→
   286→---
   287→
   288→## 4. Alignment scope (per ADR + spec)
   289→
   290→- **Mapped**: rows where `in_mocho=True` and `rda_iri` non-empty
   291→- **Skipped silently**: `edm:isShownAt`, `edm:aggregatedCHO`, `edm:provider`, etc. — EDM-structural, per ADR Decision 4; appear as `unmatched` in alignment table
   292→- **Skipped silently**: `skos:prefLabel/altLabel`, geo coordinates, EDM-native agent properties — unmatched, deferred to second pass
   293→- **Fan-out**: `dc:creator` → `rdam:P30263` only; `dc:contributor` → `dc:contributor` directly (see ADR D7–D8)
   294→- **rdf:type**: every ProvidedCHO gets `mocho:Manifestation`; where `hierarchyType` is present and mapped, also gets the DoCO or RiC-O class as an additional type; all other entity types get no rdf:type triple in this pass
   295→
   296→---
   297→
   298→## 5. Outputs
   299→
   300→| File | Description |
   301→|---|---|
   302→| `goethe-faust/output/mocho-goethe-faust.nt` | mocho-aligned NT triples (pipeline intermediate) |
   303→| `goethe-faust/output/mocho-goethe-faust.jsonld` | same triples in JSON-LD (inspection/tooling) |
   304→| `goethe-faust/output/transform_stats.json` | records processed, triples out/ignored, full ignored-properties inventory |
   305→
   306→---
   307→
   308→## 6. Verification
   309→
   310→Select one object per sector × mediatype combination for spot-checking. Coverage confirmed by grep on `items-all-goethe-faust.json`.
   311→
   312→**Sectors** — IRI base `http://ddb.vocnet.org/sparte/sparte00N`:
   313→
   314→| Code | Label | Records |
   315→|---|---|---|
   316→| sparte001 | Archiv | 50,216 |
   317→| sparte002 | Bibliothek | 50,198 |
   318→| sparte003 | Denkmalfach | 111 |
   319→| sparte004 | Mediathek | 1,283 |
   320→| sparte005 | Museum | 4,288 |
   321→| sparte006 | Sonstige | 9,215 |
   322→| sparte007 | Wissenschaftliche Einrichtung | 85 |
   323→
   324→**Mediatypes** — IRI base `http://ddb.vocnet.org/medientyp/mt00N` (mt004, mt006 absent):
   325→
   326→| Code | Label | Records |
   327→|---|---|---|
   328→| mt001 | Audio | 476 |
   329→| mt002 | Photo | 20,228 |
   330→| mt003 | Text | 52,247 |
   331→| mt005 | Video | 96 |
   332→| mt007 | Not Digitized | 42,360 |
   333→
   334→Note: not all 35 sector × mediatype combinations are populated. Small sectors (sparte003 = 111, sparte007 = 85) and small mediatypes (mt001 = 476, mt005 = 96) may lack certain intersections — document missing combinations in `transform_stats.json`.
   335→
   336→Steps:
   337→1. Run `--limit N` covering the full corpus (or run full pass); inspect NT output per spot-check object
   338→2. For each sector, select one object from each mediatype present in that sector; confirm every ProvidedCHO has a `mocho:Manifestation` rdf:type triple; where `hierarchyType` is present and mapped, confirm an additional DoCO or RiC-O rdf:type triple also appears; confirm no `edm:ProvidedCHO` rdf:type triples in output
   339→3. Confirm at least one RDA property triple is emitted for `dc:title`, `dc:creator`, or `dc:description`
   340→4. `transform_stats.json`: `triples_out / records_processed` ≈ 20–80 (reasonable range); `objects_missing_htype` documented
   341→5. Grep output NT for `europeana.eu` predicates — should be zero (all EDM predicates replaced or skipped)
   342→6. Update `goethe-faust/scripts/README.md`
   343→
   344→---
   345→
   346→## 7. RDF serialization choice
   347→
   348→| Format | Process | Size | Read |
   349→|---|---|---|---|
   350→| **N-Triples** (`.nt`) | Best — one triple/line, no parser context, grep/awk/stream-friendly | Worst — full URIs repeated every line | Poor |
   351→| **Turtle** (`.ttl`) | Good — most parsers support it | Best among text formats — prefix compression | Best |
   352→| **JSON-LD** (`.jsonld`) | Moderate — needs JSON + context handling | Moderate | Moderate (if you know JSON) |
   353→| RDF/XML | Worst | Worst | Worst |
   354→
   355→**Decision**: N-Triples for pipeline intermediate files (transform output, QLever ingest, sort/dedup); Turtle for hand-authored files (ontology, SPARQL update templates). N-Quads (`.nq`) if named graph identity is required alongside the same processing advantages.
   356→
   357→---
   358→
   359→## 8. Known issues to flag in code comments (per `alignment-ddbedm-mocho.md`)
   360→
   361→- **Triple subject keys**: ~~deduplication downstream~~ — **resolved**. `dcTermsSubject` IRI corrected in CSV (`dc:subject` → `dcterms:subject`). Transform uses `emit_subject_triples()`: value-type dispatch (literal → dc:subject candidates, IRI → dcterms:subject candidates) + per-record `(pred, obj)` deduplication across all three keys.
   362→- **Fan-out**: ~~not filtered in POC~~ — **resolved**. `creator` (464 Work-level candidates) → whitelisted to `rdaw:P10065 has creator agent of work`. `contributor` (360 candidates, no generic RDA equivalent in mocho) → kept as `dc:contributor` directly. Both bypass the alignment table via early `continue` in the transform loop.
   363→- **`Event.occurredAt`**: ~~no IRI resolved~~ — **resolved**. DDB API typo (`occuredAt`, one 'r') added to `OVERRIDES` in `align_ddbedm_to_mocho.py` → resolves to `edm:occurredAt`. `edm_field_profile.json` corrected to `occurredAt`. Will be picked up on next alignment re-run.
   364→- **`in_mocho` column type**: string `"True"` / `"False"`, not a Python bool — compare as `row['in_mocho'] == 'True'`
   365→- **`PhysicalThing.hierarchyType`**: ~~skipped~~ — **resolved**. `retype_entities()` handles both `ProvidedCHO` and `PhysicalThing`. PhysicalThing entities are archival hierarchy ancestors (Bestand, Gliederung, etc.) → typed via htype lookup to RiC-O classes only; no `mocho:Manifestation` (they are aggregation nodes, not carried objects). No fallback type if htype absent or pending.
   366→



```

```
     1→# Scripts
     2→
     3→All scripts live in `scripts/`. They use project-relative paths via
     4→`pathlib.Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent`, so they can be run from
     5→any working directory.
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## Ontology alignment (run in order)
    10→
    11→### `profile_edm_fields.py`
    12→Profiles all field keys present under `edm.RDF.*` entity types in the JSONL
    13→data file. Needed as input for `align_ddbedm_to_mocho.py`.
    14→
    15→- **Input**: `data/items-all-goethe-faust.json`
    16→- **Output**: `output/edm_field_profile.json`, `output/edm_field_profile.csv`
    17→- **Usage**: `python3 scripts/profile_edm_fields.py`
    18→- **Notes**: Reports per-entity-type field names with record counts and
    19→  coverage percentages. Counts > 100% indicate array-valued entities
    20→  (multiple agents/concepts/events per record).
    21→
    22→### `align_ddbedm_to_mocho.py`
    23→Data-driven ontology alignment from DDB-EDM to mocho. Maps each EDM/DC
    24→property actually present in the data to its corresponding RDA properties
    25→in mocho, via the DC→RDA sub-property mapping from the mocho workflow.
    26→
    27→- **Input**: `output/edm_field_profile.json` (from above),
    28→  `~/Documents/claude/mocho/ontology/ddbedm_1.0.ttl`,
    29→  `~/Documents/claude/mocho/mocho-odk/src/ontology/mocho-full.owl`,
    30→  `~/Documents/claude/mocho/output/mapping_dct_to_rda.csv`
    31→- **Output**: `output/alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv`,
    32→  `output/alignment_ddbedm_mocho.json`
    33→- **Usage**: `python3 scripts/align_ddbedm_to_mocho.py`
    34→- **Dependencies**: `rdflib`
    35→- **Notes**:
    36→  - One CSV row per (edm_field × rda_property) pair; high-fanout DC terms
    37→    (e.g. `dc:creator` → 232 RDA sub-properties) produce many rows per field
    38→  - 32 DC/DCT fields matched to RDA properties in mocho (1,271 alignment rows)
    39→  - 55 unmatched fields fall into expected categories: EDM-structural
    40→    (`edm:isShownAt`, `edm:begin/end`), DDB extensions (`ddb:hierarchyType`,
    41→    `ddb:aggregationEntity`), SKOS labels, geo coordinates, and two known
    42→    DCT→RDA gaps (`dc:identifier`, `dcterms:spatial`)
    43→  - `dcTermSubject` (DDB data variant of `dcterms:subject`) handled via
    44→    hard-coded override in `OVERRIDES` dict
    45→
    46→---
    47→

  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/goethe-faust/scripts/transform_edm_to_mocho.py",
  "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"\nPurpose:    Transform DDB-EDM JSONL records to mocho-aligned RDF triples.\n            Produces an N-Triples pipeline intermediate and a JSON-LD companion\n            file for inspection/tooling. Reference implementation for the mocho\n            ingest pipeline — decisions documented in goethe-faust/notes/alignment-adr.md.\nUsage:      python transform_edm_to_mocho.py [--jsonl FILE] [--ids FILE]\n                [--alignment FILE] [--htype FILE]\n                [--out-nt FILE] [--out-jsonld FILE] [--stats FILE] [--limit N]\nInputs:     data/items-all-goethe-faust.json         JSONL, one record per line\n            data/ids-all-goethe-faust.txt             32-char object IDs, one per line\n            output/alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv         (entity_type, json_key) → RDA candidates\n            output/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv         htype_code → DoCO/RiC-O class\nOutputs:    output/mocho-goethe-faust.nt              N-Triples (pipeline intermediate)\n            output/mocho-goethe-faust.jsonld          JSON-LD (inspection/tooling)\n            output/transform_stats.json               run stats + ignored-properties inventory\nDeps:       stdlib only (json, csv, re, sys, collections, argparse, pathlib)\nAssumes:    JSONL: one JSON object per line; record structure edm.RDF.*\n            alignment CSV columns: entity_type, json_key, edm_prefix, edm_iri,\n              record_count, coverage_pct, rda_iri, rda_label, wemi_level,\n              match_method, in_mocho  (in_mocho is string \"True\"/\"False\")\n            htype CSV column htype_code contains literal values as they appear\n              in the JSONL (e.g. \"htype_030\"), not German/English labels\n\"\"\"\n\nimport argparse\nimport csv\nimport json\nimport re\nimport sys\nfrom collections import Counter, defaultdict\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\n# ─── Paths ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nSCRIPT_DIR  = Path(__file__).resolve().parent\nPROJECT_DIR = SCRIPT_DIR.parent\n\nDEFAULT_JSONL      = PROJECT_DIR / \"data\"   / \"items-all-goethe-faust.json\"\nDEFAULT_IDS        = PROJECT_DIR / \"data\"   / \"ids-all-goethe-faust.txt\"\nDEFAULT_ALIGNMENT  = PROJECT_DIR / \"output\" / \"alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv\"\nDEFAULT_HTYPE      = PROJECT_DIR / \"output\" / \"lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv\"\nDEFAULT_OUT_NT     = PROJECT_DIR / \"output\" / \"mocho-goethe-faust.nt\"\nDEFAULT_OUT_JSONLD = PROJECT_DIR / \"output\" / \"mocho-goethe-faust.jsonld\"\nDEFAULT_STATS      = PROJECT_DIR / \"output\" / \"transform_stats.json\"\n\n# ─── Constants ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nRDF_TYPE            = \"http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type\"\nMOCHO_NS            = \"https://ise-fizkarlsruhe.github.io/ddbkg/mocho#\"\nMOCHO_MANIFESTATION = MOCHO_NS + \"Manifestation\"\nRICO_HAS_RST        = \"http://www.ica.org/standards/RiC/ontology#hasRecordSetType\"\n\n# Fan-out whitelist (ADR D7–D8): bypass alignment table for creator/contributor.\n#\n# D7: dc:creator → rdam:P30263 has creator agent of manifestation.\n#     The alignment table produced 464 Work-level candidates for creator, all\n#     highly specific (e.g. \"has plaintiff corporate body\"). Correct WEMI level\n#     is Manifestation, consistent with mocho:Manifestation base type (D9).\n#     rdam:P30263 is the generic Manifestation-level creator property.\n#\n# D8: dc:contributor → dc:contributor kept as-is.\n#     360 alignment table candidates, none generic (all specific performer roles).\n#     No generic \"has contributor agent\" superclass in mocho's current import.\n#     dc:contributor is a valid, well-understood predicate; keeps the link without\n#     asserting a role that cannot be determined from the DC value alone.\nCREATOR_IRI     = \"http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/m/P30263\"   # rdam:P30263\nCONTRIBUTOR_IRI = \"http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/contributor\" # dc:contributor\n\n# Subject keys: three JSON keys that carry subject data (ADR D6).\n# emit_subject_triples() handles value-type dispatch and cross-key deduplication.\n# Note: dcTermsSubject was incorrectly resolved to dc:subject in the alignment CSV\n# (IRI resolution error in align_ddbedm_to_mocho.py); corrected to dcterms:subject\n# in the CSV (42 rows). Dispatch routes IRI-valued subjects to dcterms:subject path\n# regardless of which of the three keys carried the value.\nSUBJECT_KEYS = frozenset({\"dcSubject\", \"dcTermsSubject\", \"dcTermSubject\"})\n\n# Keys handled outside the main alignment loop — skip silently, do not count\n# as ignored properties in stats output.\n# hierarchyType → handled by retype_entities() via htype_map\n_SKIP_IN_LOOP = frozenset({\"about\", \"hierarchyType\"})\n\n# Prefix expansion for htype lookup table\n_PREFIXES = {\n    \"doco\":    \"http://purl.org/spar/doco/\",\n    \"rico\":    \"http://www.ica.org/standards/RiC/ontology#\",\n    \"ric-rst\": \"http://www.ica.org/standards/RiC/vocabularies/recordSetTypes#\",\n    \"mocho\":   MOCHO_NS,\n}\n\n# ─── Loaders ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\ndef load_alignment(path: Path) -> tuple[dict, dict]:\n    \"\"\"Load alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv.\n\n    Returns:\n        alignment: dict[(entity_type, json_key)] → list of candidate dicts\n            Only rows where in_mocho == 'True' and rda_iri non-empty.\n            Each candidate: {rda_iri, rda_label, wemi_level, match_method}\n        edm_iri_map: dict[(entity_type, json_key)] → edm_iri\n            For all rows (used to enrich ignored_properties in stats output).\n    \"\"\"\n    alignment: dict = defaultdict(list)\n    edm_iri_map: dict = {}\n    with open(path, newline=\"\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as fh:\n        for row in csv.DictReader(fh):\n            key = (row[\"entity_type\"].strip(), row[\"json_key\"].strip())\n            edm_iri = row.get(\"edm_iri\", \"\").strip()\n            if edm_iri and key not in edm_iri_map:\n                edm_iri_map[key] = edm_iri\n            if row[\"in_mocho\"] != \"True\" or not row[\"rda_iri\"].strip():\n                continue\n            alignment[key].append({\n                \"rda_iri\":      row[\"rda_iri\"].strip(),\n                \"rda_label\":    row[\"rda_label\"].strip(),\n                \"wemi_level\":   row[\"wemi_level\"].strip(),\n                \"match_method\": row[\"match_method\"].strip(),\n            })\n    return dict(alignment), edm_iri_map\n\n\ndef _expand_prefix(curie: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Expand a prefixed IRI (e.g. 'doco:Section') to a full IRI.\"\"\"\n    for prefix, base in _PREFIXES.items():\n        if curie.startswith(prefix + \":\"):\n            return base + curie[len(prefix) + 1:]\n    return curie  # already a full IRI or unrecognised prefix\n\n\ndef load_htype_map(path: Path) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"Load lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv.\n\n    Returns dict[htype_code] → (rdf_type_iri, [rst_iri, ...]).\n\n    Rows where rdf_type == \"pending\" or empty are excluded.\n    has_record_set_type may be comma-separated (e.g. \"mocho:Bestand, ric-rst:Fonds\");\n    each part is expanded to a full IRI and emitted as a separate rico:hasRecordSetType\n    triple (see retype_entities).\n    \"\"\"\n    result: dict = {}\n    with open(path, newline=\"\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as fh:\n        for row in csv.DictReader(fh):\n            rdf_type = row[\"rdf_type\"].strip()\n            if not rdf_type or rdf_type == \"pending\":\n                continue\n            type_iri = _expand_prefix(rdf_type)\n            rst_iris: list[str] = []\n            rst_raw = row.get(\"has_record_set_type\", \"\").strip()\n            if rst_raw:\n                for part in rst_raw.split(\",\"):\n                    part = part.strip()\n                    if part:\n                        rst_iris.append(_expand_prefix(part))\n            result[row[\"htype_code\"].strip()] = (type_iri, rst_iris)\n    return result\n\n\ndef load_ids(path: Path) -> set:\n    \"\"\"Load ids-all-goethe-faust.txt. Returns set of 32-char object IDs.\"\"\"\n    with open(path, encoding=\"utf-8\") as fh:\n        return {line.strip() for line in fh if line.strip()}\n\n\n# ─── Record helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\ndef get_object_id(record: dict):\n    \"\"\"Extract 32-char object ID from ProvidedCHO.about URI. Returns None on failure.\"\"\"\n    try:\n        about = record[\"edm\"][\"RDF\"][\"ProvidedCHO\"][\"about\"]\n    except (KeyError, TypeError):\n        return None\n    if not about:\n        return None\n    obj_id = about.rstrip(\"/\").rsplit(\"/\", 1)[-1]\n    return obj_id if len(obj_id) == 32 else None\n\n\ndef _escape_literal(s: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Escape backslash and double-quote for N-Triples literal content.\"\"\"\n    return s.replace(\"\\\\\", \"\\\\\\\\\").replace('\"', '\\\\\"')\n\n\ndef value_to_nt_obj(val) -> list:\n    \"\"\"Convert a JSONL field value to a list of N-Triples object strings.\n\n    Handles all value shapes produced by the DDB EDM JSONL:\n      None / \"\"                     → []\n      str (non-empty)               → ['\"escaped\"']\n      list                          → recurse and flatten\n      {\"resource\": IRI}             → [\"<IRI>\"]   (IRI non-null/non-empty)\n      {\"lang\": L, \"$\": T}           → ['\"T\"@L']   (L non-null, T non-empty)\n      {\"lang\": null, \"$\": T}        → ['\"T\"']     (T non-empty)\n      {\"resource\": null, \"$\": \"\"}   → []\n    \"\"\"\n    if val is None:\n        return []\n    if isinstance(val, str):\n        return [f'\"{_escape_literal(val)}\"'] if val else []\n    if isinstance(val, list):\n        result = []\n        for item in val:\n            result.extend(value_to_nt_obj(item))\n        return result\n    if isinstance(val, dict):\n        resource = val.get(\"resource\")\n        if resource:  # non-null, non-empty IRI\n            return [f\"<{resource}>\"]\n        text = val.get(\"$\", \"\")\n        if not text:\n            return []\n        escaped = _escape_literal(str(text))\n        lang = val.get(\"lang\")\n        if lang:\n            return [f'\"{escaped}\"@{lang}']\n        return [f'\"{escaped}\"']\n    return []\n\n\ndef emit_subject_triples(entity: dict, subject_nt: str, alignment: dict) -> list:\n    \"\"\"Emit RDF triples for dc:subject / dcterms:subject (ADR D6).\n\n    Collects values from all three subject keys (dcSubject, dcTermsSubject,\n    dcTermSubject), dispatches by value type:\n      IRI value     → alignment candidates for (\"ProvidedCHO\", \"dcTermSubject\")\n                       i.e. dcterms:subject path\n      literal value → alignment candidates for (\"ProvidedCHO\", \"dcSubject\")\n                       i.e. dc:subject path\n    Deduplicates (pred_nt, obj_nt) pairs across keys before writing.\n    ~60% of records carry duplicate values across the three keys.\n    \"\"\"\n    lit_candidates = alignment.get((\"ProvidedCHO\", \"dcSubject\"), [])\n    iri_candidates = alignment.get((\"ProvidedCHO\", \"dcTermSubject\"), [])\n    seen: set = set()\n    nt_lines: list = []\n    for key in SUBJECT_KEYS:\n        raw_val = entity.get(key)\n        if raw_val is None:\n            continue\n        for obj_nt in value_to_nt_obj(raw_val):\n            candidates = iri_candidates if obj_nt.startswith(\"<\") else lit_candidates\n            for row in candidates:\n                pred_nt = f\"<{row['rda_iri']}>\"\n                pair = (pred_nt, obj_nt)\n                if pair not in seen:\n                    seen.add(pair)\n                    nt_lines.append(f\"{subject_nt} {pred_nt} {obj_nt} .\")\n    return nt_lines\n\n\ndef retype_entities(rdf: dict, htype_map: dict, stats: dict) -> list:\n    \"\"\"Emit rdf:type triples for ProvidedCHO and PhysicalThing (ADR D9, D10).\n\n    ProvidedCHO (D9):\n      Always emits mocho:Manifestation as base type. edm:ProvidedCHO is an EDM\n      modelling artefact, not an ontological type; it is not emitted.\n      If hierarchyType is present and mapped: also emits the DoCO or RiC-O class\n      as an additional, more specific rdf:type.\n      If has_record_set_type: emits rico:hasRecordSetType triple(s).\n      If hierarchyType is absent or pending: counts in objects_missing_specific_type.\n\n    PhysicalThing (D10):\n      Archival hierarchy ancestor nodes (Bestand, Gliederung, etc.) stored inline\n      in each record — the finding aid lineage. Not carried objects.\n      Typed via htype lookup to RiC-O class only; no mocho:Manifestation.\n      If hierarchyType is absent or pending: no rdf:type emitted (no fallback).\n    \"\"\"\n    nt_lines: list = []\n    rdf_type_nt = f\"<{RDF_TYPE}>\"\n    rico_rst_nt = f\"<{RICO_HAS_RST}>\"\n\n    # ProvidedCHO\n    cho = rdf.get(\"ProvidedCHO\")\n    if isinstance(cho, dict):\n        about = cho.get(\"about\")\n        if about:\n            s = f\"<{about}>\"\n            nt_lines.append(f\"{s} {rdf_type_nt} <{MOCHO_MANIFESTATION}> .\")\n            htype = cho.get(\"hierarchyType\")\n            if htype and htype in htype_map:\n                type_iri, rst_iris = htype_map[htype]\n                nt_lines.append(f\"{s} {rdf_type_nt} <{type_iri}> .\")\n                for rst_iri in rst_iris:\n                    nt_lines.append(f\"{s} {rico_rst_nt} <{rst_iri}> .\")\n            else:\n                stats[\"objects_missing_specific_type\"] += 1\n\n    # PhysicalThing\n    physical_things = rdf.get(\"PhysicalThing\")\n    if isinstance(physical_things, list):\n        for entity in physical_things:\n            if not isinstance(entity, dict):\n                continue\n            about = entity.get(\"about\")\n            if not about:\n                continue\n            htype = entity.get(\"hierarchyType\")\n            if htype and htype in htype_map:\n                s = f\"<{about}>\"\n                type_iri, rst_iris = htype_map[htype]\n                nt_lines.append(f\"{s} {rdf_type_nt} <{type_iri}> .\")\n                for rst_iri in rst_iris:\n                    nt_lines.append(f\"{s} {rico_rst_nt} <{rst_iri}> .\")\n\n    return nt_lines\n\n\n# ─── Core transform ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\ndef transform_record(\n    record: dict,\n    alignment: dict,\n    htype_map: dict,\n    ids_filter: set,\n    stats: dict,\n    ignored_keys: Counter,\n) -> list:\n    \"\"\"Transform one JSONL record to a list of N-Triples lines.\n\n    Returns empty list if the record's object ID is not in ids_filter\n    or if the record structure is malformed.\n    \"\"\"\n    obj_id = get_object_id(record)\n    if obj_id not in ids_filter:\n        stats[\"records_skipped_not_in_ids\"] += 1\n        return []\n\n    try:\n        rdf = record[\"edm\"][\"RDF\"]\n    except (KeyError, TypeError):\n        stats[\"records_skipped_not_in_ids\"] += 1\n        return []\n\n    nt_lines: list = []\n\n    for entity_type, entities in rdf.items():\n        if entities is None:\n            continue\n        if isinstance(entities, dict):\n            entities = [entities]\n        if not isinstance(entities, list):\n            continue\n\n        for entity in entities:\n            if not isinstance(entity, dict):\n                continue\n            subject = entity.get(\"about\")\n            if not subject:\n                continue\n            subject_nt = f\"<{subject}>\"\n\n            # Subject key dispatch + cross-key deduplication (ADR D6)\n            if entity_type == \"ProvidedCHO\":\n                nt_lines.extend(emit_subject_triples(entity, subject_nt, alignment))\n\n            for json_key, raw_val in entity.items():\n                if json_key in _SKIP_IN_LOOP or raw_val is None:\n                    continue\n\n                # Subject keys handled above\n                if entity_type == \"ProvidedCHO\" and json_key in SUBJECT_KEYS:\n                    continue\n\n                # Fan-out whitelist (ADR D7–D8)\n                if entity_type == \"ProvidedCHO\" and json_key in (\"creator\", \"contributor\"):\n                    pred_iri = CREATOR_IRI if json_key == \"creator\" else CONTRIBUTOR_IRI\n                    pred_nt = f\"<{pred_iri}>\"\n                    for obj_nt in value_to_nt_obj(raw_val):\n                        nt_lines.append(f\"{subject_nt} {pred_nt} {obj_nt} .\")\n                    continue\n\n                candidates = alignment.get((entity_type, json_key), [])\n                if not candidates:\n                    vals = value_to_nt_obj(raw_val)\n                    if vals:\n                        ignored_keys[f\"{entity_type}.{json_key}\"] += len(vals)\n                    continue\n\n                pred_nt_cache: dict = {}\n                for row in candidates:\n                    pred_iri = row[\"rda_iri\"]\n                    if pred_iri not in pred_nt_cache:\n                        pred_nt_cache[pred_iri] = f\"<{pred_iri}>\"\n                    pred_nt = pred_nt_cache[pred_iri]\n                    for obj_nt in value_to_nt_obj(raw_val):\n                        nt_lines.append(f\"{subject_nt} {pred_nt} {obj_nt} .\")\n\n    # rdf:type for ProvidedCHO (mocho:Manifestation + htype class) and PhysicalThing\n    nt_lines.extend(retype_entities(rdf, htype_map, stats))\n\n    stats[\"records_processed\"] += 1\n    stats[\"triples_out\"] += len(nt_lines)\n    return nt_lines\n\n\n# ─── JSON-LD helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n# Matches our generated NT lines: <subj> <pred> <obj> .\n_NT_RE = re.compile(r'^(<[^>]+>)\\s+(<[^>]+>)\\s+(.+) \\.$')\n_LANG_LIT_RE  = re.compile(r'^\"((?:[^\"\\\\]|\\\\.)*)\"@([\\w-]+)$')\n_PLAIN_LIT_RE = re.compile(r'^\"((?:[^\"\\\\]|\\\\.)*)\"$')\n\n_JSONLD_CONTEXT = {\n    \"rdf\":     \"http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#\",\n    \"rdfs\":    \"http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#\",\n    \"dc\":      \"http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/\",\n    \"dcterms\": \"http://purl.org/dc/terms/\",\n    \"edm\":     \"http://www.europeana.eu/schemas/edm/\",\n    \"mocho\":   MOCHO_NS,\n    \"rico\":    \"http://www.ica.org/standards/RiC/ontology#\",\n    \"doco\":    \"http://purl.org/spar/doco/\",\n    \"rdam\":    \"http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/m/\",\n    \"rdaw\":    \"http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/w/\",\n    \"rdaa\":    \"http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/a/\",\n    \"skos\":    \"http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#\",\n}\n\n\ndef _nt_obj_to_jsonld(obj_nt: str) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"Convert an N-Triples object string to a JSON-LD value object.\"\"\"\n    if obj_nt.startswith(\"<\") and obj_nt.endswith(\">\"):\n        return {\"@id\": obj_nt[1:-1]}\n    m = _LANG_LIT_RE.match(obj_nt)\n    if m:\n        text = m.group(1).replace('\\\\\"', '\"').replace(\"\\\\\\\\\", \"\\\\\")\n        return {\"@value\": text, \"@language\": m.group(2)}\n    m = _PLAIN_LIT_RE.match(obj_nt)\n    if m:\n        text = m.group(1).replace('\\\\\"', '\"').replace(\"\\\\\\\\\", \"\\\\\")\n        return {\"@value\": text}\n    return {\"@value\": obj_nt}  # fallback\n\n\ndef nt_lines_to_jsonld_nodes(nt_lines: list) -> list:\n    \"\"\"Convert a list of N-Triples lines to JSON-LD node objects.\n\n    Groups triples by subject → one node dict per subject:\n      {\"@id\": subject_iri, pred_iri: [obj_value, ...], ...}\n    \"\"\"\n    nodes: dict = {}\n    for line in nt_lines:\n        m = _NT_RE.match(line.strip())\n        if not m:\n            continue\n        subj = m.group(1)[1:-1]\n        pred = m.group(2)[1:-1]\n        obj_nt = m.group(3).strip()\n        node = nodes.setdefault(subj, {\"@id\": subj})\n        node.setdefault(pred, []).append(_nt_obj_to_jsonld(obj_nt))\n    return list(nodes.values())\n\n\n# ─── Main ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\ndef main() -> None:\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(\n        description=\"Transform DDB-EDM JSONL to mocho-aligned RDF (N-Triples + JSON-LD).\"\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\"--jsonl\",      default=DEFAULT_JSONL,      type=Path)\n    parser.add_argument(\"--ids\",        default=DEFAULT_IDS,        type=Path)\n    parser.add_argument(\"--alignment\",  default=DEFAULT_ALIGNMENT,  type=Path)\n    parser.add_argument(\"--htype\",      default=DEFAULT_HTYPE,      type=Path)\n    parser.add_argument(\"--out-nt\",     default=DEFAULT_OUT_NT,     type=Path, dest=\"out_nt\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"--out-jsonld\", default=DEFAULT_OUT_JSONLD, type=Path, dest=\"out_jsonld\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"--stats\",      default=DEFAULT_STATS,      type=Path)\n    parser.add_argument(\"--limit\",      default=0, type=int,\n                        help=\"Stop after N records (0 = no limit, default)\")\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n\n    print(\"Loading alignment table ...\", file=sys.stderr)\n    alignment, edm_iri_map = load_alignment(args.alignment)\n    print(f\"  {sum(len(v) for v in alignment.values())} candidate rows across \"\n          f\"{len(alignment)} (entity_type, json_key) keys\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n    print(\"Loading htype map ...\", file=sys.stderr)\n    htype_map = load_htype_map(args.htype)\n    print(f\"  {len(htype_map)} htype codes mapped\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n    print(\"Loading IDs filter ...\", file=sys.stderr)\n    ids_filter = load_ids(args.ids)\n    print(f\"  {len(ids_filter):,} IDs loaded\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n    stats: dict = {\n        \"records_processed\":             0,\n        \"records_skipped_not_in_ids\":    0,\n        \"triples_out\":                   0,\n        \"objects_missing_specific_type\": 0,\n        \"whitelisted_keys\": {\n            \"creator\":     \"rdam:P30263 has creator agent of manifestation\",\n            \"contributor\": \"dc:contributor (no generic RDA equivalent in mocho)\",\n        },\n    }\n    ignored_keys: Counter = Counter()\n\n    print(f\"Streaming {args.jsonl.name} ...\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n    with (\n        open(args.jsonl, encoding=\"utf-8\")           as in_fh,\n        open(args.out_nt, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\")     as nt_fh,\n        open(args.out_jsonld, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as jld_fh,\n    ):\n        # JSON-LD header: {\"@context\": {...}, \"@graph\": [\n        jld_fh.write('{\\n  \"@context\": ')\n        jld_fh.write(json.dumps(_JSONLD_CONTEXT, ensure_ascii=False))\n        jld_fh.write(',\\n  \"@graph\": [\\n')\n        first_node = True\n\n        for lineno, raw_line in enumerate(in_fh):\n            if args.limit and lineno >= args.limit:\n                break\n            raw_line = raw_line.strip()\n            if not raw_line:\n                continue\n            try:\n                record = json.loads(raw_line)\n            except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:\n                print(f\"  WARNING line {lineno + 1}: JSON decode error: {exc}\",\n                      file=sys.stderr)\n                continue\n\n            nt_lines = transform_record(\n                record, alignment, htype_map, ids_filter, stats, ignored_keys\n            )\n            if not nt_lines:\n                continue\n\n            # Write N-Triples\n            for line in nt_lines:\n                nt_fh.write(line + \"\\n\")\n\n            # Write JSON-LD nodes (one per subject in this record's triples)\n            for node in nt_lines_to_jsonld_nodes(nt_lines):\n                if not first_node:\n                    jld_fh.write(\",\\n\")\n                jld_fh.write(\"    \" + json.dumps(node, ensure_ascii=False))\n                first_node = False\n\n            if (lineno + 1) % 10_000 == 0:\n                print(f\"  {lineno + 1:,} lines, \"\n                      f\"{stats['records_processed']:,} processed, \"\n                      f\"{stats['triples_out']:,} triples\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n        jld_fh.write(\"\\n  ]\\n}\\n\")\n\n    # Build stats output\n    triples_ignored = sum(ignored_keys.values())\n    ignored_properties = {\n        k: {\n            \"count\":   v,\n            \"edm_iri\": edm_iri_map.get(tuple(k.split(\".\", 1))),\n        }\n        for k, v in ignored_keys.most_common()\n    }\n    stats_out = {\n        **stats,\n        \"triples_ignored\":     triples_ignored,\n        \"ignored_properties\":  ignored_properties,\n    }\n    with open(args.stats, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as fh:\n        json.dump(stats_out, fh, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)\n\n    print(\n        f\"\\nDone. {stats['records_processed']:,} records, \"\n        f\"{stats['triples_out']:,} triples written, \"\n        f\"{triples_ignored:,} values ignored.\",\n        file=sys.stderr,\n    )\n    print(f\"  NT:     {args.out_nt}\", file=sys.stderr)\n    print(f\"  JSON-LD:{args.out_jsonld}\", file=sys.stderr)\n    print(f\"  Stats:  {args.stats}\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\n"
}
```

```
     1→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2→"""
     3→align_ddbedm_to_mocho.py
     4→========================
     5→Data-driven ontology alignment: maps DDB-EDM fields present in
     6→items-all-goethe-faust.json to their corresponding mocho/RDA properties.
     7→
     8→Pipeline:
     9→  1. Profile data  — load edm_field_profile.json (from profile_edm_fields.py)
    10→  2. Resolve IRIs  — map JSON camelCase/plain keys to full EDM/DC IRIs
    11→                     by parsing ddbedm_1.0.ttl with rdflib
    12→  3. Load DC→RDA   — read mapping_dct_to_rda.csv for DC/DCT→RDA sub-property
    13→                     pairs produced by the mocho dct_rda_map.py workflow
    14→  4. Load mocho    — parse mocho-full.owl to collect all RDA properties
    15→                     imported into mocho (IRIs + English labels + WEMI level)
    16→  5. Align         — for each data field: DC/EDM IRI → RDA candidates →
    17→                     restrict to mocho-known properties
    18→                     Key explicit dimensions: ProvidedCHO.dcType → rdae:P20001
    19→  5b. Align vocab  — controlled-vocabulary Concepts:
    20→                     Concept.about from ddb.vocnet.org/sparte/ → sector
    21→                     Concept.about from ddb.vocnet.org/medientyp/ → rdae:P20001
    22→  6. Write outputs
    23→
    24→Inputs:
    25→  output/edm_field_profile.json          — field profile (run profile_edm_fields.py first)
    26→  ~/Documents/claude/mocho/ontology/ddbedm_1.0.ttl      — DDB-EDM ontology
    27→  ~/Documents/claude/mocho/mocho-odk/src/ontology/mocho-full.owl  — mocho
    28→  ~/Documents/claude/mocho/output/mapping_dct_to_rda.csv  — DC→RDA mapping
    29→
    30→Outputs:
    31→  output/alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv   — one row per (edm_field × rda_property)
    32→  output/alignment_ddbedm_mocho.json  — summary + full alignment
    33→
    34→Columns (CSV):
    35→  entity_type, json_key, edm_prefix, edm_iri, record_count, coverage_pct,
    36→  rda_iri, rda_label, wemi_level, match_method, in_mocho, vocab_label
    37→  (vocab_label is non-empty only for concept_vocab_sector / concept_vocab_mediatype rows)
    38→
    39→Usage:
    40→  python3 scripts/profile_edm_fields.py   # run first if profile is stale
    41→  python3 scripts/align_ddbedm_to_mocho.py
    42→
    43→Dependencies: rdflib
    44→Assumptions:
    45→  - ddbedm_1.0.ttl declares all properties used in the DDB-EDM profile
    46→  - mapping_dct_to_rda.csv schema: dct_term, rda_iri, rda_label
    47→  - mocho-full.owl is RDF/XML and includes all imported RDA properties inline
    48→"""
    49→
    50→import csv
    51→import json
    52→from collections import defaultdict
    53→from pathlib import Path
    54→
    55→from rdflib import Graph, RDF, RDFS, OWL, Namespace
    56→from rdflib.namespace import DC, DCTERMS, SKOS
    57→
    58→PROJECT  = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
    59→MOCHO    = Path.home() / "Documents/claude/mocho"
    60→
    61→PROFILE_PATH  = PROJECT / "output" / "edm_field_profile.json"
    62→DDBEDM_TTL    = MOCHO / "ontology" / "ddbedm_1.0.ttl"
    63→MOCHO_OWL     = MOCHO / "mocho-odk" / "src" / "ontology" / "mocho-full.owl"
    64→DCT_RDA_CSV   = MOCHO / "output" / "mapping_dct_to_rda.csv"
    65→OUT_CSV       = PROJECT / "output" / "alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv"
    66→OUT_JSON      = PROJECT / "output" / "alignment_ddbedm_mocho.json"
    67→
    68→# ── namespace constants ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    69→
    70→EDM  = Namespace("http://www.europeana.eu/schemas/edm/")
    71→ORE  = Namespace("http://www.openarchives.org/ore/terms/")
    72→GEO  = Namespace("http://www.w3.org/2003/01/geo/wgs84_pos#")
    73→FOAF = Namespace("http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/")
    74→RDA_ELEMENTS = "http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/"
    75→
    76→WEMI_LEVELS = {
    77→    f"{RDA_ELEMENTS}w/": "Work",
    78→    f"{RDA_ELEMENTS}e/": "Expression",
    79→    f"{RDA_ELEMENTS}m/": "Manifestation",
    80→    f"{RDA_ELEMENTS}i/": "Item",
    81→    f"{RDA_ELEMENTS}a/": "Agent",
    82→    f"{RDA_ELEMENTS}c/": "Corporate Body",
    83→    f"{RDA_ELEMENTS}p/": "Person",
    84→}
    85→
    86→# ── controlled vocabulary constants ───────────────────────────────────────────
    87→# DDB sector (Sparte) vocabulary — carried in edm:Concept.about
    88→SPARTE_VOCAB = {
    89→    "http://ddb.vocnet.org/sparte/sparte001": "Archive",
    90→    "http://ddb.vocnet.org/sparte/sparte002": "Library",
    91→    "http://ddb.vocnet.org/sparte/sparte003": "Monument Preservation",
    92→    "http://ddb.vocnet.org/sparte/sparte004": "Research",
    93→    "http://ddb.vocnet.org/sparte/sparte005": "Media Library",
    94→    "http://ddb.vocnet.org/sparte/sparte006": "Museum",
    95→    "http://ddb.vocnet.org/sparte/sparte007": "Others",
    96→}
    97→
    98→# DDB media type (Medientyp) vocabulary — carried in edm:Concept.about
    99→MEDIENTYP_VOCAB = {
   100→    "http://ddb.vocnet.org/medientyp/mt001": "Audio",
   101→    "http://ddb.vocnet.org/medientyp/mt002": "Photo",
   102→    "http://ddb.vocnet.org/medientyp/mt003": "Text",
   103→    "http://ddb.vocnet.org/medientyp/mt005": "Video",
   104→    "http://ddb.vocnet.org/medientyp/mt007": "Not Digitized",
   105→}
   106→
   107→# Media type aligns to rdae:P20001 (has content type) — same target as dc:type
   108→MEDIENTYP_RDA_IRI = "http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/e/P20001"
   109→
   110→# ── step 1: load field profile ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
   111→
   112→print("Loading field profile …")
   113→with open(PROFILE_PATH) as f:
   114→    profile = json.load(f)
   115→
   116→total_records = profile["total_records"]
   117→# {(entity_type, json_key): {record_count, record_pct}}
   118→data_fields = {
   119→    (row["entity_type"], row["json_key"]): row
   120→    for row in profile["fields"]
   121→}
   122→print(f"  {len(data_fields)} (entity_type, json_key) pairs from {total_records:,} records")
   123→
   124→# ── step 2: resolve JSON keys to EDM/DC IRIs ──────────────────────────────────
   125→
   126→print("\nParsing DDB-EDM ontology …")
   127→g_edm = Graph()
   128→g_edm.parse(DDBEDM_TTL)
   129→
   130→# Build lookup: normalised_local_name → [(prefix_label, full_iri)]
   131→# where normalised = lowercase of local name
   132→# Hard-coded overrides for known DDB data quirks: json_key → (prefix, iri)
   133→# dcTermSubject (no 's') is a DDB data variant for dcterms:subject
   134→# identifier (no prefix) is dc:identifier in ProvidedCHO context
   135→# occuredAt (one 'r') is a DDB API typo for edm:occurredAt
   136→OVERRIDES = {
   137→    "dcTermSubject": ("dcterms", str(DCTERMS) + "subject"),
   138→    "identifier":    ("dc",      str(DC) + "identifier"),
   139→    "occuredAt":     ("edm",     "http://www.europeana.eu/schemas/edm/occurredAt"),
   140→}
   141→
   142→prop_lookup = defaultdict(list)  # normalised_local_name → [(prefix_label, full_iri)]
   143→for prop_type in (OWL.ObjectProperty, OWL.DatatypeProperty, OWL.AnnotationProperty):
   144→    for subj in g_edm.subjects(RDF.type, prop_type):
   145→        iri = str(subj)
   146→        local = iri.rsplit("/", 1)[-1].rsplit("#", 1)[-1]
   147→        try:
   148→            prefix = g_edm.qname(subj).split(":")[0]
   149→        except Exception:
   150→            prefix = ""
   151→        prop_lookup[local.lower()].append((prefix, iri))
   152→
   153→# Also include classes for context
   154→for subj in g_edm.subjects(RDF.type, OWL.Class):
   155→    iri = str(subj)
   156→    local = iri.rsplit("/", 1)[-1].rsplit("#", 1)[-1]
   157→    prop_lookup[local.lower()].append(("class", iri))
   158→
   159→print(f"  {len(prop_lookup)} unique local names from DDB-EDM")
   160→
   161→
   162→def resolve_iri(entity_type: str, json_key: str):
   163→    """Map a JSON camelCase/plain key to (prefix_label, full_iri).
   164→
   165→    Checks hard-coded overrides first, then the ontology lookup, then
   166→    heuristic prefix-stripping.
   167→
   168→    Resolution order:
   169→    1. Direct match of json_key (lowercase) against the ontology lookup.
   170→    2. Strip known camelCase prefixes (dcTerms, dc, edm, ore, foaf, geo, skos)
   171→       and match the lowercased remainder.
   172→    3. Entity-type-specific defaults for keys without an obvious namespace.
   173→    Returns (prefix_label, iri) or None if unresolved.
   174→    """
   175→    # Hard-coded overrides
   176→    if json_key in OVERRIDES:
   177→        return OVERRIDES[json_key]
   178→
   179→    key_lower = json_key.lower()
   180→
   181→    # Direct match
   182→    if key_lower in prop_lookup:
   183→        return prop_lookup[key_lower][0]
   184→
   185→    # Strip camelCase namespace prefixes in order (longest first to avoid greedy mismatch)
   186→    prefixes = [
   187→        ("dcterms", "dcterms", str(DCTERMS)),
   188→        ("dcterms", "dcTerms", str(DCTERMS)),
   189→        ("dc",      "dc",      str(DC)),
   190→        ("edm",     "edm",     str(EDM)),
   191→        ("ore",     "ore",     str(ORE)),
   192→        ("skos",    "skos",    str(SKOS)),
   193→        ("geo",     "geo",     str(GEO)),
   194→        ("foaf",    "foaf",    str(FOAF)),
   195→    ]
   196→    for label, strip_prefix, ns in prefixes:
   197→        if json_key.startswith(strip_prefix) and len(json_key) > len(strip_prefix):
   198→            local = json_key[len(strip_prefix):]
   199→            local_lower = local[0].lower() + local[1:]
   200→            iri = ns + local_lower
   201→            # Verify it exists in the ontology or return it as a best-effort match
   202→            if local_lower in prop_lookup:
   203→                return prop_lookup[local_lower][0]
   204→            # Best-effort: construct IRI even if not explicitly in ontology
   205→            return (label, iri)
   206→
   207→    # Entity-type-specific defaults for unnamespaced keys
   208→    entity_defaults = {
   209→        # TimeSpan: begin/end are edm: properties
   210→        "TimeSpan": str(EDM),
   211→        # Place: lat/long/alt are geo:
   212→        "Place":    str(GEO),
   213→        # Agent: EDM agent properties
   214→        "Agent":    str(EDM),
   215→    }
   216→    ns = entity_defaults.get(entity_type)
   217→    if ns:
   218→        iri = ns + json_key
   219→        return (entity_type.lower(), iri)
   220→
   221→    return None
   222→
   223→
   224→# Resolve all data fields
   225→field_iris = {}  # (entity_type, json_key) -> (prefix_label, iri) or None
   226→for (entity_type, json_key) in data_fields:
   227→    field_iris[(entity_type, json_key)] = resolve_iri(entity_type, json_key)
   228→
   229→resolved   = sum(1 for v in field_iris.values() if v is not None)
   230→unresolved = sum(1 for v in field_iris.values() if v is None)
   231→print(f"  Resolved: {resolved}  Unresolved: {unresolved}")
   232→
   233→# ── step 3: load DC→RDA mapping ───────────────────────────────────────────────
   234→
   235→print("\nLoading DC→RDA mapping …")
   236→# dct_term → [(rda_iri, rda_label)]
   237→dct_to_rda: dict[str, list[tuple[str, str]]] = defaultdict(list)
   238→with open(DCT_RDA_CSV, newline="", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   239→    for row in csv.DictReader(f):
   240→        dct_to_rda[row["dct_term"]].append((row["rda_iri"], row["rda_label"]))
   241→
   242→print(f"  {len(dct_to_rda)} DC/DCT terms → {sum(len(v) for v in dct_to_rda.values())} RDA pairs")
   243→
   244→# ── step 4: load mocho RDA properties ────────────────────────────────────────
   245→
   246→print("\nLoading mocho-full.owl …")
   247→g_mocho = Graph()
   248→g_mocho.parse(MOCHO_OWL)
   249→
   250→mocho_rda: dict[str, str] = {}  # iri → English label
   251→for prop_type in (OWL.ObjectProperty, OWL.DatatypeProperty, OWL.AnnotationProperty):
   252→    for subj in g_mocho.subjects(RDF.type, prop_type):
   253→        iri = str(subj)
   254→        if RDA_ELEMENTS not in iri:
   255→            continue
   256→        # Prefer English label
   257→        label = ""
   258→        for lbl in g_mocho.objects(subj, RDFS.label):
   259→            if getattr(lbl, "language", None) == "en":
   260→                label = str(lbl)
   261→                break
   262→        if not label:
   263→            for lbl in g_mocho.objects(subj, RDFS.label):
   264→                label = str(lbl)
   265→                break
   266→        mocho_rda[iri] = label
   267→
   268→print(f"  {len(mocho_rda)} RDA properties in mocho")
   269→
   270→
   271→def wemi_level(iri: str) -> str:
   272→    for prefix, level in WEMI_LEVELS.items():
   273→        if iri.startswith(prefix):
   274→            return level
   275→    return ""
   276→
   277→
   278→# ── step 5: align ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   279→
   280→print("\nAligning …")
   281→
   282→# Build reverse map: full IRI → prefixed dct_term label
   283→# e.g. "http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/creator" → ["dc:creator", "dcterms:creator"]
   284→iri_to_dct: dict[str, list[str]] = defaultdict(list)
   285→for term in dct_to_rda:
   286→    prefix, local = term.split(":")
   287→    if prefix == "dc":
   288→        ns = str(DC)
   289→    elif prefix == "dcterms":
   290→        ns = str(DCTERMS)
   291→    else:
   292→        continue
   293→    full_iri = ns + local
   294→    iri_to_dct[full_iri].append(term)
   295→
   296→rows = []
   297→summary_matched = 0
   298→summary_unmatched = 0
   299→unmatched_keys = []
   300→
   301→for (entity_type, json_key), field_row in sorted(data_fields.items()):
   302→    record_count = field_row["record_count"]
   303→    coverage_pct = round(100 * record_count / total_records, 2)
   304→
   305→    resolved_pair = field_iris.get((entity_type, json_key))
   306→    if resolved_pair is None:
   307→        edm_prefix = ""
   308→        edm_iri = ""
   309→    else:
   310→        edm_prefix, edm_iri = resolved_pair
   311→
   312→    # Skip structural/internal keys
   313→    if json_key in ("about",):
   314→        continue
   315→
   316→    # Find RDA candidates via DCT→RDA map
   317→    rda_candidates = []
   318→    match_method = "unmatched"
   319→
   320→    if edm_iri:
   321→        # Check direct match in DCT map
   322→        dct_terms = iri_to_dct.get(edm_iri, [])
   323→        for dct_term in dct_terms:
   324→            for rda_iri, rda_label in dct_to_rda.get(dct_term, []):
   325→                if rda_iri in mocho_rda:
   326→                    rda_candidates.append((rda_iri, mocho_rda[rda_iri], "via_dct_map"))
   327→                else:
   328→                    rda_candidates.append((rda_iri, rda_label, "via_dct_map_not_in_mocho"))
   329→
   330→        # If no DCT match, check if the EDM IRI is directly in mocho
   331→        if not rda_candidates and edm_iri in mocho_rda:
   332→            rda_candidates.append((edm_iri, mocho_rda[edm_iri], "direct"))
   333→
   334→    if rda_candidates:
   335→        # Separate into mocho-present and mocho-absent
   336→        in_mocho   = [(i, l, m) for i, l, m in rda_candidates if "not_in_mocho" not in m]
   337→        not_mocho  = [(i, l, m) for i, l, m in rda_candidates if "not_in_mocho" in m]
   338→
   339→        for rda_iri, rda_label, method in in_mocho:
   340→            rows.append({
   341→                "entity_type":   entity_type,
   342→                "json_key":      json_key,
   343→                "edm_prefix":    edm_prefix,
   344→                "edm_iri":       edm_iri,
   345→                "record_count":  record_count,
   346→                "coverage_pct":  coverage_pct,
   347→                "rda_iri":       rda_iri,
   348→                "rda_label":     rda_label,
   349→                "wemi_level":    wemi_level(rda_iri),
   350→                "match_method":  method,
   351→                "in_mocho":      True,
   352→            })
   353→        summary_matched += 1
   354→
   355→        # Also record any RDA candidates not yet in mocho (useful diagnostic)
   356→        for rda_iri, rda_label, method in not_mocho:
   357→            rows.append({
   358→                "entity_type":   entity_type,
   359→                "json_key":      json_key,
   360→                "edm_prefix":    edm_prefix,
   361→                "edm_iri":       edm_iri,
   362→                "record_count":  record_count,
   363→                "coverage_pct":  coverage_pct,
   364→                "rda_iri":       rda_iri,
   365→                "rda_label":     rda_label,
   366→                "wemi_level":    wemi_level(rda_iri),
   367→                "match_method":  "via_dct_map_not_in_mocho",
   368→                "in_mocho":      False,
   369→            })
   370→    else:
   371→        rows.append({
   372→            "entity_type":   entity_type,
   373→            "json_key":      json_key,
   374→            "edm_prefix":    edm_prefix,
   375→            "edm_iri":       edm_iri,
   376→            "record_count":  record_count,
   377→            "coverage_pct":  coverage_pct,
   378→            "rda_iri":       "",
   379→            "rda_label":     "",
   380→            "wemi_level":    "",
   381→            "match_method":  "unmatched",
   382→            "in_mocho":      False,
   383→        })
   384→        summary_unmatched += 1
   385→        unmatched_keys.append(f"{entity_type}.{json_key} [{edm_iri}]")
   386→
   387→# ── step 5b: align controlled vocabulary Concepts ─────────────────────────────
   388→# Sector and media type are encoded as edm:Concept entities identified by
   389→# their about IRI.  The main loop skips Concept.about; we handle it here.
   390→
   391→print("\nAligning controlled vocabulary Concepts …")
   392→
   393→# Resolve media type RDA label from mocho (falls back to hard-coded)
   394→mt_rda_label = mocho_rda.get(MEDIENTYP_RDA_IRI, "has content type")
   395→mt_rda_in_mocho = MEDIENTYP_RDA_IRI in mocho_rda
   396→
   397→vocab_rows: list[dict] = []
   398→
   399→# Sector: DDB institutional sector classification.
   400→# No direct RDA bibliographic property; recorded as concept_vocab_sector
   401→# so downstream can use it for faceting / provenance filtering.
   402→for iri, label in SPARTE_VOCAB.items():
   403→    vocab_rows.append({
   404→        "entity_type":  "Concept",
   405→        "json_key":     "about",
   406→        "edm_prefix":   "ddb-sparte",
   407→        "edm_iri":      iri,
   408→        "record_count": "",
   409→        "coverage_pct": "",
   410→        "rda_iri":      "",
   411→        "rda_label":    "",
   412→        "wemi_level":   "",
   413→        "match_method": "concept_vocab_sector",
   414→        "in_mocho":     False,
   415→        "vocab_label":  label,
   416→    })
   417→
   418→# Media type: DDB media type classification → rdae:P20001 (has content type)
   419→# Parallel to ProvidedCHO.dcType — both classify the intellectual content kind.
   420→for iri, label in MEDIENTYP_VOCAB.items():
   421→    vocab_rows.append({
   422→        "entity_type":  "Concept",
   423→        "json_key":     "about",
   424→        "edm_prefix":   "ddb-medientyp",
   425→        "edm_iri":      iri,
   426→        "record_count": "",
   427→        "coverage_pct": "",
   428→        "rda_iri":      MEDIENTYP_RDA_IRI if mt_rda_in_mocho else "",
   429→        "rda_label":    mt_rda_label if mt_rda_in_mocho else "",
   430→        "wemi_level":   wemi_level(MEDIENTYP_RDA_IRI),
   431→        "match_method": "concept_vocab_mediatype",
   432→        "in_mocho":     mt_rda_in_mocho,
   433→        "vocab_label":  label,
   434→    })
   435→
   436→rows.extend(vocab_rows)
   437→print(f"  {len(SPARTE_VOCAB)} sector entries, {len(MEDIENTYP_VOCAB)} media type entries")
   438→
   439→# Backfill vocab_label on rows produced by the main alignment loop
   440→for row in rows:
   441→    row.setdefault("vocab_label", "")
   442→
   443→# ── step 6: write outputs ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   444→
   445→OUT_CSV.parent.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
   446→
   447→fieldnames = [
   448→    "entity_type", "json_key", "edm_prefix", "edm_iri",
   449→    "record_count", "coverage_pct",
   450→    "rda_iri", "rda_label", "wemi_level", "match_method", "in_mocho",
   451→    "vocab_label",
   452→]
   453→
   454→with open(OUT_CSV, "w", newline="", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   455→    writer = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=fieldnames)
   456→    writer.writeheader()
   457→    writer.writerows(rows)
   458→
   459→# Group for JSON: per (entity_type, json_key) → list of RDA mappings.
   460→# Vocab rows (concept_vocab_*) are routed to vocab_concept_alignments instead.
   461→groups: dict[str, dict] = {}
   462→vocab_groups: dict[str, list] = {"sector": [], "media_type": []}
   463→
   464→for row in rows:
   465→    if row["match_method"].startswith("concept_vocab_"):
   466→        domain = "sector" if row["match_method"] == "concept_vocab_sector" else "media_type"
   467→        vocab_groups[domain].append({
   468→            "iri":        row["edm_iri"],
   469→            "label":      row["vocab_label"],
   470→            "rda_iri":    row["rda_iri"],
   471→            "rda_label":  row["rda_label"],
   472→            "wemi_level": row["wemi_level"],
   473→            "in_mocho":   row["in_mocho"],
   474→        })
   475→        continue
   476→
   477→    key = f"{row['entity_type']}.{row['json_key']}"
   478→    if key not in groups:
   479→        groups[key] = {
   480→            "entity_type":  row["entity_type"],
   481→            "json_key":     row["json_key"],
   482→            "edm_prefix":   row["edm_prefix"],
   483→            "edm_iri":      row["edm_iri"],
   484→            "record_count": row["record_count"],
   485→            "coverage_pct": row["coverage_pct"],
   486→            "rda_mappings": [],
   487→        }
   488→    if row["rda_iri"]:
   489→        groups[key]["rda_mappings"].append({
   490→            "rda_iri":      row["rda_iri"],
   491→            "rda_label":    row["rda_label"],
   492→            "wemi_level":   row["wemi_level"],
   493→            "match_method": row["match_method"],
   494→            "in_mocho":     row["in_mocho"],
   495→        })
   496→
   497→output = {
   498→    "total_records": total_records,
   499→    "summary": {
   500→        "data_fields_with_rda_mapping": summary_matched,
   501→        "data_fields_unmatched":        summary_unmatched,
   502→        "total_alignment_rows":         len(rows),
   503→        "unmatched_fields":             sorted(unmatched_keys),
   504→    },
   505→    "alignment": list(groups.values()),
   506→    "vocab_concept_alignments": vocab_groups,
   507→}
   508→
   509→with open(OUT_JSON, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   510→    json.dump(output, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
   511→
   512→# ── print summary ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   513→
   514→print(f"\n{'='*60}")
   515→print(f"Data fields with RDA mapping : {summary_matched}")
   516→print(f"Data fields unmatched        : {summary_unmatched}")
   517→print(f"Sector vocab entries         : {len(vocab_groups['sector'])}")
   518→print(f"Media type vocab entries     : {len(vocab_groups['media_type'])}")
   519→print(f"Total alignment rows         : {len(rows)}")
   520→
   521→print("\nUnmatched fields:")
   522→for key in sorted(unmatched_keys):
   523→    print(f"  {key}")
   524→
   525→print(f"\nSaved {OUT_CSV}")
   526→print(f"Saved {OUT_JSON}")
   527→



```

{
  "command": "python3 -c \"\nimport csv\nrows = list(csv.DictReader(open('/Users/mta/Documents/claude/goethe-faust/output/alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv')))\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\n# Show all unmatched (entity_type, json_key) pairs with their edm_iri\nunmatched = [(r['entity_type'], r['json_key'], r['edm_iri'], r['record_count']) \n             for r in rows if r['in_mocho']=='False']\nprint('=== UNMATCHED FIELDS ===')\nfor et, jk, iri, rc in sorted(unmatched, key=lambda x: -int(x[3]) if x[3] else 0):\n    print(f'  {et}.{jk} [{iri}] rc={rc}')\n\nprint()\n# Show all 32 matched fields with record_count\nprint('=== MATCHED FIELDS (unique entity_type.json_key) ===')\nseen = set()\nmatched_fields = []\nfor r in rows:\n    if r['in_mocho']=='True':\n        key = (r['entity_type'], r['json_key'])\n        if key not in seen:\n            seen.add(key)\n            matched_fields.append((r['entity_type'], r['json_key'], r['edm_iri'], r['record_count'], r['coverage_pct']))\nfor et, jk, iri, rc, pct in sorted(matched_fields, key=lambda x: -int(x[3]) if x[3] else 0):\n    print(f'  {et}.{jk} [{iri}] rc={rc} ({pct}%)')\n\"\n"
}

{
  "command": "python3 -c \"\nimport csv\nrows = list(csv.DictReader(open('/Users/mta/Documents/claude/goethe-faust/output/alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv')))\n# Fan-out per field\nfrom collections import defaultdict, Counter\n\nfanout = defaultdict(int)\nfor r in rows:\n    if r['in_mocho']=='True':\n        fanout[(r['entity_type'], r['json_key'])] += 1\nprint('=== FAN-OUT per (entity_type, json_key) for in_mocho=True ===')\nfor (et, jk), count in sorted(fanout.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):\n    print(f'  {et}.{jk}: {count} candidates')\nprint()\n\n# WEMI breakdown for ProvidedCHO only\nprint('=== ProvidedCHO matched fields, WEMI level breakdown ===')\ncho_wemi = defaultdict(Counter)\nfor r in rows:\n    if r['in_mocho']=='True' and r['entity_type']=='ProvidedCHO':\n        cho_wemi[r['json_key']][r['wemi_level']] += 1\nfor jk, wemi_counts in sorted(cho_wemi.items(), key=lambda x: -sum(x[1].values())):\n    print(f'  {jk}: {dict(wemi_counts)}')\n\"\n"
}
