### User *(2026-04-15T16:52:34.263Z)*

**Tool result**
```
     1→# Implementation Plan: `transform_edm_to_mocho.py`
     2→
     3→## Context
     4→
     5→All design decisions are finalised in `goethe-faust/notes/alignment-plan.md` (approved)
     6→and `goethe-faust/notes/alignment-adr.md` (D1–D10). This is the reference implementation
     7→of the mocho ingest transformation — validated on the goethe-faust corpus (115,432 records)
     8→and intended as the canonical basis for all subsequent mocho corpus transforms.
     9→The script streams with constant memory, stdlib only.
    10→
    11→---
    12→
    13→## Critical files
    14→
    15→| Path | Role |
    16→|---|---|
    17→| `goethe-faust/scripts/transform_edm_to_mocho.py` | **New script** |
    18→| `goethe-faust/data/items-all-goethe-faust.json` | Source JSONL |
    19→| `goethe-faust/data/ids-all-goethe-faust.txt` | ID filter set |
    20→| `goethe-faust/output/alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv` | Alignment table |
    21→| `goethe-faust/output/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv` | htype → DoCO/RiC-O |
    22→| `goethe-faust/output/mocho-goethe-faust.nt` | **New output** — N-Triples pipeline intermediate |
    23→| `goethe-faust/output/mocho-goethe-faust.jsonld` | **New output** — JSON-LD for inspection/tooling |
    24→| `goethe-faust/output/transform_stats.json` | **New output** — run stats + ignored triples inventory |
    25→| `goethe-faust/scripts/README.md` | Must be updated |
    26→
    27→---
    28→
    29→## Implementation
    30→
    31→### 1. Header block
    32→
    33→```
    34→Purpose:    Transform DDB-EDM JSONL records to mocho-aligned RDF triples (N-Triples + JSON-LD)
    35→Usage:      python transform_edm_to_mocho.py [--jsonl FILE] [--ids FILE] [--out-nt FILE]
    36→            [--out-jsonld FILE] [--stats FILE] [--limit N]
    37→Inputs:     items-all-goethe-faust.json, ids-all-goethe-faust.txt,
    38→            alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv, lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv
    39→Outputs:    mocho-goethe-faust.nt, mocho-goethe-faust.jsonld, transform_stats.json
    40→Deps:       stdlib only (json, csv, re, collections, argparse, pathlib)
    41→Assumes:    JSONL one JSON object per line; edm.RDF.* structure per alignment-ddbedm-mocho.md
    42→```
    43→
    44→### 2. Constants
    45→
    46→```python
    47→CHO_BASE             = "http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de/item/"
    48→RDF_TYPE             = "http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type"
    49→MOCHO_NS             = "https://ise-fizkarlsruhe.github.io/ddbkg/mocho#"
    50→MOCHO_MANIFESTATION  = MOCHO_NS + "Manifestation"
    51→RICO_RST             = "http://www.ica.org/standards/RiC/ontology#hasRecordSetType"
    52→CREATOR_IRI          = "http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/m/P30263"   # has creator agent of manifestation
    53→CONTRIBUTOR_IRI      = "http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/contributor" # kept as-is; no generic RDA equiv in mocho
    54→SUBJECT_KEYS         = {"dcSubject", "dcTermsSubject", "dcTermSubject"}
    55→
    56→# Prefix expansion for load_htype_map
    57→PREFIXES = {
    58→    "doco":    "http://purl.org/spar/doco/",
    59→    "rico":    "http://www.ica.org/standards/RiC/ontology#",
    60→    "ric-rst": "http://www.ica.org/standards/RiC/vocabularies/recordSetTypes#",
    61→    "mocho":   MOCHO_NS,
    62→}
    63→```
    64→
    65→### 3. `load_alignment(path)` → `dict[tuple[str,str], list[dict]]`
    66→
    67→- Read `alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv` with `csv.DictReader`
    68→- Include row iff `row['in_mocho'] == 'True'` and `row['rda_iri']` non-empty
    69→  (string comparison — `in_mocho` is not a Python bool)
    70→- Key: `(row['entity_type'], row['json_key'])`
    71→- Value: list of `{'rda_iri': ..., 'rda_label': ..., 'wemi_level': ..., 'match_method': ...}`
    72→
    73→### 4. `load_htype_map(path)` → `dict[str, tuple[str, list[str]]]`
    74→
    75→- Read `lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv`
    76→- Skip rows where `rdf_type == 'pending'` or `rdf_type` empty
    77→- Key: `row['htype_code']` (e.g. `"htype_030"`)
    78→- Value: `(rdf_type_iri, [rst_iri, ...])` where:
    79→  - `rdf_type_iri` = expand prefix → full IRI using PREFIXES dict
    80→  - `[rst_iri, ...]` = split `row['has_record_set_type']` on `', '`, expand each
    81→    prefix → full IRI; empty list if column is blank
    82→  - **Multi-value**: `has_record_set_type` can be comma-separated
    83→    (e.g. `"mocho:Bestand, ric-rst:Fonds"`) → emit one `rico:hasRecordSetType`
    84→    triple per individual
    85→
    86→### 5. `load_ids(path)` → `set[str]`
    87→
    88→- One 32-char ID per line; strip whitespace
    89→
    90→### 6. `get_object_id(record)` → `str | None`
    91→
    92→- `record['edm']['RDF']['ProvidedCHO']['about']`
    93→- Split on `/`, return last segment (32-char ID)
    94→- Return `None` if path missing or pattern mismatch
    95→
    96→### 7. `value_to_nt_obj(val)` → `list[str]`
    97→
    98→Handle all value shapes, returning valid NT object strings:
    99→
   100→| Input shape | Output |
   101→|---|---|
   102→| `None` | `[]` |
   103→| `""` (empty string) | `[]` |
   104→| `str s` (non-empty) | `['"s"']` with `\` and `"` escaped |
   105→| `list` | recurse on each element, flatten |
   106→| `{"resource": IRI, ...}` where IRI non-null/non-empty | `["<IRI>"]` |
   107→| `{"resource": null/empty, "lang": L, "$": text}` where L non-null, text non-empty | `['"text"@L']` with escaping |
   108→| `{"resource": null/empty, "lang": null, "$": text}` where text non-empty | `['"text"']` with escaping |
   109→| `{"resource": null/empty, "$": ""}` | `[]` |
   110→
   111→Escaping: replace `\` → `\\`, then `"` → `\"` in literal strings.
   112→
   113→### 8. `emit_subject_triples(entity, subject_nt, alignment)` → `list[str]`
   114→
   115→- `lit_candidates = alignment.get(("ProvidedCHO", "dcSubject"), [])`
   116→- `iri_candidates = alignment.get(("ProvidedCHO", "dcTermSubject"), [])`
   117→- `seen: set[tuple[str,str]]` for per-entity dedup
   118→- For each key in `SUBJECT_KEYS`: get raw val, call `value_to_nt_obj`
   119→- For each obj_nt: if `obj_nt.startswith("<")` → use `iri_candidates`, else `lit_candidates`
   120→- Only append `(pred_nt, obj_nt)` if not in `seen`
   121→
   122→### 9. `retype_entities(rdf, htype_map)` → `list[str]`
   123→
   124→**ProvidedCHO** (single dict):
   125→1. Always emit `<cho_uri> <rdf:type> <mocho:Manifestation> .`
   126→2. Get `htype_code = entity.get('hierarchyType')`; look up in `htype_map`
   127→3. If found: emit `<cho_uri> <rdf:type> <rdf_type_iri> .`
   128→4. For each rst_iri in list: emit `<cho_uri> <rico:hasRecordSetType> <rst_iri> .`
   129→
   130→**PhysicalThing** (array, may be absent/None):
   131→1. For each entity in array: get `htype_code`, look up in `htype_map`
   132→2. If found: emit `<uri> <rdf:type> <rdf_type_iri> .`
   133→3. For each rst_iri: emit `<uri> <rico:hasRecordSetType> <rst_iri> .`
   134→4. If not found (absent or pending): emit nothing (no fallback type)
   135→
   136→### 10. `transform_record(record, alignment, htype_map, ids_filter)` → `list[str]`
   137→
   138→```
   139→get_object_id → filter against ids_filter (skip if not in set)
   140→rdf = record['edm']['RDF']
   141→for entity_type, entities in rdf.items():
   142→    normalise to list (ProvidedCHO/Aggregation are dicts, not arrays)
   143→    for entity in entities:
   144→        subject = entity.get('about') — skip if None
   145→        if entity_type == 'ProvidedCHO':
   146→            emit_subject_triples(...)
   147→        for json_key, raw_val in entity.items():
   148→            skip 'about', None values
   149→            skip SUBJECT_KEYS for ProvidedCHO (handled above)
   150→            if ProvidedCHO and json_key in ('creator','contributor'):
   151→                emit single triple with CREATOR_IRI / CONTRIBUTOR_IRI; continue
   152→            candidates = alignment.get((entity_type, json_key), [])
   153→            for row in candidates:
   154→                emit triples via value_to_nt_obj
   155→retype_entities(rdf, htype_map)  # appended after entity loop
   156→```
   157→
   158→Track per-record stats: `records_processed`, `triples_out`, `unmatched_keys` Counter
   159→(key = `"EntityType.json_key"` for any key with no alignment candidates and not
   160→explicitly whitelisted/skipped).
   161→
   162→### 11. JSON-LD output
   163→
   164→JSON-LD is written as a streaming array — one object per ProvidedCHO subject.
   165→Built per-record from the same `nt_lines` output using a helper:
   166→
   167→```python
   168→def nt_lines_to_jsonld_node(nt_lines: list[str]) -> dict:
   169→    # Group triples by subject → {subject: {predicate: [objects]}}
   170→    # For each subject, produce a JSON-LD node dict:
   171→    #   "@id": subject_iri
   172→    #   predicate_iri: [{"@id": obj} or {"@value": lit, "@language": lang}]
   173→    # Return list of node dicts (one per subject in this record's triples)
   174→```
   175→
   176→Write strategy: open JSON-LD file at start, write `[\n`; after each record
   177→write the node object(s) as `  {...},\n`; strip trailing comma and write `]\n`
   178→at end. No full in-memory accumulation.
   179→
   180→JSON-LD context is minimal — no prefix compaction in this pass (full IRIs).
   181→A separate `@context` block with prefix declarations is prepended.
   182→
   183→### 12. `main()`
   184→
   185→- `argparse`: `--jsonl`, `--ids`, `--out-nt`, `--out-jsonld`, `--stats`, `--limit`
   186→- Default paths relative to script location
   187→- Stream: `for lineno, line in enumerate(in_fh)`
   188→- Write both `.nt` and `.jsonld` simultaneously per record
   189→- Write stats to JSON on completion
   190→
   191→### 13. Stats output (`transform_stats.json`)
   192→
   193→```json
   194→{
   195→  "records_processed": 115432,
   196→  "records_skipped_not_in_ids": ...,
   197→  "triples_out": ...,
   198→  "triples_ignored": ...,
   199→  "objects_missing_specific_type": ...,
   200→  "whitelisted_keys": {"creator": "rdam:P30263", "contributor": "dc:contributor"},
   201→  "ignored_properties": {
   202→    "Aggregation.edmRights":         {"count": 115432, "edm_iri": "..."},
   203→    "Aggregation.isShownAt":         {"count": 115432, "edm_iri": "..."},
   204→    "ProvidedCHO.hasType":           {"count": 97272,  "edm_iri": "..."},
   205→    "...": "..."
   206→  }
   207→}
   208→```
   209→
   210→`triples_ignored` = total value-level triple candidates skipped (sum of `count`
   211→across `ignored_properties`). `ignored_properties` is a full inventory — every
   212→`EntityType.json_key` that had at least one value in the corpus but zero
   213→alignment candidates — including EDM-structural fields (D4), SKOS labels, geo
   214→coords, etc. Sorted by count descending. Each entry records `edm_iri` from the
   215→alignment table where available (for cross-reference), else `null`.
   216→
   217→---
   218→
   219→## One stale comment to fix in alignment-plan.md §3.5.4
   220→
   221→The inline comment in the creator/contributor block still says
   222→`rdaw:P10065 (has creator agent of work)` — should read
   223→`rdam:P30263 (has creator agent of manifestation)`. Fix while writing the script.
   224→
   225→---
   226→
   227→## Verification (per alignment-plan.md §6)
   228→
   229→1. Run full pass; check `triples_out / records_processed` ≈ 20–80
   230→2. Spot-check one object per sector × mediatype — every ProvidedCHO has
   231→   `mocho:Manifestation` rdf:type; mapped htype adds DoCO/RiC-O type
   232→3. Grep NT for `europeana.eu` predicates — expect zero
   233→4. Grep NT for `edm:ProvidedCHO` as rdf:type object — expect zero
   234→5. Check PhysicalThing URIs in NT have RiC-O types (e.g. `rico:RecordSet`)
   235→6. Confirm `dc:contributor` appears as predicate; `rdam:P30263` appears as predicate
   236→7. Update `goethe-faust/scripts/README.md`
   237→



```

**Tool result**
```
     1→# Scripts
     2→
     3→All scripts live in `scripts/`. They use project-relative paths via
     4→`pathlib.Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent`, so they can be run from
     5→any working directory.
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## Ontology alignment (run in order)
    10→
    11→### `profile_edm_fields.py`
    12→Profiles all field keys present under `edm.RDF.*` entity types in the JSONL
    13→data file. Needed as input for `align_ddbedm_to_mocho.py`.
    14→
    15→- **Input**: `data/items-all-goethe-faust.json`
    16→- **Output**: `output/edm_field_profile.json`, `output/edm_field_profile.csv`
    17→- **Usage**: `python3 scripts/profile_edm_fields.py`
    18→- **Notes**: Reports per-entity-type field names with record counts and
    19→  coverage percentages. Counts > 100% indicate array-valued entities
    20→  (multiple agents/concepts/events per record).
    21→
    22→### `align_ddbedm_to_mocho.py`
    23→Data-driven ontology alignment from DDB-EDM to mocho. Maps each EDM/DC
    24→property actually present in the data to its corresponding RDA properties
    25→in mocho, via the DC→RDA sub-property mapping from the mocho workflow.
    26→
    27→- **Input**: `output/edm_field_profile.json` (from above),
    28→  `~/Documents/claude/mocho/ontology/ddbedm_1.0.ttl`,
    29→  `~/Documents/claude/mocho/mocho-odk/src/ontology/mocho-full.owl`,
    30→  `~/Documents/claude/mocho/output/mapping_dct_to_rda.csv`
    31→- **Output**: `output/alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv`,
    32→  `output/alignment_ddbedm_mocho.json`
    33→- **Usage**: `python3 scripts/align_ddbedm_to_mocho.py`
    34→- **Dependencies**: `rdflib`
    35→- **Notes**:
    36→  - One CSV row per (edm_field × rda_property) pair; high-fanout DC terms
    37→    (e.g. `dc:creator` → 232 RDA sub-properties) produce many rows per field
    38→  - 32 DC/DCT fields matched to RDA properties in mocho (1,271 alignment rows)
    39→  - 55 unmatched fields fall into expected categories: EDM-structural
    40→    (`edm:isShownAt`, `edm:begin/end`), DDB extensions (`ddb:hierarchyType`,
    41→    `ddb:aggregationEntity`), SKOS labels, geo coordinates, and two known
    42→    DCT→RDA gaps (`dc:identifier`, `dcterms:spatial`)
    43→  - `dcTermSubject` (DDB data variant of `dcterms:subject`) handled via
    44→    hard-coded override in `OVERRIDES` dict
    45→
    46→---
    47→
    48→## Data pipeline (run in order)
    49→
    50→### `fetch-search-all.py`
    51→Fetches all DDB search results for the query "goethe" via the DDB Solr API
    52→and merges them into a single JSON file.
    53→
    54→- **Input**: DDB Solr API (live network access required)
    55→- **Output**: `data/ddb-search-goethe-all.json`
    56→- **Usage**: `python scripts/fetch-search-all.py`
    57→- **Notes**: `TOTAL` must match the actual result count; update if the
    58→  dataset changes. 0.3 s delay between requests to avoid rate-limiting.
    59→
    60→### `build_dataframe.py`
    61→Builds a flat per-object DataFrame from the raw JSONL.
    62→
    63→- **Input**: `data/items-all-goethe-faust.json`
    64→- **Output**: `output/items-dataframe.parquet` (115,398 × 10, 8.6 MB),
    65→  `output/items-dataframe-sample.csv` (first 500 rows)
    66→- **Columns**: `object_id`, `sector`, `provider_name`, `timespan_begin`,
    67→  `timespan_end`, `dc_type` (list), `dc_subject` (list), `metadata_format`,
    68→  `view_fields` (list), `digitized` (bool)
    69→- **Usage**: `python scripts/build_dataframe.py`
    70→- **Dependencies**: `pandas`, `pyarrow`
    71→- **Notes**:
    72→  - `YEAR_RE` handles both free-text years and compact YYYYMMDD dates
    73→    (e.g. `18300213`) via two regex alternatives
    74→  - `timespan_begin` priority: `edm.RDF.TimeSpan.begin` →
    75→    `edm.RDF.TimeSpan.end` → `edm.RDF.ProvidedCHO.issued` (fallback)
    76→  - After fixes: 12,958 records (~11.2%) still lack a `timespan_begin` year
    77→
    78→---
    79→
    80→## Analysis scripts
    81→
    82→### `analyse_bucket.py`
    83→Reports top N dc:type and dc:subject values for records within a given
    84→`timespan_begin` year range. Reads from the parquet DataFrame.
    85→
    86→- **Input**: `output/items-dataframe.parquet`
    87→- **Output**: printed summary (or JSON with `--json`)
    88→- **Usage**:
    89→  ```
    90→  python scripts/analyse_bucket.py --start 2000 --end 2024
    91→  python scripts/analyse_bucket.py --start 2000 --end 2024 --top 10 --json
    92→  ```
    93→- **Dependencies**: `pandas`, `pyarrow`
    94→- **Notes**: Useful for characterising anomalous buckets in `fig_years.png`
    95→  (e.g. the 2000–2024 spike is dominated by Goethe-Universität Frankfurt
    96→  institutional records — theses and working papers — rather than cultural
    97→  heritage items)
    98→
    99→### `analyse_items.py`
   100→Aggregates item-level statistics across 6 dimensions from the raw JSONL.
   101→
   102→- **Input**: `data/items-all-goethe-faust.json`
   103→- **Output**: `output/items-analysis.json`
   104→- **Usage**: `python scripts/analyse_items.py`
   105→
   106→### `analyse_years.py`
   107→Extracts creation years from `edm.RDF.TimeSpan`, selects an optimal bucket
   108→size, and produces a bar chart. X-range restricted to 1600–present; linear
   109→y-scale.
   110→
   111→- **Input**: `data/items-all-goethe-faust.json`
   112→- **Output**: `output/fig_years.png`, `output/years-analysis.json`
   113→- **Usage**: `python scripts/analyse_years.py`
   114→- **Notes**:
   115→  - `YEAR_RE` handles YYYYMMDD dates; 85.8% of records have a usable year
   116→  - Auto-selects bucket size (5 / 10 / 25 / 50 / 100 yr) to keep ≤ 45 bins;
   117→    25-year buckets selected (18 non-empty bins from 1600+)
   118→  - Pre-1600 records (n = 2,551) omitted from chart; included in JSON
   119→  - Goethe era (1749–1832) highlighted in red
   120→  - Bar counts: inside bars (white) for tall bars; above for short bars
   121→
   122→### `audit_timespan_coverage.py`
   123→Audits temporal coverage: for records missing a `TimeSpan` year, checks
   124→whether `dc:date`, `dc:issued` (and dcterms equivalents) in `ProvidedCHO`
   125→could provide a fallback year. Also reports all `ProvidedCHO` keys present
   126→in those records, to identify further date-bearing fields.
   127→
   128→- **Input**: `data/items-all-goethe-faust.json`
   129→- **Output**: printed summary (no files written)
   130→- **Usage**: `python scripts/audit_timespan_coverage.py`
   131→- **Notes**: Led to discovery that `YEAR_RE` was broken for YYYYMMDD dates
   132→  and that `issued` is a viable fallback; both fixes applied to
   133→  `build_dataframe.py` and `analyse_years.py`
   134→
   135→### `match_objecttypes.py`
   136→Maps DDB objecttype strings to FaBiO / DoCO ontology classes using a
   137→4-tier pipeline: exact match → translated exact match → Levenshtein →
   138→sentence embeddings.
   139→
   140→- **Input**: `data/ddb-search-goethe-all.json`, `data/schemas/fabio.owl`,
   141→  `data/schemas/doco.owl`
   142→- **Output**: `output/ddb-type2fabio.json`
   143→- **Usage**: `python scripts/match_objecttypes.py`
   144→- **Dependencies**: `deep-translator`, `rapidfuzz`, `sentence-transformers`,
   145→  `rdflib`
   146→
   147→### `summarise_results.py`
   148→Prints a human-readable summary of `ddb-type2fabio.json`.
   149→
   150→- **Input**: `output/ddb-type2fabio.json`
   151→- **Output**: printed summary (no files written)
   152→- **Usage**: `python scripts/summarise_results.py`
   153→
   154→---
   155→
   156→## Visualisation scripts
   157→
   158→### `visualise_items.py`
   159→Generates figures from `items-analysis.json` (German labels, pre-translation).
   160→
   161→- **Input**: `output/items-analysis.json`
   162→- **Output**: `output/fig1_metadata_format.png`, `output/fig2_sparte.png`,
   163→  `output/fig3_providers_top20.png`, `output/fig4_dc_type_top20.png`,
   164→  `output/fig5_dc_subject_top30.png`, `output/fig6_view_fields_top20.png`
   165→- **Usage**: `python scripts/visualise_items.py`
   166→- **Notes**: Labels remain in German. Use `translate_and_plot.py` or
   167→  `plot_latex_figs.py` for English-translated versions.
   168→
   169→### `translate_and_plot.py`
   170→Translates German labels with Helsinki-NLP/opus-mt-de-en and regenerates
   171→all figures with English labels. Reads from the parquet DataFrame.
   172→
   173→- **Input**: `output/items-dataframe.parquet`
   174→- **Output**: `output/fig2_sector.png`, `output/fig4_dc_type_top20.png`,
   175→  `output/fig5_dc_subject_top30.png`, `output/fig6_view_fields_top20.png`,
   176→  `output/dataset-summary.png`
   177→- **Usage**:
   178→  ```
   179→  HF_HOME=data/hf-cache HF_HUB_DISABLE_XET=1 python scripts/translate_and_plot.py
   180→  ```
   181→- **Dependencies**: `transformers`, `pandas`, `pyarrow`, `matplotlib`
   182→- **Notes**: Model cached in `data/hf-cache/`. Manual overrides in `OVERRIDES`
   183→  dict for domain-specific terms (Hochschulschrift, Druckgraphik, etc.)
   184→
   185→### `plot_latex_figs.py`
   186→Regenerates 4 figures as square 7×7 in PNGs (1050×1050 px) for a LaTeX
   187→2×2 subfigure layout. Translates German labels via opus-mt-de-en.
   188→
   189→- **Input**: `output/items-dataframe.parquet`
   190→- **Output**: `output/fig1_metadata_format.png`, `output/fig2_sector.png`,
   191→  `output/fig4_dc_type_top20.png`, `output/fig5_dc_subject_top20.png`
   192→- **Usage**:
   193→  ```
   194→  HF_HOME=data/hf-cache HF_HUB_DISABLE_XET=1 python scripts/plot_latex_figs.py
   195→  ```
   196→- **Dependencies**: `transformers`, `pandas`, `pyarrow`, `matplotlib`
   197→- **Notes**: `fig5` is top-20 subjects (not top-30). `fig1` uses explicit
   198→  subplot margins (not `bbox_inches="tight"`) to preserve square aspect ratio.
   199→
   200→### `extract_view_id_name.py`
   201→Extracts unique `(id, name)` pairs from `view.fields` across all items in a
   202→JSON or NDJSON file.
   203→
   204→- **Input**: any JSON array or NDJSON file of item objects
   205→- **Output**: JSON array of `[id, name]` tuples, one per line, sorted by id
   206→- **Usage**: `python scripts/extract_view_id_name.py <input> <output>`
   207→- **Example**:
   208→  ```
   209→  python scripts/extract_view_id_name.py data/items-all-goethe-faust.json output/view_id_name.json
   210→  ```
   211→
   212→### `extract_view_fields.py`
   213→Extracts and pretty-prints `view.fields` for a single item by ID.
   214→
   215→- **Input**: `data/items/<item-id>.json`
   216→- **Output**: `view-<item-id>.json` (project root)
   217→- **Usage**: `python scripts/extract_view_fields.py <item-id>`
   218→- **Example**:
   219→  ```
   220→  python scripts/extract_view_fields.py 222SF6AM6ZCXSJTNREHJUCX5SRTJDBK7
   221→  ```
   222→
   223→---
   224→
   225→## Data-collection scripts
   226→
   227→### `fetch-items.sh`
   228→Fetches DDB item records by ID from the DDB API, saves each as an individual
   229→JSON file, and appends compact single-line JSON to the JSONL file. Skips IDs
   230→already present in the JSONL. Must be run from the `data/` directory.
   231→
   232→- **Input**: `<ids-file>` (one ID per line), optional `[limit]` count
   233→- **Output**: `data/items/<uuid>.json` per record; appends to
   234→  `data/items-all-goethe-faust.json`
   235→- **Usage**:
   236→  ```
   237→  cd data && bash ../scripts/fetch-items.sh ids-all-goethe-faust.txt
   238→  cd data && bash ../scripts/fetch-items.sh ids-missing.txt   # re-fetch gap
   239→  ```
   240→- **Notes**: 0.2 s delay between requests. HTTP failures are logged but do not
   241→  abort the run; failed IDs can be re-fetched by running again.
   242→
   243→### `find_missing_items.py`
   244→Compares `ids-all-goethe-faust.txt` against `items-all-goethe-faust.json` and
   245→writes the gap — IDs present in the list but absent from the JSONL — to
   246→`ids-missing.txt` for targeted re-fetching.
   247→
   248→- **Input**: `data/ids-all-goethe-faust.txt`, `data/items-all-goethe-faust.json`
   249→- **Output**: `data/ids-missing.txt`
   250→- **Usage**:
   251→  ```
   252→  python scripts/find_missing_items.py
   253→  cd data && bash ../scripts/fetch-items.sh ids-missing.txt
   254→  ```
   255→
   256→### `fetch-progress.sh`
   257→Shell script to monitor fetch progress.
   258→



```

```
     1→# scripts/
     2→
     3→Standalone operational scripts for GeMeA. Each script is self-contained with a header comment.
     4→
     5→## Conventions
     6→
     7→Every script must begin with:
     8→```
     9→# Purpose:      one-line description
    10→# Usage:        how to invoke
    11→# Inputs:       what it reads
    12→# Outputs:      what it writes
    13→# Dependencies: external tools / packages required
    14→# Assumptions:  preconditions
    15→```
    16→
    17→Scripts use `argparse` for CLI arguments. Place all scripts here; document them below when added.
    18→
    19→## Index
    20→
    21→### `sh/` — shell scripts (orchestration, data movement)
    22→
    23→| Script | Purpose |
    24→|--------|---------|
    25→| `sh/download_data.sh` | Fetch mocho-normalized RDF from public data dump |
    26→| `sh/setup_gnd_fuseki.sh` | (deprecated) Start local Fuseki container and load GND authority files |
    27→| `sh/setup_gnd_qlever.sh` | Convert GND JSON-LD to N-Triples, build QLever index, start SPARQL server |
    28→
    29→### `py/` — general Python scripts
    30→
    31→| Script | Purpose |
    32→|--------|---------|
    33→| `py/sr10_explore_token_distribution.py` | SR-10: plot raw token-count distribution with percentile markers; outputs `notes/images/fig_token_distribution.png` |
    34→| `py/sr10_analyse_title_lengths.py` | SR-10: plot title-length distribution per year bucket; outputs `notes/images/fig_title_lengths.png` |
    35→| `py/sr10_render_title_viz.py` | SR-10: generate per-theme standalone HTML files from fig_title_lengths.jsx |
    36→
    37→### `ner/` — NER study-round scripts (SR-01 – SR-11)
    38→
    39→| Script | Purpose |
    40→|--------|---------|
    41→| `ner/sr01_check_isbd_titles.py` | SR-01: analyse ISBD punctuation patterns in DF_DE_TITLES; reports per-pattern counts and sample titles |
    42→| `ner/sr01_rate_isbd_fields.py` | SR-01: rate all 4.47M DF_DE_TITLES records for ISBD field presence; assigns silver_tier; optionally writes per-pattern examples with DDB links |
    43→| `ner/sr03_validate_heuristic_fields.py` | SR-03: sample 200 heuristic-tier records from sr01_isbd_field_ratings.csv for manual false-positive review; writes a review sheet CSV |
    44→| `ner/sr03_fp_review.py` | SR-03: apply automated regex rules + per-row manual overrides to classify each heuristic flag as TP or FP |
    45→| `ner/sr03_validate_fp_review.py` | SR-03: validate FP review output — checks row count, field name validity, and referential integrity |
    46→| `ner/sr03_extract_fp_examples.py` | SR-03: extract DDB links and field flags from sr03_heuristic_validation_sample.csv |
    47→| `ner/sr04_validate_translator_disambiguation.py` | SR-04: sample 100 heuristic `f_person` records and classify SoR text as TRANSLATOR / EDITOR / PERSON |
    48→| `ner/sr04_evaluate_translator_heuristic.py` | SR-04: evaluate keyword heuristic against manual `true_class` annotations; prints precision/recall/F1, confusion matrix, FN detail |
    49→| `ner/sr05_validate_trailing_period.py` | SR-05: sample 200 titles ending with `.`; applies heuristic classifier (ISBD_CLOSE / ABBREV / ORDINAL / NATURAL / NOISE) |
    50→| `ner/sr05_trailing_period_review.py` | SR-05: annotate `sr05_trailing_period_sample.csv` with refined `true_class` and `notes`; prints FP rate summary |
    51→| `ner/sr08_sample_gold.py` | SR-08: draw ~500-record stratified NER gold sample by era × silver_tier × dc_type |
    52→| `ner/sr08_prefill_spans.py` | SR-08: pre-fill TITLE / OTHER_TITLE / PERSON spans for tier-1 and tier-2 records using ISBD rules |
    53→| `ner/sr08_verify_spans.py` | SR-08: verify character offset integrity of pre-filled spans; prints spot-check records |
    54→| `ner/sr08_gold_dctype_breakdown.py` | SR-08: dc_type × era breakdown of the gold sample; writes `data/processed/sr08_gold_dctype_breakdown.csv` |
    55→| `ner/sr09_eval_nuner_tier2.py` | SR-09: NuNER tier-2 sanity check — compare to ISBD silver spans, report P/R/F1 per label |
    56→| `ner/sr11_dctype_by_era.py` | SR-11: count and rank dc_type values per era stratum; writes `data/processed/sr11_dctype_by_era.csv` |
    57→| `ner/sr11_sample_validation.py` | SR-11 T11.1a: sample 50 pre-1750 tier-0 records for manual prompt validation |
    58→| `ner/sr11_annotate.py` | SR-11 T11.1b: interactive span annotation helper; resolves character offsets, writes back in place |
    59→
    60→### `analysis/` — DDB corpus analysis scripts
    61→
    62→| Script | Purpose |
    63→|--------|---------|
    64→| `analysis/count_hierarchy_types.py` | Count primary/secondary objects by hierarchy_type; outputs `data/processed/hierarchy_type_counts.csv` and `notes/images/hierarchy_type_counts.png` |
    65→| `analysis/validate_htype_title_quality.py` | Compute fraction of generic/structural titles per htype; outputs `data/processed/htype_title_quality.csv` and `notes/images/htype_title_quality.png` |
    66→| `analysis/explore_top_titles.py` | Print top-N most frequent titles per hierarchy_type to inform pattern blocklists |
    67→| `analysis/sample_work_titles.py` | Sample mid-frequency titles (ranks 31–150) for htype_001 and htype_018 to evaluate content quality |
    68→| `analysis/sample_htype_titles.py` | Random sample of titles for specified htypes with DDB item links |
    69→| `analysis/filter_content_titles.py` | Classify all titles into work_title / section_label / physical_label (Option C hybrid); outputs `data/processed/title_class_counts.csv`, `title_class_sample.csv`, `notes/images/title_class_breakdown.png` |
    70→| `analysis/count_lang.py` | Count objects by `lang` code (explodes multi-value entries); outputs `data/processed/lang_counts.csv` and `notes/images/lang_counts.png` |
    71→| `analysis/lang_by_year.py` | Count objects by language × year/decade (year from `dc_issued`); outputs `data/processed/lang_by_year.csv` and `notes/images/lang_by_year.png` |
    72→| `analysis/wordcloud_book.py` | Word cloud of "book" in each DDB language, sized by object count; outputs `notes/images/wordcloud_book.png` |
    73→| `analysis/detect_lang_titles.py` | Detect title language with fasttext lid.176, compare against dc:language annotation; outputs `data/processed/lang_detect_titles.csv` and `lang_detect_summary.csv` |
    74→| `analysis/filter_de_content.py` | Filter s2_meta.parquet to German + Latin content titles (removes BLANKET_EXCLUDE htypes + non-ger/gmh/nds/lat lang); outputs `data/out/s2/s2_meta_de_content.parquet` and `data/processed/filter_de_content_summary.csv` |
    75→| `analysis/tokenize_de_titles.py` | Tokenize titles in s2_meta_de_content.parquet with xlm-roberta-large; computes `all_tokens` and `content_tokens` per title; outputs `data/processed/de_titles_tokenized.parquet` |
    76→
    77→### `utils/` — shared utilities
    78→
    79→| Script | Purpose |
    80→|--------|---------|
    81→| `utils/jsonld_to_nt.py` | Convert a JSON-LD file (.jsonld or .jsonld.gz) to N-Triples using rdflib |
    82→| `utils/check_generic_title_words.py` | Query DNB SPARQL endpoint to count GND Work records per GENERIC_TITLE_WORD; validates high-collision words |
    83→| `utils/check_note_urls.py` | Check URLs referenced in notes/ for broken links |
    84→| `utils/fetch-ids-by-sector.py` | Fetch DDB object IDs by sector from the OAI-PMH endpoint |
    85→| `utils/find_missing_ids.py` | Find IDs present in one data source but missing from another |
    86→



```

        },
        {
          "label": "Extend unmatched fields",
          "description": "Add explicit mappings for the 55 unmatched fields: Agent.prefLabel/dateOfBirth/sameAs, Place.lat/long, TimeSpan.begin/end, ProvidedCHO.identifier/hasMet/hasType. These cover >100k records each and currently produce zero output triples."
        },
        {
          "label": "Both WEMI + extension",
          "description": "Fix the fan-out via WEMI-level selection AND extend alignment to cover unmatched EDM-native/Agent/Place/TimeSpan fields. Full rework of align_ddbedm_to_mocho.py."
        }

      "header": "Unmatched route",
      "multiSelect": false,
      "options": [
        {
          "label": "Hard-coded in align script",
          "description": "Add an EXPLICIT_MAPPINGS dict in align_ddbedm_to_mocho.py — same pattern as OVERRIDES — with (entity_type, json_key) → (pred_iri, pred_label, wemi_level). Keeps everything in one script."
        },

        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

**Tool result**
```
     1→For each DDB object:
     2→1. By default, rdf:type mocho:Manifestation
     3→2. More specific domain-specific subclasses will be depending on the sector, the mediatype, htype, and/or the dctype.
     4→2.1 for example, if the mediatype is AUDIO, then it can either belong to aco:Classes or mo:Classes based on the dc:type value. see goethe-faust/scripts/old-config/audio_type2class.json
     5→
     6→2.2 another example, if the mediatype is TEXT, and it's coming from sector 2, then it's rda:Manifestation (library: books)
     7→
     8→2.3 if mediatype is TEXT, and it's coming from sector 1, then it's likely to be a rico:Class (archive: documents)
     9→
    10→2.4 For mediatype="IMAGE", and it's coming from sector 6 (museum), then it's likely to be a vra:Image, etc.
    11→
    12→These need to be discussed thoroughly so transform-script-plan.md can take shape.



```

### Assistant *(2026-04-15T17:27:30.094Z)*

**Tool call:** `Write`
```json
{
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/.claude/plans/elegant-wondering-blanket.md",
  "content": "# Plan: Build `lookup_dctype_to_class.csv` — Type Dispatch for the Transform\n\n## Context\n\n`transform_edm_to_mocho.py` needs to assign domain-specific rdf:type subclasses to\nProvidedCHO entities based on three dimensions: mediatype (mt001–mt007), sector\n(sparte001–007), and the dc:type string value. This dispatch logic existed in\n`old-config/type2class.json` and `old-config/audio_type2class.json` (FRBR/FaBiO\nclasses, 4 WEMI levels), but was never ported to the mocho-aligned pipeline.\n\nThe current `alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv` only covers property alignment (EDM field →\nRDA property via DC→RDA map). It has no type dispatch. The htype lookup\n(`lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv`) covers archival hierarchy types (htype_030–048) but\nnot the full sector × mediatype × dc:type space.\n\nThis plan builds a new lookup file and generator script to fill that gap. The\ntransform script will use it in `retype_entities()` as a second dispatch after\nthe htype lookup.\n\n---\n\n## Critical files\n\n| Path | Role |\n|---|---|\n| `goethe-faust/scripts/gen_dctype_class_mapping.py` | **New script** — generates the lookup |\n| `goethe-faust/output/lookup_dctype_to_class.csv` | **New output** — type dispatch table |\n| `goethe-faust/scripts/old-config/type2class.json` | Source: general dc:type → FRBR/FaBiO classes |\n| `goethe-faust/scripts/old-config/audio_type2class.json` | Source: audio dc:type → MO/ACO/FaBiO classes |\n| `goethe-faust/output/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv` | Reference pattern for lookup CSV schema |\n| `goethe-faust/notes/alignment-adr.md` | Will need a new decision (D11) for this dispatch |\n| `goethe-faust/scripts/README.md` | Must be updated |\n\n---\n\n## Output CSV schema\n\n`lookup_dctype_to_class.csv` — one row per (mediatype, sector, dc_type_de) triple:\n\n| Column | Description |\n|---|---|\n| `mediatype` | IRI (e.g. `http://ddb.vocnet.org/medientyp/mt001`) or `any` |\n| `sector` | IRI (e.g. `http://ddb.vocnet.org/sparte/sparte005`) or `any` |\n| `dc_type_de` | German dc:type literal string as it appears in the corpus (the lookup key) |\n| `dc_type_en` | English translation (from old-config `en` field) |\n| `rdf_type_m` | Manifestation-level class IRI (the primary rdf:type to emit alongside mocho:Manifestation) |\n| `rdf_type_e` | Expression-level class IRI (emit if present) |\n| `rdf_type_w` | Work-level class IRI (emit if present) |\n| `rdf_type_i` | Item-level class IRI (emit if present) |\n| `source_vocab` | Which vocabulary the class belongs to: `mo`, `aco`, `vra`, `rico`, `doco`, `fabio`, `mocho` |\n| `notes` | Free-text notes from old-config `remarks` field |\n\nLookup priority in the transform script:\n1. Exact match on (mediatype, sector, dc_type_de)\n2. Match on (mediatype, `any`, dc_type_de)\n3. Match on (`any`, `any`, dc_type_de)\n4. No match → no additional rdf:type beyond mocho:Manifestation + htype\n\n---\n\n## Class mapping: old-config → mocho-aligned\n\nThe old-config uses FRBR/FaBiO with 4 positional WEMI slots `[W, E, M, I]`.\nThe new lookup uses named columns and mocho-aligned vocabularies:\n\n### Audio (mt001) — from `audio_type2class.json`\n\n| Old classes (M slot) | New `rdf_type_m` |\n|---|---|\n| `mo:Record`, `aco:AudioClip` | `aco:AudioClip` (MO/ACO distinction made at rdf_type_e if needed) |\n| `mo:MusicalManifestation` | `mo:MusicalManifestation` |\n| `fabio:AudioDocument` at E + `aco:AudioClip` at M | `aco:AudioClip` |\n\nKey audio mappings:\n- Musical (Schallplatte, CD, Langspielplatte, Folie, Schellackplate, Schallfolie+music topic): `rdf_type_w = mo:MusicalWork`, `rdf_type_m = mo:MusicalManifestation`, `rdf_type_i = aco:AudioFile`\n- Non-musical audio (Audiofile, Hörfunksendung, Inszenierung, Tondokument non-music, Magnettonband/Vortrag): `rdf_type_e = fabio:AudioDocument`, `rdf_type_m = aco:AudioClip`, `rdf_type_i = aco:AudioFile`\n- Default audio: `rdf_type_m = aco:AudioClip`, `rdf_type_i = aco:AudioFile`\n\n### Text (mt003) — from `type2class.json`\n\nSector-dependent:\n\n| Sector | `rdf_type_m` |\n|---|---|\n| sparte001 (Archive) | `rico:Record` (for most text types; htype takes precedence if present) |\n| sparte002 (Library) | FaBiO class at M level → map to mocho equivalent (see below) |\n| sparte005 (Museum) | keep as mocho:Manifestation (no further subclass without VRA text class) |\n| `any` | FaBiO class from old-config |\n\nFaBiO → mocho class mapping for library text types (representative):\n- `fabio:Thesis` / `fabio:DoctoralThesis` / `fabio:MastersThesis` → keep as `fabio:Thesis` etc. (FaBiO is in mocho imports)\n- `fabio:Textbook` → `fabio:Textbook`\n- `fabio:NewspaperArticle` → `fabio:NewspaperArticle`\n- `fabio:ReportDocument` → `fabio:ReportDocument`\n- `fabio:PrintObject` (generic Manifestation) → omit (redundant with mocho:Manifestation)\n- `doco:Section` etc. → `doco:Section` (DoCO is in mocho)\n\n### Photo (mt002) — not in old-config, new mappings needed\n\n| Sector | `rdf_type_m` |\n|---|---|\n| sparte005 (Museum) | `vra:Image` |\n| sparte001 (Archive) | `rico:Record` |\n| `any` | leave as mocho:Manifestation |\n\n### Video (mt005) — not in old-config, new mappings needed\n\n| Sector | `rdf_type_m` |\n|---|---|\n| sparte004 (Mediathek) | `aco:MovingImage` (if in mocho) or leave as mocho:Manifestation |\n| `any` | leave as mocho:Manifestation |\n\n### Not Digitized (mt007)\n\nNo class dispatch — emit mocho:Manifestation only.\n\n---\n\n## Script: `gen_dctype_class_mapping.py`\n\n### Header\n\n```\nPurpose:    Generate lookup_dctype_to_class.csv — type dispatch table mapping\n            (mediatype, sector, dc_type_de) to mocho-aligned rdf:type class IRIs.\n            Used by transform_edm_to_mocho.py for domain-specific rdf:type dispatch.\nUsage:      python gen_dctype_class_mapping.py\nInputs:     scripts/old-config/type2class.json\n            scripts/old-config/audio_type2class.json\nOutputs:    output/lookup_dctype_to_class.csv\nDeps:       stdlib only (json, csv, pathlib)\nAssumes:    old-config files are read-only reference; class mapping to mocho-aligned\n            equivalents is encoded in CLASS_MAP dict in this script.\n```\n\n### Implementation steps\n\n1. **Load source files** — `type2class.json` (general) and `audio_type2class.json` (audio).\n\n2. **Define `CLASS_MAP`** — dict mapping old FRBR/FaBiO class strings → new mocho-aligned IRIs:\n   ```python\n   CLASS_MAP = {\n       \"mo:MusicalWork\":           \"http://purl.org/ontology/mo/MusicalWork\",\n       \"mo:MusicalManifestation\":  \"http://purl.org/ontology/mo/MusicalManifestation\",\n       \"mo:Record\":                \"http://purl.org/ontology/mo/Record\",\n       \"aco:AudioClip\":            \"https://w3id.org/ac-ontology/aco#AudioClip\",\n       \"aco:AudioItem\":            \"https://w3id.org/ac-ontology/aco#AudioItem\",\n       \"aco:AudioFile\":            \"https://w3id.org/ac-ontology/aco#AudioFile\",\n       \"fabio:AudioDocument\":      \"http://purl.org/spar/fabio/AudioDocument\",\n       \"fabio:DoctoralThesis\":     \"http://purl.org/spar/fabio/DoctoralThesis\",\n       \"fabio:MastersThesis\":      \"http://purl.org/spar/fabio/MastersThesis\",\n       \"fabio:Thesis\":             \"http://purl.org/spar/fabio/Thesis\",\n       \"fabio:Textbook\":           \"http://purl.org/spar/fabio/Textbook\",\n       \"fabio:NewspaperArticle\":   \"http://purl.org/spar/fabio/NewspaperArticle\",\n       \"fabio:ReportDocument\":     \"http://purl.org/spar/fabio/ReportDocument\",\n       \"fabio:WorkCollection\":     \"http://purl.org/spar/fabio/WorkCollection\",\n       \"doco:Section\":             \"http://purl.org/spar/doco/Section\",\n       # Generic FaBiO carriers (redundant with mocho:Manifestation) → omit\n       \"fabio:PrintObject\":        \"\",\n       \"fabio:AnalogItem\":         \"\",\n       \"fabio:DigitalItem\":        \"\",\n       \"frbr:Work\":                \"\",\n       \"frbr:Manifestation\":       \"\",\n       \"frbr:Item\":                \"\",\n       # TBD entries — flag for manual review\n       \"vra:Image\":                \"TBD\",\n       \"rico:Record\":              \"http://www.ica.org/standards/RiC/ontology#Record\",\n   }\n   ```\n\n3. **Process audio entries** (`audio_type2class.json`):\n   - For each key (dc_type_de): read `class` array (positional: [W, E, M, I])\n   - Flatten lists at position M (e.g. `[\"mo:Record\", \"aco:AudioClip\"]`) → use first non-empty as primary\n   - Apply `CLASS_MAP` to resolve full IRIs; skip empty ### Assistant *(2026-04-15T17:58:11.140Z)*

**Tool call:** `AskUserQuestion`
```json
{
  "questions": [
    {
      "question": "When multiple dispatch dimensions are present for a ProvidedCHO, how should they be combined? For example: a Text record from sector 1 (Archive) with htype_034 (Archivale → rico:Record) and dc:type 'Brief' (Letter → rico:Record from type2class). Both give rico:Record — fine. But what if they give different classes?",
      "header": "Dispatch model",
      "multiSelect": false,
      "options": [
        {
          "label": "Accumulate all (emit all classes)",
          "description": "Every dimension that has a match emits its rdf:type triple(s). A record can have mocho:Manifestation + rico:Record (from htype) + fabio:Letter (from dc:type) simultaneously. Dedup identical IRIs. No priority; all apply."
        },
        {
          "label": "Priority chain (first wins)",
          "description": "Apply in order: htype → dc:type → mediatype+sector. The first dimension that produces a class wins for that WEMI level; lower-priority dimensions are skipped for that level. Prevents conflicting types at the same WEMI level."
        },
        {
          "label": "mediatype gates dc:type",
          "description": "mediatype determines which type dispatch table to use (audio vs. general). Within that table, (sector, dc:type) together select the row. htype is always applied independently (archival only). This mirrors the old audio_type2class vs type2class split."
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "question": "Should htype dispatch and dc:type dispatch ever produce types at the same WEMI level for the same record?",
      "header": "htype vs dc:type",
      "multiSelect": false,
      "options": [
        {
          "label": "Yes — both emit, dedup",
          "description": "htype and dc:type can both produce a Manifestation-level type. The transform deduplicates identical IRIs. If they differ, both are emitted (e.g. rico:Record from htype AND fabio:Letter from dc:type)."
        },
        {
          "label": "No — htype takes precedence",
          "description": "If htype produces a class, dc:type dispatch is skipped for that entity. htype is a more controlled vocabulary; dc:type is a free string and less reliable."
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

  "new_string": "---\n\n## Dispatch priority and combination rules\n\nFour dimensions can contribute rdf:type triples for a ProvidedCHO. Their roles\nare complementary, not competing:\n\n| Dimension | Source | Output | When |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| `htype` | `lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv` | DoCO class (library) or RiC-O class (archival) | htype_001–029 → DoCO; htype_030–048 → RiC-O |\n| `mediatype` × `sector` × `dc:type` | `lookup_dctype_to_class.csv` | Domain class (ACO/MO/VRA/FaBiO/etc.) | Any ProvidedCHO with a dc:type value |\n| base | hardcoded | `mocho:Manifestation` | Always |\n\n**Combination rule**: Accumulate — both htype and dc:type dispatch can fire\nfor the same entity. Identical rdf:type IRIs are deduped. Both layers are\nindependent: an archival letter can be both `rico:Record` (from htype_034) AND\n`fabio:Letter` (from dc:type `Brief`). Neither blocks the other.\n\n**Separation by htype domain**:\n- htype_030–048 (archival): htype dispatch produces RiC-O classes; dc:type dispatch\n  may also produce a more specific document genre class. Both emitted.\n- htype_001–029 (library/DoCO): htype dispatch produces DoCO structural classes\n  (Chapter, Section, etc.); dc:type dispatch may produce a FaBiO publication type.\n  Both emitted if present.\n- No htype: only dc:type dispatch contributes a specific class (beyond mocho:Manifestation).\n\n**mediatype gates the dispatch table**:\n- `mt001` (Audio): use `audio_type2class.json` source rows → ACO/MO classes\n- All other mediatypes: use `type2class.json` source rows, filtered by sector\n- `mt007` (Not Digitized): no dc:type dispatch\n\n**Priority order for the transform loop** (implemented in `retype_entities()`):\n1. Always emit `mocho:Manifestation`\n2. Apply htype dispatch (existing `lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv`)\n3. Apply dc:type dispatch (new `lookup_dctype_to_class.csv`), using mediatype to gate table\n4. Dedup: collect all rdf:type IRIs in a set, emit each once\n\n**Validation before finalising**: Run a sampling script (`sample_type_dispatch.py`)\non 50 random records per sector × mediatype cell to show what classes each strategy\nwould emit. Manual review determines if the accumulate model is correct or if\npriority overrides are needed for specific cases.\n\n---\n\n## Sampling script: `sample_type_dispatch.py`\n\nA lightweight diagnostic script (not part of the transform pipeline) that:\n\n1. Reads `items-all-goethe-faust.json` (streaming, random sample)\n2. For each sampled record, shows:\n   - object_id, sector IRI, mediatype IRI\n   - dc:type value(s)\n   - htype code\n   - rdf:type classes emitted by htype dispatch (from lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv)\n   - rdf:type classes emitted by dc:type dispatch (from lookup_dctype_to_class.csv)\n   - combined rdf:type set (deduped)\n3. Samples at least 5 records per populated (sector × mediatype) cell\n4. Output: `output/type_dispatch_sample.csv` + printed summary\n\nThis is a prerequisite for finalising `retype_entities()` in the transform script.\n\n---\n\n## TBD items requiring follow-up before the transform is written"
}

It has no type dispatch. The htype lookup\n(`lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv`) covers archival hierarchy types (htype_030–048) but\nnot the full sector × mediatype × dc:type space.\n\nThis plan builds a new lookup file and generator script to fill that gap. The\ntransform script will use it in `retype_entities()` as a second dispatch after\nthe htype lookup.\n\n---\n\n## Critical files\n\n| Path | Role |\n|---|---|\n| `goethe-faust/scripts/gen_dctype_class_mapping.py` | **New script** — generates the lookup |\n| `goethe-faust/output/lookup_dctype_to_class.csv` | **New output** — type dispatch table |\n| `goethe-faust/scripts/old-config/type2class.json` | Source: general dc:type → FRBR/FaBiO classes |\n| `goethe-faust/scripts/old-config/audio_type2class.json` | Source: audio dc:type → MO/ACO/FaBiO classes |\n| `goethe-faust/output/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv` | Reference pattern for lookup CSV schema |\n| `goethe-faust/notes/alignment-adr.md` | Will need a new decision (D11) for this dispatch |\n| `goethe-faust/scripts/README.md` | Must be updated |\n\n---\n\n## Output CSV schema\n\n`lookup_dctype_to_class.csv` — one row per (mediatype, sector, dc_type_de) triple:\n\n| Column | Description |\n|---|---|\n| `mediatype` | IRI (e.g. `http://ddb.vocnet.org/medientyp/mt001`) or `any` |\n| `sector` | IRI (e.g. `http://ddb.vocnet.org/sparte/sparte005`) or `any` |\n| `dc_type_de` | German dc:type literal string as it appears in the corpus (the lookup key) |\n| `dc_type_en` | English translation (from old-config `en` field) |\n| `rdf_type_m` | Manifestation-level class IRI (the primary rdf:type to emit alongside mocho:Manifestation) |\n| `rdf_type_e` | Expression-level class IRI (emit if present) |\n| `rdf_type_w` | Work-level class IRI (emit if present) |\n| `rdf_type_i` | Item-level class IRI (emit if present) |\n| `source_vocab` | Which vocabulary the class belongs to: `mo`, `aco`, `vra`, `rico`, `doco`, `fabio`, `mocho` |\n| `notes` | Free-text notes from old-config `remarks` field |\n\nLookup priority in the transform script:\n1. Exact match on (mediatype, sector, dc_type_de)\n2. Match on (mediatype, `any`, dc_type_de)\n3. Match on (`any`, `any`, dc_type_de)\n4. No match → no additional rdf:type beyond mocho:Manifestation + htype\n\n---\n\n## Class mapping: old-config → mocho-aligned\n\nThe old-config uses FRBR/FaBiO with 4 positional WEMI slots `[W, E, M, I]`.\nThe new lookup uses named columns and mocho-aligned vocabularies:\n\n### Audio (mt001) — from `audio_type2class.json`\n\n| Old classes (M slot) | New `rdf_type_m` |\n|---|---|\n| `mo:Record`, `aco:AudioClip` | `aco:AudioClip` (MO/ACO distinction made at rdf_type_e if needed) |\n| `mo:MusicalManifestation` | `mo:MusicalManifestation` |\n| `fabio:AudioDocument` at E + `aco:AudioClip` at M | `aco:AudioClip` |\n\nKey audio mappings:\n- Musical (Schallplatte, CD, Langspielplatte, Folie, Schellackplate, Schallfolie+music topic): `rdf_type_w = mo:MusicalWork`, `rdf_type_m = mo:MusicalManifestation`, `rdf_type_i = aco:AudioFile`\n- Non-musical audio (Audiofile, Hörfunksendung, Inszenierung, Tondokument non-music, Magnettonband/Vortrag): `rdf_type_e = fabio:AudioDocument`, `rdf_type_m = aco:AudioClip`, `rdf_type_i = aco:AudioFile`\n- Default audio: `rdf_type_m = aco:AudioClip`, `rdf_type_i = aco:AudioFile`\n\n### Text (mt003) — from `type2class.json`\n\nSector-dependent:\n\n| Sector | `rdf_type_m` |\n|---|---|\n| sparte001 (Archive) | `rico:Record` (for most text types; htype takes precedence if present) |\n| sparte002 (Library) | FaBiO class at M level → map to mocho equivalent (see below) |\n| sparte005 (Museum) | keep as mocho:Manifestation (no further subclass without VRA text class) |\n| `any` | FaBiO class from old-config |\n\nFaBiO → mocho class mapping for library text types (representative):\n- `fabio:Thesis` / `fabio:DoctoralThesis` / `fabio:MastersThesis` → keep as `fabio:Thesis` etc. (FaBiO is in mocho imports)\n- `fabio:Textbook` → `fabio:Textbook`\n- `fabio:NewspaperArticle` → `fabio:NewspaperArticle`\n- `fabio:ReportDocument` → `fabio:ReportDocument`\n- `fabio:PrintObject` (generic Manifestation) → omit (redundant with mocho:Manifestation)\n- `doco:Section` etc. → `doco:Section` (DoCO is in mocho)\n\n### Photo (mt002) — not in old-config, new mappings needed\n\n| Sector | `rdf_type_m` |\n|---|---|\n| sparte005 (Museum) | `vra:Image` |\n| sparte001 (Archive) | `rico:Record` |\n| `any` | leave as mocho:Manifestation |\n\n### Video (mt005) — not in old-config, new mappings needed\n\n| Sector | `rdf_type_m` |\n|---|---|\n| sparte004 (Mediathek) | `aco:MovingImage` (if in mocho) or leave as mocho:Manifestation |\n| `any` | leave as mocho:Manifestation |\n\n### Not Digitized (mt007)\n\nNo class dispatch — emit mocho:Manifestation only.\n\n---\n\n## Script: `gen_dctype_class_mapping.py`\n\n### Header\n\n```\nPurpose:    Generate lookup_dctype_to_class.csv — type dispatch table mapping\n            (mediatype, sector, dc_type_de) to mocho-aligned rdf:type class IRIs.\n            Used by transform_edm_to_mocho.py for domain-specific rdf:type dispatch.\nUsage:      python gen_dctype_class_mapping.py\nInputs:     scripts/old-config/type2class.json\n            scripts/old-config/audio_type2class.json\nOutputs:    output/lookup_dctype_to_class.csv\nDeps:       stdlib only (json, csv, pathlib)\nAssumes:    old-config files are read-only reference; class mapping to mocho-aligned\n            equivalents is encoded in CLASS_MAP dict in this script.\n```\n\n### Implementation steps\n\n1. **Load source files** — `type2class.json` (general) and `audio_type2class.json` (audio).\n\n2. **Define `CLASS_MAP`** — dict mapping old FRBR/FaBiO class strings → new mocho-aligned IRIs:\n   ```python\n   CLASS_MAP = {\n       \"mo:MusicalWork\":           \"http://purl.org/ontology/mo/MusicalWork\",\n       \"mo:MusicalManifestation\":  \"http://purl.org/ontology/mo/MusicalManifestation\",\n       \"mo:Record\":                \"http://purl.org/ontology/mo/Record\",\n       \"aco:AudioClip\":            \"https://w3id.org/ac-ontology/aco#AudioClip\",\n       \"aco:AudioItem\":            \"https://w3id.org/ac-ontology/aco#AudioItem\",\n       \"aco:AudioFile\":            \"https://w3id.org/ac-ontology/aco#AudioFile\",\n       \"fabio:AudioDocument\":      \"http://purl.org/spar/fabio/AudioDocument\",\n       \"fabio:DoctoralThesis\":     \"http://purl.org/spar/fabio/DoctoralThesis\",\n       \"fabio:MastersThesis\":      \"http://purl.org/spar/fabio/MastersThesis\",\n       \"fabio:Thesis\":             \"http://purl.org/spar/fabio/Thesis\",\n       \"fabio:Textbook\":           \"http://purl.org/spar/fabio/Textbook\",\n       \"fabio:NewspaperArticle\":   \"http://purl.org/spar/fabio/NewspaperArticle\",\n       \"fabio:ReportDocument\":     \"http://purl.org/spar/fabio/ReportDocument\",\n       \"fabio:WorkCollection\":     \"http://purl.org/spar/fabio/WorkCollection\",\n       \"doco:Section\":             \"http://purl.org/spar/doco/Section\",\n       # Generic FaBiO carriers (redundant with mocho:Manifestation) → omit\n       \"fabio:PrintObject\":        \"\",\n       \"fabio:AnalogItem\":         \"\",\n       \"fabio:DigitalItem\":        \"\",\n       \"frbr:Work\":                \"\",\n       \"frbr:Manifestation\":       \"\",\n       \"frbr:Item\":                \"\",\n       # TBD entries — flag for manual review\n       \"vra:Image\":                \"TBD\",\n       \"rico:Record\":              \"http://www.ica.org/standards/RiC/ontology#Record\",\n   }\n   ```\n\n3. **Process audio entries** (`audio_type2class.json`):\n   - For each key (dc_type_de): read `class` array (positional: [W, E, M, I])\n   - Flatten lists at position M (e.g. `[\"mo:Record\", \"aco:AudioClip\"]`) → use first non-empty as primary\n   - Apply `CLASS_MAP` to resolve full IRIs; skip empty strings\n   - Set `mediatype = mt001`, `sector = any` unless entry has `sector` field\n   - Emit one row per dc_type_de\n\n4. **Process general entries** (`type2class.json`):\n   - Same positional class array processing\n   - `mediatype = any` (the transform uses mediatype to route audio to audio_type2class; remaining types go here)\n   - Keep `sector = any` unless entry has `sector` field\n\n5. **Add manual Photo/Video rows** — hard-coded in the script:\n   - `(mt002, sparte005, any, Photo/Museum)` → `rdf_type_m = vra:Image` (TBD pending VRA IRI confirmation)\n   - `(mt002, sparte001, any, Photo/Archive)` → `rdf_type_m = rico:Record`\n\n6. **Write CSV** — sort by (mediatype, sector, dc_type_de).\n\n7. **Print summary**: total rows, rows with rdf_type_m populated, rows with TBD IRIs (need follow-up).\n\n---\n\n## Dispatch priority and combination rules\n\nFour dimensions can contribute rdf:type triples for a ProvidedCHO. Their roles\nare complementary, not competing:\n\n| Dimension | Source | Output | When |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| `htype` | `lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv` | DoCO class (library) or RiC-O class (archival) | htype_001–029 → DoCO; htype_030–048 → RiC-O |\n| `mediatype` × `sector` × `dc:type` | `lookup_dctype_to_class.csv` | Domain class (ACO/MO/VRA/FaBiO/etc.) | Any ProvidedCHO with a dc:type value |\n| base | hardcoded | `mocho:Manifestation` | Always |\n\n**Combination rule**: Accumulate — both htype and dc:type dispatch can fire\nfor the same entity. Identical rdf:type IRIs are deduped. Both layers are\nindependent: an archival letter can be both `rico:Record` (from htype_034) AND\n`fabio:Letter` (from dc:type `Brief`). Neither blocks the other.\n\n**Separation by htype domain**:\n- htype_030–048 (archival): htype dispatch produces RiC-O classes; dc:type dispatch\n  may also produce a more specific document genre class. Both emitted.\n- htype_001–029 (library/DoCO): htype dispatch produces DoCO structural classes\n  (Chapter, Section, etc.); dc:type dispatch may produce a FaBiO publication type.\n  Both emitted if present.\n- No htype: only dc:type dispatch contributes a specific class (beyond mocho:Manifestation).\n\n**mediatype gates the dispatch table**:\n- `mt001` (Audio): use `audio_type2class.json` source rows → ACO/MO classes\n- All other mediatypes: use `type2class.json` source rows, filtered by sector\n- `mt007` (Not Digitized): no dc:type dispatch\n\n**Priority order for the transform loop** (implemented in `retype_entities()`):\n1. Always emit `mocho:Manifestation`\n2. Apply htype dispatch (existing `lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv`)\n3. Apply dc:type dispatch (new `lookup_dctype_to_class.csv`), using mediatype to gate table\n4. Dedup: collect all rdf:type IRIs in a set, emit each once\n\n**Validation before finalising**: Run a sampling script (`sample_type_dispatch.py`)\non 50 random records per sector × mediatype cell to show what classes each strategy\nwould emit. Manual review determines if the accumulate model is correct or if\npriority overrides are needed for specific cases.\n\n---\n\n## Sampling script: `sample_type_dispatch.py`\n\nA lightweight diagnostic script (not part of the transform pipeline) that:\n\n1. Reads `items-all-goethe-faust.json` (streaming, random sample)\n2. For each sampled record, shows:\n   - object_id, sector IRI, mediatype IRI\n   - dc:type value(s)\n   - htype code\n   - rdf:type classes emitted by htype dispatch (from lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv)\n   - rdf:type classes emitted by dc:type dispatch (from lookup_dctype_to_class.csv)\n   - combined rdf:type set (deduped)\n3. Samples at least 5 records per populated (sector × mediatype) cell\n4. Output: `output/type_dispatch_sample.csv` + printed summary\n\nThis is a prerequisite for finalising `retype_entities()` in the transform script.\n\n---\n\n## TBD items requiring follow-up before the transform is written\n\n- **VRA Core IRIs**: confirm the VRA Core 4 class IRI for Image (`vra:Image`). Check if VRA is in mocho-odk imports or needs to be added.\n- **ACO IRIs**: confirm `aco:AudioClip`, `aco:AudioFile` IRIs against the ACO spec and mocho imports.\n- **`mo:MusicalManifestation` typo**: old-config has `mo:MuscialExpression` (typo) — correct to `mo:MusicalExpression` in CLASS_MAP.\n- **FaBiO in mocho**: confirm which FaBiO classes are actually imported in `mocho-full.owl` before emitting them as rdf:type targets. Classes not in mocho should be flagged in the `source_vocab` column but still emitted (they are valid LOD IRIs).\n- **Video (mt005)**: no old-config entries. Decide whether `aco:MovingImage` or another class applies.\n- **Not Digitized (mt007)**: confirm no class dispatch needed.\n\n---\n\n## ADR entry (D11, to be added to `alignment-adr.md`)\n\n**Decision**: Type dispatch beyond mocho:Manifestation is driven by a lookup table\n`lookup_dctype_to_class.csv` keyed by (mediatype, sector, dc_type_de), separate from\nthe property alignment table. The htype lookup (`lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv`) remains\nthe primary dispatch for archival hierarchy types; the dc:type dispatch supplements it\nfor non-archival and cross-sector cases.\n\n---\n\n## Verification\n\n1. Run `gen_dctype_class_mapping.py`; check row count, TBD count in summary output.\n2. Grep output for `TBD` — all must be resolved before transform is written.\n3. Cross-check audio rows: every entry in `audio_type2class.json` with a non-empty M-slot class should appear in the CSV.\n4. Spot-check: `Schallplatte` → `mo:MusicalManifestation` at rdf_type_m; `Audiofile` → `aco:AudioClip` at rdf_type_m.\n5. Update `goethe-faust/scripts/README.md` with new script entry.\n6. Add D11 to `goethe-faust/notes/alignment-adr.md`.\n",
  "planFilePath": "/Users/mta/.claude/plans/elegant-wondering-blanket.md"
}
```

   142→   CLASS_MAP = {
   143→       "mo:MusicalWork":           "http://purl.org/ontology/mo/MusicalWork",

   153→       "fabio:Textbook":           "http://purl.org/spar/fabio/Textbook",
   154→       "fabio:NewspaperArticle":   "http://purl.org/spar/fabio/NewspaperArticle",
   155→       "fabio:ReportDocument":     "http://purl.org/spar/fabio/ReportDocument",
   156→       "fabio:WorkCollection":     "http://purl.org/spar/fabio/WorkCollection",
   157→       "doco:Section":             "http://purl.org/spar/doco/Section",
   158→       # Generic FaBiO carriers (redundant with mocho:Manifestation) → omit
   159→       "fabio:PrintObject":        "",
   160→       "fabio:AnalogItem":         "",
   161→       "fabio:DigitalItem":        "",
   162→       "frbr:Work":                "",
   163→       "frbr:Manifestation":       "",
   164→       "frbr:Item":                "",
   165→       # TBD entries — flag for manual review
   166→       "vra:Image":                "TBD",
   167→       "rico:Record":              "http://www.ica.org/standards/RiC/ontology#Record",
   168→   }
   169→   ```
   170→
   171→3. **Process audio entries** (`audio_type2class.json`):
   172→   - For each key (dc_type_de): read `class` array (positional: [W, E, M, I])
   173→   - Flatten lists at position M (e.g. `["mo:Record", "aco:AudioClip"]`) → use first non-empty as primary
   174→   - Apply `CLASS_MAP` to resolve full IRIs; skip empty strings
   175→   - Set `mediatype = mt001`, `sector = any` unless entry has `sector` field
   176→   - Emit one row per dc_type_de
   177→
   178→4. **Process general entries** (`type2class.json`):
   179→   - Same positional class array processing
   180→   - `mediatype = any` (the transform uses mediatype to route audio to audio_type2class; remaining types go here)
   181→   - Keep `sector = any` unless entry has `sector` field
   182→
   183→5. **Add manual Photo/Video rows** — hard-coded in the script:
   184→   - `(mt002, sparte005, any, Photo/Museum)` → `rdf_type_m = vra:Image` (TBD pending VRA IRI confirmation)
   185→   - `(mt002, sparte001, any, Photo/Archive)` → `rdf_type_m = rico:Record`
   186→
   187→6. **Write CSV** — sort by (mediatype, sector, dc_type_de).
   188→
   189→7. **Print summary**: total rows, rows with rdf_type_m populated, rows with TBD IRIs (need follow-up).
   190→
   191→---
   192→
   193→## Dispatch priority and combination rules
   194→
   195→Four dimensions can contribute rdf:type triples for a ProvidedCHO. Their roles
   196→are complementary, not competing:
   197→
   198→| Dimension | Source | Output | When |
   199→|---|---|---|---|
   200→| `htype` | `lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv` | DoCO class (library) or RiC-O class (archival) | htype_001–029 → DoCO; htype_030–048 → RiC-O |
   201→| `mediatype` × `sector` × `dc:type` | `lookup_dctype_to_class.csv` | Domain class (ACO/MO/VRA/FaBiO/etc.) | Any ProvidedCHO with a dc:type value |
   202→| base | hardcoded | `mocho:Manifestation` | Always |
   203→
   204→**Combination rule**: Accumulate — both htype and dc:type dispatch can fire
   205→for the same entity. Identical rdf:type IRIs are deduped. Both layers are
   206→independent: an archival letter can be both `rico:Record` (from htype_034) AND
   207→`fabio:Letter` (from dc:type `Brief`). Neither blocks the other.
   208→
   209→**Separation by htype domain**:
   210→- htype_030–048 (archival): htype dispatch produces RiC-O classes; dc:type dispatch
   211→  may also produce a more specific document genre class. Both emitted.
   212→- htype_001–029 (library/DoCO): htype dispatch produces DoCO structural classes
   213→  (Chapter, Section, etc.); dc:type dispatch may produce a FaBiO publication type.
   214→  Both emitted if present.
   215→- No htype: only dc:type dispatch contributes a specific class (beyond mocho:Manifestation).
   216→
   217→**mediatype gates the dispatch table**:
   218→- `mt001` (Audio): use `audio_type2class.json` source rows → ACO/MO classes
   219→- All other mediatypes: use `type2class.json` source rows, filtered by sector
   220→- `mt007` (Not Digitized): no dc:type dispatch
   221→
   222→**Priority order for the transform loop** (implemented in `retype_entities()`):
   223→1. Always emit `mocho:Manifestation`
   224→2. Apply htype dispatch (existing `lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv`)
   225→3. Apply dc:type dispatch (new `lookup_dctype_to_class.csv`), using mediatype to gate table
   226→4. Dedup: collect all rdf:type IRIs in a set, emit each once
   227→
   228→**Validation before finalising**: Run a sampling script (`sample_type_dispatch.py`)
   229→on 50 random records per sector × mediatype cell to show what classes each strategy
   230→would emit. Manual review determines if the accumulate model is correct or if
   231→priority overrides are needed for specific cases.
   232→
   233→---
   234→
   235→## Sampling script: `sample_type_dispatch.py`
   236→
   237→A lightweight diagnostic script (not part of the transform pipeline) that:
   238→
   239→1. Reads `items-all-goethe-faust.json` (streaming, random sample)
   240→2. For each sampled record, shows:
   241→   - object_id, sector IRI, mediatype IRI
   242→   - dc:type value(s)
   243→   - htype code
   244→   - rdf:type classes emitted by htype dispatch (from lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv)
   245→   - rdf:type classes emitted by dc:type dispatch (from lookup_dctype_to_class.csv)
   246→   - combined rdf:type set (deduped)
   247→3. Samples at least 5 records per populated (sector × mediatype) cell
   248→4. Output: `output/type_dispatch_sample.csv` + printed summary
   249→
   250→This is a prerequisite for finalising `retype_entities()` in the transform script.
   251→
   252→---
   253→
   254→## TBD items requiring follow-up before the transform is written
   255→
   256→- **VRA Core IRIs**: confirm the VRA Core 4 class IRI for Image (`vra:Image`). Check if VRA is in mocho-odk imports or needs to be added.
   257→- **ACO IRIs**: confirm `aco:AudioClip`, `aco:AudioFile` IRIs against the ACO spec and mocho imports.
   258→- **`mo:MusicalManifestation` typo**: old-config has `mo:MuscialExpression` (typo) — correct to `mo:MusicalExpression` in CLASS_MAP.
   259→- **FaBiO in mocho**: confirm which FaBiO classes are actually imported in `mocho-full.owl` before emitting them as rdf:type targets. Classes not in mocho should be flagged in the `source_vocab` column but still emitted (they are valid LOD IRIs).
   260→- **Video (mt005)**: no old-config entries. Decide whether `aco:MovingImage` or another class applies.
   261→- **Not Digitized (mt007)**: confirm no class dispatch needed.
   262→
   263→---
   264→
   265→## ADR entry (D11, to be added to `alignment-adr.md`)
   266→
   267→**Decision**: Type dispatch beyond mocho:Manifestation is driven by a lookup table
   268→`lookup_dctype_to_class.csv` keyed by (mediatype, sector, dc_type_de), separate from
   269→the property alignment table. The htype lookup (`lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv`) remains
   270→the primary dispatch for archival hierarchy types; the dc:type dispatch supplements it
   271→for non-archival and cross-sector cases.
   272→
   273→---
   274→
   275→## Verification
   276→
   277→1. Run `gen_dctype_class_mapping.py`; check row count, TBD count in summary output.
   278→2. Grep output for `TBD` — all must be resolved before transform is written.
   279→3. Cross-check audio rows: every entry in `audio_type2class.json` with a non-empty M-slot class should appear in the CSV.
   280→4. Spot-check: `Schallplatte` → `mo:MusicalManifestation` at rdf_type_m; `Audiofile` → `aco:AudioClip` at rdf_type_m.
   281→5. Update `goethe-faust/scripts/README.md` with new script entry.
   282→6. Add D11 to `goethe-faust/notes/alignment-adr.md`.
   283→



```

  "content": "# Plan: Build `lookup_dctype_to_class.csv` — Type Dispatch for the Transform\n\n## Context\n\n`transform_edm_to_mocho.py` needs to assign domain-specific rdf:type subclasses to\nProvidedCHO entities based on three dimensions: mediatype (mt001–mt007), sector\n(sparte001–007), and the dc:type string value. This dispatch logic existed in\n`old-config/type2class.json` and `old-config/audio_type2class.json` (FRBR/FaBiO\nclasses, 4 WEMI levels), but was never ported to the mocho-aligned pipeline.\n\nThe current `alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv` only covers property alignment (EDM field →\nRDA property via DC→RDA map). It has no type dispatch. The htype lookup\n(`lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv`) covers archival hierarchy types (htype_030–048) but\nnot the full sector × mediatype × dc:type space.\n\nThis plan builds a new lookup file and generator script to fill that gap. The\ntransform script will use it in `retype_entities()` as a second dispatch after\nthe htype lookup.\n\n---\n\n## 1. Critical files\n\n| Path | Role |\n|---|---|\n| `goethe-faust/scripts/gen_dctype_class_mapping.py` | **New script** — generates the lookup |\n| `goethe-faust/output/lookup_dctype_to_class.csv` | **New output** — type dispatch table |\n| `goethe-faust/scripts/old-config/type2class.json` | Source: general dc:type → FRBR/FaBiO classes |\n| `goethe-faust/scripts/old-config/audio_type2class.json` | Source: audio dc:type → MO/ACO/FaBiO classes |\n| `goethe-faust/output/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv` | Reference pattern for lookup CSV schema |\n| `goethe-faust/notes/alignment-adr.md` | Will need a new decision (D11) for this dispatch |\n| `goethe-faust/scripts/README.md` | Must be updated |\n\n---\n\n## 2. Output CSV schema\n\n`lookup_dctype_to_class.csv` — one row per (mediatype, sector, dc_type_de) triple:\n\n| Column | Description |\n|---|---|\n| `mediatype` | IRI (e.g. `http://ddb.vocnet.org/medientyp/mt001`) or `any` |\n| `sector` | IRI (e.g. `http://ddb.vocnet.org/sparte/sparte005`) or `any` |\n| `dc_type_de` | German dc:type literal string as it appears in the corpus (the lookup key) |\n| `dc_type_en` | English translation (from old-config `en` field) |\n| `rdf_type_m` | Manifestation-level class IRI (the primary rdf:type to emit alongside mocho:Manifestation) |\n| `rdf_type_e` | Expression-level class IRI (emit if present) |\n| `rdf_type_w` | Work-level class IRI (emit if present) |\n| `rdf_type_i` | Item-level class IRI (emit if present) |\n| `source_vocab` | Which vocabulary the class belongs to: `mo`, `aco`, `vra`, `rico`, `doco`, `fabio`, `mocho` |\n| `notes` | Free-text notes from old-config `remarks` field |\n\n### 2.1 Lookup priority in the transform script\n\n1. Exact match on (mediatype, sector, dc_type_de)\n2. Match on (mediatype, `any`, dc_type_de)\n3. Match on (`any`, `any`, dc_type_de)\n4. No match → no additional rdf:type beyond mocho:Manifestation + htype\n\n---\n\n## 3. Class mapping: old-config → mocho-aligned\n\nThe old-config uses FRBR/FaBiO with 4 positional WEMI slots `[W, E, M, I]`.\nThe new lookup uses named columns and mocho-aligned vocabularies.\n\n### 3.1 Audio (mt001) — from `audio_type2class.json`\n\n| Old classes (M slot) | New `rdf_type_m` |\n|---|---|\n| `mo:Record`, `aco:AudioClip` | `aco:AudioClip` (MO/ACO distinction made at rdf_type_e if needed) |\n| `mo:MusicalManifestation` | `mo:MusicalManifestation` |\n| `fabio:AudioDocument` at E + `aco:AudioClip` at M | `aco:AudioClip` |\n\nKey audio mappings:\n- Musical (Schallplatte, CD, Langspielplatte, Folie, Schellackplate, Schallfolie+music topic): `rdf_type_w = mo:MusicalWork`, `rdf_type_m = mo:MusicalManifestation`, `rdf_type_i = aco:AudioFile`\n- Non-musical audio (Audiofile, Hörfunksendung, Inszenierung, Tondokument non-music, Magnettonband/Vortrag): `rdf_type_e = fabio:AudioDocument`, `rdf_type_m = aco:AudioClip`, `rdf_type_i = aco:AudioFile`\n- Default audio: `rdf_type_m = aco:AudioClip`, `rdf_type_i = aco:AudioFile`\n\n### 3.2 Text (mt003) — from `type2class.json`\n\nSector-dependent:\n\n| Sector | `rdf_type_m` |\n|---|---|\n| sparte001 (Archive) | `rico:Record` (for most text types; htype takes precedence if present) |\n| sparte002 (Library) | FaBiO class at M level → map to mocho equivalent (see §3.2.1) |\n| sparte005 (Museum) | keep as mocho:Manifestation (no further subclass without VRA text class) |\n| `any` | FaBiO class from old-config |\n\n#### 3.2.1 FaBiO → mocho class mapping for library text types\n\n- `fabio:Thesis` / `fabio:DoctoralThesis` / `fabio:MastersThesis` → keep (FaBiO is in mocho imports)\n- `fabio:Textbook` → `fabio:Textbook`\n- `fabio:NewspaperArticle` → `fabio:NewspaperArticle`\n- `fabio:ReportDocument` → `fabio:ReportDocument`\n- `fabio:PrintObject` (generic Manifestation) → omit (redundant with mocho:Manifestation)\n- `doco:Section` etc. → `doco:Section` (DoCO is in mocho)\n\n### 3.3 Photo (mt002) — not in old-config, new mappings needed\n\n| Sector | `rdf_type_m` |\n|---|---|\n| sparte005 (Museum) | `vra:Image` |\n| sparte001 (Archive) | `rico:Record` |\n| `any` | leave as mocho:Manifestation |\n\n### 3.4 Video (mt005) — not in old-config, new mappings needed\n\n| Sector | `rdf_type_m` |\n|---|---|\n| sparte004 (Mediathek) | `aco:MovingImage` (if in mocho) or leave as mocho:Manifestation |\n| `any` | leave as mocho:Manifestation |\n\n### 3.5 Not Digitized (mt007)\n\nNo class dispatch — emit mocho:Manifestation only.\n\n---\n\n## 4. \"aco:AudioClip\"]`) → use first non-empty as primary\n   - Apply `CLASS_MAP` to resolve full IRIs; skip empty strings\n   - Set `mediatype = mt001`, `sector = any` unless entry has `sector` field\n
