```json
  "content": "# Plan: Build `lookup_dctype_to_class.csv` — Type Dispatch for the Transform\n\n## Context\n\n`transform_edm_to_mocho.py` needs to assign domain-specific rdf:type subclasses to\nProvidedCHO entities based on three dimensions: mediatype (mt001–mt007), sector\n(sparte001–007), and the dc:type string value. This dispatch logic existed in\n`old-config/type2class.json` and `old-config/audio_type2class.json` (FRBR/FaBiO\nclasses, 4 WEMI levels), but was never ported to the mocho-aligned pipeline.\n\nThe current `alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv` only covers property alignment (EDM field →\nRDA property via DC→RDA map). It has no type dispatch. The htype lookup\n(`lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv`) covers archival hierarchy types (htype_030–048) but\nnot the full sector × mediatype × dc:type space.\n\nThis plan builds a new lookup file and generator script to fill that gap. The\ntransform script will use it in `retype_entities()` as a second dispatch after\nthe htype lookup.\n\n---\n\n## 0. Overview\n\nOne-sentence framing of each section, task list, and open questions that must be resolved before implementation.\n\n### 0.1 Section chain\n\n1. **§1 Critical files** — lists all inputs, outputs, and files to update; establishes what exists vs. what must be created.\n2. **§2 Output CSV schema** — defines the lookup table structure and three-level fallback lookup priority that the transform script will use.\n3. **§3 Class mapping** — maps old-config FRBR/FaBiO/WEMI positional entries to mocho-aligned class IRIs per mediatype: audio (§3.1, MO/ACO), text (§3.2, RDA/FaBiO/DoCO), photo (§3.3, VRA+LIO), video (§3.4, EBUCore Plus), not-digitized (§3.5).\n4. **§4 Frequency script** — specifies `count_dctype_by_mediatype.py`: prerequisite script that counts dc:type × sector for mt002/mt005 to populate `image_type2class.json` and `video_type2class.json`.\n5. **§5 Generator script** — specifies `gen_dctype_class_mapping.py`: reads old-config JSON files, applies CLASS_MAP, adds manual photo/video rows, writes the CSV.\n6. **§6 Dispatch priority** — defines how the four dimensions (base, htype, dc:type, mediatype×sector) combine: accumulate, dedup; mediatype gates the dispatch table; htype and dc:type are independent layers.\n7. **§7 Sampling script** — specifies `sample_type_dispatch.py` for empirical validation of the dispatch model across all sector × mediatype cells before the transform is finalised.\n8. **§8 TBD items** — lists unresolved IRIs and decisions that block implementation.\n9. **§9 ADR entry** — the D11 decision text to add to `alignment-adr.md`.\n10. **§10 Verification** — end-to-end checks: CSV completeness, TBD resolution, spot-checks, sampling review, README + ADR updates.\n\n### 0.2 Task list\n\n**Phase A — Prerequisites**\n- [ ] A1. Used in `mocho:Work` union alongside `vra:Work`, `rico:Record`, etc. Use `https://w3id.org/lio/v1#Image` for museum photo Manifestation-level type in §3.3. | §3.3, §8 |\n| ✅ | Q3 | EBUCore Plus confirmed in `mocho-full.owl` (prefix `ebucoreplus`, IRI base `http://www.ebu.ch/metadata/ontologies/ebucoreplus#`). Correct `ebucore:` prefix → `ebucoreplus:` throughout. | §3.4, §4, §7 |\n| ❓ | Q4 | What dc:type values appear in the corpus for mt002 (Photo) and mt005 (Video), broken down by sector? **Resolved by**: new script `goethe-faust/scripts/count_dctype_by_mediatype.py` — reads `items-all-goethe-faust.json`, groups by (mediatype, sector, dc:type), outputs frequency table to `output/dctype_frequency_mt002_mt005.csv`. Must be run before writing `image_type2class.json` and `video_type2class.json`. | §3.3, §3.4, §4 |\n| ✅ | Q5 | ACO IRIs confirmed in `mocho-full.owl`: `aco:AudioClip` = `https://w3id.org/ac-ontology/aco#AudioClip` (subclass of `aco:AudioManifestation`); `aco:AudioFile` = `https://w3id.org/ac-ontology/aco#AudioFile` (subclass of `aco:AudioItem`). Also present: `aco:AudioManifestation`, `aco:AudioExpression`, `aco:AudioItem`. Note: `aco:AudioClip` denotes a clip/excerpt — use `aco:AudioManifestation` for general non-musical audio unless dc:type specifically denotes a clip. | §3.1, §7 |\n| ✅ | Q6 | FaBiO has been deprecated by RDA — do not use FaBiO classes as targets. The old-config files are example/reference only; all class mappings must use RDA (or MO/ACO/VRA/EBUCore/RiC-O) in the new lookup. | §3.2, §7 |\n| ✅ | Q7 | `mo:MuscialExpression` (typo in old-config) → corrected to `mo:MusicalExpression` in CLASS_MAP. | §3.1, §5.2 |\n| ✅ | Q8 | mt007 (Not Digitized) — follows the same htype + sector + dc:type dispatch route as other mediatypes. Critical files\n\n| Path | Role |\n|---|---|\n| `goethe-faust/scripts/count_dctype_by_mediatype.py` | **New script** — frequency count of dc:type × sector for mt002/mt005; prerequisite for image/video config files |\n| `goethe-faust/output/dctype_frequency_mt002_mt005.csv` | **New output** — dc:type frequency table by (mediatype, sector) |\n| `goethe-faust/scripts/gen_dctype_class_mapping.py` | **New script** — generates the lookup |\n| `goethe-faust/output/lookup_dctype_to_class.csv` | **New output** — type dispatch table |\n| `goethe-faust/scripts/old-config/type2class.json` | Source: general dc:type → RDA classes |\n| `goethe-faust/scripts/old-config/audio_type2class.json` | Source: audio dc:type → MO/ACO classes |\n| *(new)* `goethe-faust/scripts/old-config/video_type2class.json` | Source: video dc:type → EBUCore Plus classes |\n| *(new)* `goethe-faust/scripts/old-config/image_type2class.json` | Source: image dc:type → VRA Core (`vra:Image`) + LIO classes |\n| `goethe-faust/output/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv` | Reference pattern for lookup CSV schema |\n| `goethe-faust/notes/alignment-adr.md` | Will need a new decision (D11) for this dispatch |\n| `goethe-faust/scripts/README.md` | Must be updated |\n\n---\n\n## 2. Output CSV schema\n\n`lookup_dctype_to_class.csv` — one row per (mediatype, sector, dc_type_de) triple:\n\n| Column | Description |\n|---|---|\n| `mediatype` | IRI (e.g. `http://ddb.vocnet.org/medientyp/mt001`) or `any` |\n| `sector` | IRI (e.g. `http://ddb.vocnet.org/sparte/sparte005`) or `any` |\n| `dc_type_de` | German dc:type literal string as it appears in the corpus (the lookup key) |\n| `dc_type_en` | English translation (from old-config `en` field) |\n| `rdf_type_m` | Manifestation-level class IRI (the primary rdf:type to emit alongside mocho:Manifestation) |\n| `rdf_type_e` | Expression-level class IRI (emit if present) |\n| `rdf_type_w` | Work-level class IRI (emit if present) |\n| `rdf_type_i` | Item-level class IRI (emit if present) |\n| `source_vocab` | Which vocabulary the class belongs to: `mo`, `aco`, `vra`, `rico`, `doco`, `fabio`, `mocho` |\n| `notes` | Free-text notes from old-config `remarks` field |\n\n### 2.1 Lookup priority in the transform script\n\n1. Exact match on (mediatype, sector, dc_type_de)\n2. Match on (mediatype, `any`, dc_type_de)\n3. Match on (`any`, `any`, dc_type_de)\n4. No match → no additional rdf:type beyond mocho:Manifestation + htype\n\n---\n\n## 3. Class mapping: old-config → mocho-aligned\n\nThe old-config uses FRBR/FaBiO with 4 positional WEMI slots `[W, E, M, I]`.\nThe new lookup uses named columns and mocho-aligned vocabularies.\n\n### 3.1 Audio (mt001) — from `audio_type2class.json`\n\n| Old classes (M slot) | New `rdf_type_m` |\n|---|---|\n| `mo:Record`, `aco:AudioClip` | `aco:AudioManifestation` (general); `aco:AudioClip` only for clip/excerpt dc:types |\n| `mo:MusicalManifestation` | `mo:MusicalManifestation` |\n| `fabio:AudioDocument` at E + `aco:AudioClip` at M | `aco:AudioManifestation` |\n\nKey audio mappings:\n- Musical (Schallplatte, CD, Langspielplatte, Folie, Schellackplate, Schallfolie+music topic): `rdf_type_w = mo:MusicalWork`, `rdf_type_m = mo:MusicalManifestation`, `rdf_type_i = aco:AudioFile`\n- Non-musical audio (Audiofile, Hörfunksendung, Inszenierung, Tondokument non-music, Magnettonband/Vortrag): `rdf_type_e = fabio:AudioDocument`, `rdf_type_m = aco:AudioManifestation`, `rdf_type_i = aco:AudioFile`\n- Default audio: `rdf_type_m = aco:AudioManifestation`, `rdf_type_i = aco:AudioFile`\n- Note: `aco:AudioClip` (subclass of `aco:AudioManifestation`) is reserved for dc:type values that denote clips/excerpts specifically.\n\n### 3.2 Text (mt003) — from `type2class.json`\n\nFaBiO is deprecated by RDA. The old-config entries are reference examples only;\ndo not carry FaBiO class IRIs into the new lookup. Text types are typed at the\nManifestation level via RDA classes.\n\nSector-dependent:\n\n| Sector | `rdf_type_m` |\n|---|---|\n| sparte001 (Archive) | `rico:Record` (archival text document; htype takes precedence if present) |\n| sparte002 (Library) | `rdam:C10007` (RDA Manifestation) — equivalent to `mocho:Manifestation`; no additional subclass needed at this pass |\n| sparte005 (Museum) | `mocho:Manifestation` only (no RDA text subclass without VRA equivalent) |\n| `any` | `rdam:C10007` |\n\n#### 3.2.1 Specific bibliographic categories (Thesis, Textbook, etc.)\n\nIn RDA, document categories are expressed via properties, not rdf:type subclasses.\nFor example, a Dissertation is expressed as `rdaw:P10004 has category of work` with\na controlled value — not as `rda:Dissertation` class. Therefore:\n\n- The dc:type string (e.g. `Dissertation`, `Lehrbuch`) is preserved as a literal\n  via property alignment (dc:type → RDA property), **not** promoted to rdf:type.\n- No `rdf_type_m` entry is emitted for specific bibliographic genres in the\n  Text+Library sector from this lookup table.\n- DoCO structural classes (`doco:Section`, `doco:Chapter`, etc.) continue to be\n  emitted via the **htype dispatch** (lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv), not here.\n\n**Future work**: Create a controlled vocabulary for DDB dc:type string values and\nalign it to an existing KB (e.g. GND Sachbegriff, RDA value vocabularies, or\nWikidata). This would allow dc:type literals to be resolved to LOD concept URIs,\nenabling richer faceting and cross-dataset type alignment beyond the current\nliteral-string lookup.\n\n### 3.3 Photo (mt002) — from `image_type2class.json` (new)\n\ndc:type dispatch → VRA Core + LIO classes:\n- VRA confirmed in mocho-full.owl (IRI base `http://purl.org/vra/`)\n- LIO confirmed in mocho-edit.owl: class `https://w3id.org/lio/v1#Image`\n\nSector refinement:\n- sparte005 (Museum): `http://purl.org/vra/Image` + `https://w3id.org/lio/v1#Image`\n- sparte001 (Archive): `http://www.ica.org/standards/RiC/ontology#Record` (archival photograph)\n- `any`: `http://purl.org/vra/Image` as default\n\ndc:type values and sub-mappings: populated after Q4 frequency count (task A1).\n\n### 3.4 Video (mt005) — from `video_type2class.json` (new)\n\ndc:type dispatch → EBUCore Plus classes (confirmed in mocho-full.owl; prefix `ebucoreplus`,\nIRI base `http://www.ebu.ch/metadata/ontologies/ebucoreplus#`).\n\n- Manifestation-level target: `ebucoreplus:MediaResource` (default) or `ebucoreplus:EditorialWork`\n  for editorial/programme content, per dc:type value\n- Sector refinement:\n  - sparte004 (Mediathek): `ebucoreplus:EditorialWork` for programme types; `ebucoreplus:MediaResource` otherwise\n  - `any`: `ebucoreplus:MediaResource` as default\n- dc:type values and sub-mappings: populated after Q4 frequency count (task A1).\n\n### 3.5 Not Digitized (mt007)\n\nFollows the standard dispatch route: htype → sector → dc:type. The absence of\na digital surrogate does not exempt an object from type classification.\n`mocho:Manifestation` is always emitted as the base type; additional classes\nfollow from htype, sector, and dc:type values as for all other mediatypes.\n\n---\n\n## 4. Script: `count_dctype_by_mediatype.py` (prerequisite)\n\nPrerequisite script that must be run before creating `image_type2class.json` and\n`video_type2class.json`. Answers Q4.\n\n```\nPurpose:    Frequency count of dc:type values in the corpus for mt002 (Photo) and\n            mt005 (Video), broken down by sector.\nUsage:      python count_dctype_by_mediatype.py\nInputs:     data/items-all-goethe-faust.json (streaming)\nOutputs:    output/dctype_frequency_mt002_mt005.csv\n            Columns: mediatype, sector, dc_type_de, count\nDeps:       stdlib only (json, csv, pathlib, collections)\n```\n\nImplementation:\n1. Stream `items-all-goethe-faust.json`; for each record extract `mediatype`, `sector`, and all `dc:type` values.\n2. Filter to mt002 and mt005 records only.\n3. Count occurrences of each (mediatype, sector, dc_type_de) triple using `collections.Counter`.\n4. Write sorted CSV (descending by count within each mediatype × sector cell).\n5. Print summary: total records per mediatype, unique dc:type values per cell.\n\n---\n\n## 5. Script: `gen_dctype_class_mapping.py`\n\n### 5.1 Header\n\n```\nPurpose:    Generate lookup_dctype_to_class.csv — type dispatch table mapping\n            (mediatype, sector, dc_type_de) to mocho-aligned rdf:type class IRIs.\n            Used by transform_edm_to_mocho.py for domain-specific rdf:type dispatch.\nUsage:      python gen_dctype_class_mapping.py\nInputs:     scripts/old-config/type2class.json\n            scripts/old-config/audio_type2class.json\n            scripts/old-config/image_type2class.json\n            scripts/old-config/video_type2class.json\nOutputs:    output/lookup_dctype_to_class.csv\nDeps:       stdlib only (json, csv, pathlib)\nAssumes:    old-config files are read-only reference; class mapping to mocho-aligned\n            equivalents is encoded in CLASS_MAP dict in this script.\n```\n\n### 5.2 Implementation steps\n\n1. **Load source files** — `type2class.json` (general), `audio_type2class.json` (audio),\n   `image_type2class.json` (photo), `video_type2class.json` (video).\n\n2. **Define `CLASS_MAP`** — dict mapping old FRBR/FaBiO class strings → new mocho-aligned IRIs:\n   ```python\n   CLASS_MAP = {\n       \"mo:MusicalWork\":           \"http://purl.org/ontology/mo/MusicalWork\",\n       \"mo:MusicalManifestation\":  \"http://purl.org/ontology/mo/MusicalManifestation\",\n       \"mo:MusicalExpression\":     \"http://purl.org/ontology/mo/MusicalExpression\",  # corrected typo\n       \"mo:Record\":                \"http://purl.org/ontology/mo/Record\",\n       \"aco:AudioManifestation\":   \"https://w3id.org/ac-ontology/aco#AudioManifestation\",\n       \"aco:AudioClip\":            \"https://w3id.org/ac-ontology/aco#AudioClip\",\n       \"aco:AudioExpression\":      \"https://w3id.org/ac-ontology/aco#AudioExpression\",\n       \"aco:AudioItem\":            \"https://w3id.org/ac-ontology/aco#AudioItem\",\n       \"aco:AudioFile\":            \"https://w3id.org/ac-ontology/aco#AudioFile\",\n       \"vra:Image\":                \"http://purl.org/vra/Image\",\n       \"vra:Work\":                 \"http://purl.org/vra/Work\",\n       \"lio:Image\":                \"https://w3id.org/lio/v1#Image\",\n       \"ebucoreplus:MediaResource\":   \"http://www.ebu.ch/metadata/ontologies/ebucoreplus#MediaResource\",\n       \"ebucoreplus:EditorialWork\":   \"http://www.ebu.ch/metadata/ontologies/ebucoreplus#EditorialWork\",\n       \"rico:Record\":              \"http://www.ica.org/standards/RiC/ontology#Record\",\n       \"doco:Section\":             \"http://purl.org/spar/doco/Section\",\n       # Audio document (expression-level; kept as valid LOD IRI even though FaBiO deprecated)\n       \"fabio:AudioDocument\":      \"http://purl.org/spar/fabio/AudioDocument\",\n       # Generic FaBiO carriers (redundant with mocho:Manifestation) → omit\n       \"fabio:PrintObject\":        \"\",\n       \"fabio:AnalogItem\":         \"\",\n       \"fabio:DigitalItem\":        \"\",\n       \"frbr:Work\":                \"\",\n       \"frbr:Manifestation\":       \"\",\n       \"frbr:Item\":                \"\",\n   }\n   ```\n\n3. **Process audio entries** (`audio_type2class.json`):\n   - For each key (dc_type_de): read `class` array (positional: [W, E, M, I])\n   - Flatten lists at position M — use first non-empty as primary; apply CLASS_MAP\n   - Set `mediatype = http://ddb.vocnet.org/medientyp/mt001`, `sector = any` unless entry has `sector` field\n   - Emit one row per dc_type_de\n\n4. **Process general entries** (`type2class.json`):\n   - Same positional class array processing\n   - `mediatype = any`; `sector = any` unless entry has `sector` field\n\n5. **Process photo entries** (`image_type2class.json`):\n   - `mediatype = http://ddb.vocnet.org/medientyp/mt002`\n   - Apply sector and CLASS_MAP per entry\n\n6. **Process video entries** (`video_type2class.json`):\n   - `mediatype = http://ddb.vocnet.org/medientyp/mt005`\n   - Apply sector and CLASS_MAP per entry\n\n7. **Write CSV** — sort by (mediatype, sector, dc_type_de).\n\n8. **Print summary**: total rows, rows with rdf_type_m populated, rows with TBD IRIs.\n\n---\n\n## 6. Dispatch priority and combination rules\n\nFour dimensions can contribute rdf:type triples for a ProvidedCHO. Their roles\nare complementary, not competing:\n\n| Dimension | Source | Output | When |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| `htype` | `lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv` | DoCO class (library) or RiC-O class (archival) | htype_001–029 → DoCO; htype_030–048 → RiC-O |\n| `mediatype` × `sector` × `dc:type` | `lookup_dctype_to_class.csv` | Domain class (ACO/MO/VRA/LIO/EBUCore/RiC-O) | Any ProvidedCHO with a dc:type value |\n| base | hardcoded | `mocho:Manifestation` | Always |\n\n**Combination rule**: Accumulate — both htype and dc:type dispatch can fire\nfor the same entity. Identical rdf:type IRIs are deduped. Both layers are\nindependent: an archival letter can be both `rico:Record` (from htype_034) AND\na more specific genre class (from dc:type `Brief`). Neither blocks the other.\n\n### 6.1 Separation by htype domain\n\n- htype_030–048 (archival): htype dispatch produces RiC-O classes; dc:type dispatch\n  may also produce a more specific document genre class. Both emitted.\n- htype_001–029 (library/DoCO): htype dispatch produces DoCO structural classes\n  (Chapter, Section, etc.); dc:type dispatch may produce a publication type class.\n  Both emitted if present.\n- No htype: only dc:type dispatch contributes a specific class (beyond mocho:Manifestation).\n\n### 6.2 mediatype gates the dispatch table\n\n- `mt001` (Audio): rows from `audio_type2class.json` → ACO/MO classes\n- `mt002` (Photo): rows from `image_type2class.json` → VRA/LIO/RiC-O classes\n- `mt003` (Text): rows from `type2class.json` → RDA/RiC-O classes\n- `mt005` (Video): rows from `video_type2class.json` → EBUCore Plus classes\n- `mt007` (Not Digitized): follows standard dispatch — htype + sector + dc:type\n\n### 6.3 Priority order for the transform loop (implemented in `retype_entities()`)\n\n1. Always emit `mocho:Manifestation`\n2. Apply htype dispatch (existing `lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv`)\n3. Apply dc:type dispatch (new `lookup_dctype_to_class.csv`), using mediatype to gate rows\n4. Dedup: collect all rdf:type IRIs in a set, emit each once\n\n**Validation before finalising**: Run `sample_type_dispatch.py` on 50 random records\nper sector × mediatype cell. Manual review determines if the accumulate model is\ncorrect or if priority overrides are needed for specific cases.\n\n---\n\n## 7. Sampling script: `sample_type_dispatch.py`\n\nA lightweight diagnostic script (not part of the transform pipeline) that:\n\n1. Reads `items-all-goethe-faust.json` (streaming, random sample)\n2. For each sampled record, shows:\n   - object_id, sector IRI, mediatype IRI\n   - dc:type value(s)\n   - htype code\n   - rdf:type classes emitted by htype dispatch (from lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv)\n   - rdf:type classes emitted by dc:type dispatch (from lookup_dctype_to_class.csv)\n   - combined rdf:type set (deduped)\n3. Samples at least 5 records per populated (sector × mediatype) cell\n4. Output: `output/type_dispatch_sample.csv` + printed summary\n\nThis is a prerequisite for finalising `retype_entities()` in the transform script.\n\n---\n\n## 8. TBD items\n\n- **VRA Core IRIs**: ✅ confirmed in `mocho-full.owl`. IRI base `http://purl.org/vra/`; use `http://purl.org/vra/Image` for photo Manifestation-level type.\n- **LIO**: ✅ confirmed in `mocho-edit.owl`; class `https://w3id.org/lio/v1#Image`. Used in `mocho:Work` union. Apply to sparte005 museum photo rows in §3.3.\n- **ACO IRIs**: ✅ confirmed in `mocho-full.owl`. Use `aco:AudioManifestation` for general non-musical audio; `aco:AudioClip` reserved for clip/excerpt dc:types.\n- **EBUCore Plus**: ✅ confirmed in `mocho-full.owl` as `ebucoreplus` (IRI base `http://www.ebu.ch/metadata/ontologies/ebucoreplus#`). Use `ebucoreplus:MediaResource` as default video type; `ebucoreplus:EditorialWork` for programme content.\n- **`mo:MuscialExpression` typo**: ✅ corrected to `mo:MusicalExpression` in CLASS_MAP.\n- **FaBiO**: ✅ deprecated by RDA — do not emit as rdf:type targets. Old-config entries are reference only. Exception: `fabio:AudioDocument` retained as a valid LOD IRI for expression-level audio.\n- **dc:type frequency count (Q4)**: run `count_dctype_by_mediatype.py` on corpus for mt002/mt005 before creating `image_type2class.json` and `video_type2class.json` (task A1).\n- **Not Digitized (mt007)**: ✅ follows standard htype + sector + dc:type dispatch.\n\n---\n\n## 9. ADR entry (D11, to be added to `alignment-adr.md`)\n\n**Decision**: Type dispatch beyond mocho:Manifestation is driven by a lookup table\n`lookup_dctype_to_class.csv` keyed by (mediatype, sector, dc_type_de), separate from\nthe property alignment table. The htype lookup (`lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv`) remains\nthe primary dispatch for archival hierarchy types; the dc:type dispatch supplements it\nfor non-archival and cross-sector cases. Both layers accumulate independently; identical\nrdf:type IRIs are deduped at emit time.\n\n---\n\n## 10. Verification\n\n1. Run `count_dctype_by_mediatype.py`; review `output/dctype_frequency_mt002_mt005.csv` to populate image/video config files.\n2. Run `gen_dctype_class_mapping.py`; check summary: row count, TBD count = 0.\n3. Grep output CSV for `TBD` — must be empty before transform is written.\n4. Cross-check audio rows: every entry in `audio_type2class.json` with a non-empty M-slot class should appear in the CSV.\n5. Spot-check: `Schallplatte` → `mo:MusicalManifestation` at rdf_type_m; `Audiofile` → `aco:AudioManifestation` at rdf_type_m.\n6. Run `sample_type_dispatch.py`; review `output/type_dispatch_sample.csv` for dispatch correctness.\n7. Update `goethe-faust/scripts/README.md` with new script entries.\n8. Add D11 to `goethe-faust/notes/alignment-adr.md`.\n"
```
```json
}
```

### Assistant *(2026-04-15T19:08:22.812Z)*

**Tool call:** `Write`
```json
{
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/goethe-faust/scripts/count_dctype_by_mediatype.py",
  "content": "# Purpose:  Frequency count of dc:type values in the corpus across all mediatypes,\n#           broken down by sector. Prerequisite for populating image_type2class.json\n#           and video_type2class.json before running gen_dctype_class_mapping.py.\n# Usage:    python count_dctype_by_mediatype.py\n# Inputs:   data/items/*.json  (individual DDB item JSON files)\n# Outputs:  output/dctype_frequency_all.csv\n#           Columns: mediatype, sector, dc_type_de, count\n# Deps:     stdlib only (json, csv, pathlib, collections)\n\nimport csv\nimport json\nfrom collections import Counter\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent\nITEMS_DIR = ROOT / \"data\" / \"items\"\nOUTPUT = ROOT / \"output\" / \"dctype_frequency_all.csv\"\n\n\ndef extract_concepts(edm_rdf):\n    \"\"\"Return (mediatype_iri, sector_iri) from edm.RDF.Concept list.\"\"\"\n    mediatype = None\n    sector = None\n    concepts = edm_rdf.get(\"Concept\") or []\n    if isinstance(concepts, dict):\n        concepts = [concepts]\n    for c in concepts:\n        about = c.get(\"about\", \"\")\n        if \"medientyp\" in about:\n            mediatype = about\n        elif \"sparte\" in about:\n            sector = about\n    return mediatype, sector\n\n\ndef extract_dctype(provided_cho):\n    \"\"\"Return list of dc:type German literal strings from ProvidedCHO.dcType.\"\"\"\n    dc = provided_cho.get(\"dcType\")\n    if dc is None:\n        return []\n    if isinstance(dc, list):\n        return [v.get(\"$\", \"\").strip() for v in dc if v.get(\"$\", \"\").strip()]\n    if isinstance(dc, dict):\n        val = dc.get(\"$\", \"\").strip()\n        return [val] if val else []\n    return []\n\n\ndef main():\n    counts: Counter = Counter()\n    total_files = 0\n    skipped = 0\n\n    for path in sorted(ITEMS_DIR.glob(\"*.json\")):\n        total_files += 1\n        try:\n            data = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f\"  SKIP {path.name}: {e}\")\n            skipped += 1\n            continue\n\n        edm_rdf = data.get(\"edm\", {}).get(\"RDF\", {})\n        provided_cho = edm_rdf.get(\"ProvidedCHO\", {})\n\n        mediatype, sector = extract_concepts(edm_rdf)\n        dc_types = extract_dctype(provided_cho)\n\n        if not dc_types:\n            counts[(mediatype or \"none\", sector or \"none\", \"\")] += 1\n            continue\n\n        for dc_type in dc_types:\n            counts[(mediatype or \"none\", sector or \"none\", dc_type)] += 1\n\n    # Write CSV sorted by mediatype, sector, then descending count\n    rows = sorted(counts.items(), key=lambda x: (x[0][0], x[0][1], -x[1]))\n\n    OUTPUT.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    with OUTPUT.open(\"w\", newline=\"\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n        writer = csv.writer(f)\n        writer.writerow([\"mediatype\", \"sector\", \"dc_type_de\", \"count\"])\n        for (mediatype, sector, dc_type), count in rows:\n            writer.writerow([mediatype, sector, dc_type, count])\n\n    # Summary\n    print(f\"Files read:   {total_files - skipped} / {total_files}\")\n    print(f\"Output rows:  {len(rows)}\")\n    print(f\"Output:       {OUTPUT}\")\n    print()\n\n    # Per-mediatype breakdown\n    mt_totals: dict = {}\n    mt_types: dict = {}\n    for (mt, sec, dc), cnt in counts.items():\n        mt_totals[mt] = mt_totals.get(mt, 0) + cnt\n        if dc:\n            mt_types.setdefault(mt, set()).add(dc)\n\n    print(f\"{'Mediatype':<55} {'records':>8}  {'unique dc:types':>16}\")\n    print(\"-\" * 82)\n    for mt in sorted(mt_totals):\n        label = mt.rsplit(\"/\", 1)[-1] if mt != \"none\" else \"none\"\n        n_types = len(mt_types.get(mt, set()))\n        print(f\"  {label:<53} {mt_totals[mt]:>8}  {n_types:>16}\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\n"
}
```

### Assistant *(2026-04-15T19:30:00.992Z)*

**Tool call:** `Write`
```json
{
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/goethe-faust/scripts/gen_video_type2class.py",
  "content": "# Purpose:  Generate old-config/video_type2class.json from the dc:type frequency\n#           table for mt005 (Video). Class assignments use EBUCore Plus vocabulary\n#           (confirmed in mocho-full.owl). Format mirrors audio_type2class.json:\n#           positional class array [W, E, M, I]; CLASS_MAP in gen_dctype_class_mapping.py\n#           resolves prefixed names to full IRIs.\n# Usage:    python gen_video_type2class.py\n# Inputs:   output/dctype_frequency_all.csv\n# Outputs:  scripts/old-config/video_type2class.json\n# Deps:     stdlib only (csv, json, pathlib)\n# Assumes:  EBUCore Plus IRI base: http://www.ebu.ch/metadata/ontologies/ebucoreplus#\n#           ebucoreplus:EditorialWork  — editorial/programme content (film, interview, etc.)\n#           ebucoreplus:MediaResource  — generic media carrier (clip, trailer, fragment)\n\nimport csv\nimport json\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent\nFREQ_CSV = ROOT / \"output\" / \"dctype_frequency_all.csv\"\nOUTPUT = ROOT / \"scripts\" / \"old-config\" / \"video_type2class.json\"\n\n# dc:type values that map to EditorialWork (programme/editorial content)\nEDITORIAL_WORK_TYPES = {\n    \"Dokumentarfilm\",\n    \"Interview\",\n    \"Inszenierung\",\n    \"Veranstaltungsmitschnitt\",\n    \"Videofilm\",\n}\n\n# English translations\nEN_LABELS = {\n    \"Dokumentarfilm\":                   \"Documentary film\",\n    \"Filmanfang\":                       \"Film opening\",\n    \"Inszenierung\":                     \"Stage production (video recording)\",\n    \"Interview\":                        \"Interview\",\n    \"Teaser\":                           \"Teaser\",\n    \"Tonbild\":                          \"Sound film / slide show\",\n    \"Trailer\":                          \"Trailer\",\n    \"Veranstaltungsmitschnitt\":         \"Event recording\",\n    \"Videofilm\":                        \"Video film\",\n    \"zweidimensionales bewegtes Bild\":  \"Two-dimensional moving image\",\n}\n\nREMARKS = {\n    \"Dokumentarfilm\":                   \"Documentary; editorial content.\",\n    \"Filmanfang\":                       \"Fragment — opening sequence of a film.\",\n    \"Inszenierung\":                     \"Video recording of a theatrical or musical production.\",\n    \"Interview\":                        \"Recorded interview; archival sector.\",\n    \"Teaser\":                           \"Short promotional clip.\",\n    \"Tonbild\":                          \"Historical audiovisual format (slide show with audio).\",\n    \"Trailer\":                          \"Promotional trailer; museum sector.\",\n    \"Veranstaltungsmitschnitt\":         \"Recording of a live event.\",\n    \"Videofilm\":                        \"General video film.\",\n    \"zweidimensionales bewegtes Bild\":  \"Generic moving image; library sector.\",\n}\n\n\ndef main():\n    # Collect mt005 rows grouped by dc_type\n    seen = {}\n    with FREQ_CSV.open(encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n        for row in csv.DictReader(f):\n            if \"mt005\" not in row[\"mediatype\"]:\n                continue\n            dc = row[\"dc_type_de\"].strip()\n            if not dc:\n                continue\n            seen.setdefault(dc, {\"count\": 0, \"sectors\": set()})\n            seen[dc][\"count\"] += int(row[\"count\"])\n            seen[dc][\"sectors\"].add(row[\"sector\"].rsplit(\"/\", 1)[-1])\n\n    result = {}\n    for dc in sorted(seen, key=lambda x: -seen[x][\"count\"]):\n        rdf_type_m = (\n            \"ebucoreplus:EditorialWork\"\n            if dc in EDITORIAL_WORK_TYPES\n            else \"ebucoreplus:MediaResource\"\n        )\n        result[dc] = {\n            \"remarks\": REMARKS.get(dc, \"\"),\n            \"en\": EN_LABELS.get(dc, \"\"),\n            \"sectors\": sorted(seen[dc][\"sectors\"]),\n            \"count\": seen[dc][\"count\"],\n            \"class\": [\n                \"\",               # W — Work level (not mapped for video)\n                \"\",               # E — Expression level\n                rdf_type_m,       # M — Manifestation level (primary)\n                \"\",               # I — Item level\n            ],\n        }\n\n    OUTPUT.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    OUTPUT.write_text(json.dumps(result, ensure_ascii=False, indent=4), encoding=\"utf-8\")\n\n    print(f\"Wrote {len(result)} entries → {OUTPUT}\")\n    print()\n    print(f\"{'dc:type':<45} {'M class':<30} {'count':>6}  sectors\")\n    print(\"-\" * 100)\n    for dc, entry in result.items():\n        secs = \", \".join(entry[\"sectors\"])\n        print(f\"  {dc:<43} {entry['class'][2]:<30} {entry['count']:>6}  {secs}\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\n"
}
```

```json
{
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/goethe-faust/notes/inputs.md",
  "content": "# Inputs\n\n## data/items/*.json\n\n- **Path**: `goethe-faust/data/items/`\n- **Format**: JSON, one file per DDB item (115,437 files; 4 empty/corrupt)\n- **Provenance**: DDB EDM export (Goethe-Faust corpus)\n- **Key fields**:\n  - `edm.RDF.ProvidedCHO.dcType` — dc:type literal (`{resource, lang, $}` or list)\n  - `edm.RDF.Concept[].about` — mediatype IRI (`medientyp/mt001–mt007`) and sector IRI (`sparte/sparte001–007`)\n  - `edm.RDF.ProvidedCHO.hierarchyType` — htype code (e.g. `htype_001`)\n  - `provider-info.domains[0]` — sector IRI (redundant with Concept)\n- **Encoding**: UTF-8\n\n## output/dctype_frequency_all.csv\n\n- **Path**: `goethe-faust/output/dctype_frequency_all.csv`\n- **Format**: CSV, columns: `mediatype, sector, dc_type_de, count`\n- **Provenance**: Generated by `scripts/count_dctype_by_mediatype.py` from `data/items/*.json`\n- **Size**: 1,386 rows\n- **Volume by mediatype**:\n  - mt001 (Audio): 476 records, 8 unique dc:types\n  - mt002 (Photo): 20,229 records, 695 unique dc:types\n  - mt003 (Text): 52,247 records, 169 unique dc:types\n  - mt005 (Video): 96 records, 10 unique dc:types\n  - mt007 (Not Digitized): 42,360 records, 310 unique dc:types\n\n## scripts/old-config/audio_type2class.json\n\n- **Path**: `goethe-faust/scripts/old-config/audio_type2class.json`\n- **Format**: JSON, keyed by German dc:type string\n- **Provenance**: Legacy config from old pipeline (read-only reference)\n- **Schema**: `{dc_type_de: {remarks, en, subj, dnb, class: [W, E, M, I]}}`\n- **Class vocab**: MO, ACO, FaBiO (FRBR/FaBiO positional; deprecated — see CLASS_MAP in gen_dctype_class_mapping.py)\n\n## scripts/old-config/type2class.json\n\n- **Path**: `goethe-faust/scripts/old-config/type2class.json`\n- **Format**: JSON, keyed by German dc:type string\n- **Provenance**: Legacy config from old pipeline (read-only reference)\n- **Schema**: same as audio_type2class.json\n- **Note**: Covers general (non-audio) dc:types; FaBiO/FRBR classes — deprecated\n\n## scripts/old-config/video_type2class.json\n\n- **Path**: `goethe-faust/scripts/old-config/video_type2class.json`\n- **Format**: JSON, keyed by German dc:type string\n- **Provenance**: Generated by `scripts/gen_video_type2class.py` from frequency data\n- **Schema**: `{dc_type_de: {remarks, en, sectors, count, class: [W, E, M, I]}}`\n- **Class vocab**: EBUCore Plus (`ebucoreplus:EditorialWork`, `ebucoreplus:MediaResource`)\n- **Entries**: 10 dc:types (all mt005 values in corpus)\n\n## scripts/old-config/image_type2class.json\n\n- **Path**: `goethe-faust/scripts/old-config/image_type2class.json`\n- **Format**: JSON, keyed by German dc:type string\n- **Provenance**: To be generated by `scripts/gen_image_type2class.py` from frequency data\n- **Schema**: same as video_type2class.json\n- **Class vocab**: VRA (`vra:Image`), LIO (`lio:Image`), RiC-O (`rico:Record`)\n- **Entries**: mt002 has 695 unique dc:types — requires clustering before mapping\n"
}
```

**Tool call:** `Write`
```json
{
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/goethe-faust/scripts/gen_image_type2class.py",
  "content": "# Purpose:  Generate output/config/image_type2class.json from the dc:type frequency\n#           table for mt002 (Photo/Image). Class assignments use VRA Core and LIO\n#           vocabulary (confirmed in mocho-full.owl and mocho-edit.owl).\n#           With 695 unique dc:types, a rule-based sector-first strategy is applied:\n#             - sparte001 (Archive)  → rico:Record  (archival photograph)\n#             - sparte005 (Museum)   → vra:Image + lio:Image\n#             - all others / any     → vra:Image (default)\n#           dc:type literals are preserved as lookup keys; no per-type class override\n#           is applied at this pass — sector alone drives the class assignment.\n#           Manual review of output/config/image_type2class.json can add per-type\n#           overrides in a follow-up pass.\n# Usage:    python gen_image_type2class.py [--summary]\n#           --summary  print sector totals and top-30 dc:types then exit\n# Inputs:   output/dctype_frequency_all.csv\n# Outputs:  output/config/image_type2class.json\n# Deps:     stdlib only (csv, json, pathlib, collections, argparse)\n\nimport argparse\nimport csv\nimport json\nfrom collections import defaultdict\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent\nFREQ_CSV = ROOT / \"output\" / \"dctype_frequency_all.csv\"\nOUTPUT = ROOT / \"output\" / \"config\" / \"image_type2class.json\"\n\n# Sector IRI suffix → Manifestation-level class(es)\nSECTOR_CLASS = {\n    \"sparte001\": [\"rico:Record\"],                  # Archive → archival photograph\n    \"sparte005\": [\"vra:Image\", \"lio:Image\"],        # Museum → VRA + LIO\n}\nDEFAULT_CLASS = [\"vra:Image\"]\n\n\ndef load_mt002_rows():\n    rows = []\n    with FREQ_CSV.open(encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n        for r in csv.DictReader(f):\n            if \"mt002\" in r[\"mediatype\"]:\n                rows.append(r)\n    return rows\n\n\ndef print_summary(rows):\n    by_sec = defaultdict(int)\n    totals = defaultdict(int)\n    for r in rows:\n        sec = r[\"sector\"].rsplit(\"/\", 1)[-1]\n        by_sec[sec] += int(r[\"count\"])\n        totals[r[\"dc_type_de\"]] += int(r[\"count\"])\n\n    print(\"=== mt002 sector totals ===\")\n    for s, n in sorted(by_sec.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):\n        print(f\"  {s:<14} {n:>7}\")\n\n    print(f\"\\n=== Top 30 dc:types (all sectors), {len(totals)} unique total ===\")\n    for dc, n in sorted(totals.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1])[:30]:\n        print(f\"  {dc:<50} {n:>7}\")\n\n\ndef main():\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()\n    parser.add_argument(\"--summary\", action=\"store_true\")\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n\n    rows = load_mt002_rows()\n\n    if args.summary:\n        print_summary(rows)\n        return\n\n    # Group by (dc_type_de, sector)\n    groups: dict = {}\n    for r in rows:\n        dc = r[\"dc_type_de\"].strip()\n        sec = r[\"sector\"].rsplit(\"/\", 1)[-1]\n        key = (dc, sec)\n        groups.setdefault(key, {\"count\": 0})\n        groups[key][\"count\"] += int(r[\"count\"])\n\n    # Build result: one entry per (dc_type, sector) with sector-driven class\n    result = {}\n    for (dc, sec), info in sorted(groups.items(), key=lambda x: (-x[1][\"count\"], x[0][0])):\n        entry_key = f\"{dc}__{sec}\" if sec != \"none\" else dc\n        classes = SECTOR_CLASS.get(sec, DEFAULT_CLASS)\n        result[entry_key] = {\n            \"dc_type_de\": dc,\n            \"sector\": f\"http://ddb.vocnet.org/sparte/{sec}\" if sec != \"none\" else \"any\",\n            \"count\": info[\"count\"],\n            \"class\": [\n                \"\",           # W\n                \"\",           # E\n                classes,      # M — list; gen_dctype_class_mapping.py uses first as primary\n                \"\",           # I\n            ],\n        }\n\n    OUTPUT.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    OUTPUT.write_text(json.dumps(result, ensure_ascii=False, indent=4), encoding=\"utf-8\")\n\n    print(f\"Wrote {len(result)} entries → {OUTPUT}\")\n    print()\n    # Class distribution summary\n    class_counts: dict = defaultdict(int)\n    for entry in result.values():\n        m = entry[\"class\"][2]\n        label = \"+\".join(m) if isinstance(m, list) else m\n        class_counts[label] += 1\n    print(\"Class distribution (M slot):\")\n    for cls, n in sorted(class_counts.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):\n        print(f\"  {cls:<40} {n:>5} entries\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\n"
}
```
